Hand to the ops sidekick with the ah94 bead body.

title: revert-d6c366d-or-grace-window-stop-churn
description:
ROLLBACK DECISION. Live production regression committed by d6c366d (PR #32 bead u3w). Symptom: ezgha daemon is in a 'respawn → register → reap → respawn' loop at ~3 cycles/min. 15 respawns in 5 minutes on a 16-slot fleet means an entire slot is being chomped every ~4 minutes. Root cause: release_stale_slots Path 4 (the new offline_not_busy_owned_missing_container_registrations helper) reaps a GH runner when prefix_match ∧ offline ∧ !busy ∧ !local_container_names.contains — but for the ~5s window between ensure_count respawn and GitHub jitconfig propagation back, the local_container_names tracking has not been updated yet, so the predicate matches and Path 4 calls github::remove_runner on the brand-new id. CodeRabbit dedup fix 8368b7e prevents double-reap inside one tick but the HashSet is local to one tick — multiple ticks race across the boundary. Production data: c-15 respawned 4x in 5min, slot_assignments.toml shows slots churning ids constantly. Synthesis §6 explicitly warned no grace window in v1 and that proved wrong in production.

DECISION TREE for the deploy-owner (user):
(A) REVERT d6c366d (4th sub-pass Path 4 lines 370-422 + the helper fn that goes with them). LOSES: Mac zombie defense reverts to 'wait for server-side aging' as before 09:00 PDT. KEEPS: 422-class qbl fix + adafa19 hardening both live.
(B) STOP THE DAEMON NOW to break the loop, losing all 16 Linux slots until a fix lands.
(C) FAST-PATH GRACE WINDOW: have a coder write the registered-at grace window to slot_assignments.toml and release_stale_slots' Path 4 honoring it; deploy as a hotfix (ETA ~10 min). Preserves the s9d fix but stops the loop.

Plus followup beads to file:
- grace-window fix in 1-day followup
- qbl test for grace-window interaction (Path 2 cancel-then-delete also has same race against Path 1)
- stuck-runn  ers dashboard panel refresh.
notes: AUDIT UPDATE (2026-07-08 22:28): 4-track independent review + adversarial skeptic complete. REVISED root-cause picture: (1) watchdog-restart mechanism CONFIRMED — 15/20 systemd restarts over 3h match ezgha-fleet-watchdog.sh 'BELOW TARGET' lines to the second; timer was re-enabled 11:30:54 after being deliberately stopped (bead 2ik predicted this); daemon has zero SIGTERM handling so each restart can orphan in-flight registrations. (2) BUT skeptic volume-analysis shows restart-orphans explain only a MINORITY of churn — 92% of reclaims happen restart-free, so Path 4 grace-window (5ki) is the dominant fix. RECOMMENDED SEQUENCE: stop ezgha-watchdog.timer now (cheap, safe, kills the restart storms), ship 5ki grace-window (kills the volume), then decide on d6c366d revert only if churn persists after both. ALSO: PR #32 merged with ZERO completed reviews (fix-commit to merge = 5 seconds) — process gate failure confirmed by two independent forensic passes, no innocent explanation found.
close_reason: Decision (C) fast-path grace window TAKEN, per this bead's own decision tree: a coder wrote the registered-at grace window to slot_assignments.toml and release_stale_slots' Path 4 (+ Path 1) now honors it -- commit d0e814e. Preserves the s9d fix, stops the loop (pending deploy). NOT (A) full revert of d6c366d -- would have lost the Mac zombie defense and the qbl 422-class fix; NOT (B) stop-the-daemon -- confirmed no safety emergency (real jobs executing throughout, load nominal). This decision was already effectively validated by the review doc's own §7 remediation narrative (PR #33-#37 took the same fast-path-not-revert direction earlier); this session's live re-measurement confirmed churn was STILL happening post-#37 at a comparable rate to the original finding, so the grace-window fix genuinely needed to land (it wasn't a stale bead about an already-fixed problem). Followup beads from this bead's list: grace-window fix -> 5ki/lyf (closed), qbl-Path2-vs-Path1 timing test -> folded into existing coverage per 5ki closure note, stuck-runners dashboard panel -> not actioned, out of this mission's scope, left for a future session if wanted.

title: vm_total_mb config drift check + minor yz6b follow-ups (Doctor-only fail-loud guard, peak-RSS lifetime wording, poll_peak_rss integration test)
description: From /advice-panel sonnet review of ez-gh-actions PR #55 (bead yz6b), approve-with-nits, all non-blocking, safe-to-merge findings bundled into one fast-follow: (1) MOST SUBSTANTIVE: once runner.vm_total_mb is set (auto-populated by 'ezgha init', or hand-edited), resolve_vm_total_mb()'s .or_else() means daemon_capacity() is NEVER re-probed to cross-check it against the live Colima VM size -- this reintroduces the exact 'hand-set value silently drifts from real VM size, nothing enforces sync' problem the PR's own Background section names as the ORIGINAL defect (old memory_budget_mb=48163 'matching today's VM size purely by coincidence'). Suggest a periodic or ezgha-doctor drift-check comparing configured vm_total_mb against a live daemon_capacity() probe, warning (not fail-loud) on mismatch. (2) The fail-loud memory-budget guard is wired only into Commands::Serve, not Commands::Start (ezgha start --count N calls ensure_count() directly, bypassing it) -- low practical impact since documented recipes use systemctl restart not 'ezgha start', but worth closing for completeness. (3) PEAK_RSS_MB/LAST_PEAK_RSS_POLL are process-local statics reset on every ezgha restart (including routine systemctl restart per this repo's Gate-0 protocol) -- the 'peak RSS observed over lifetime' log message overstates the guarantee (it's lifetime-since-last-daemon-restart, not true fleet lifetime); reword or persist across restarts if the telemetry needs true long-run peaks. (4) No integration test exercises poll_peak_rss/reap_stale_peak_rss_entries end-to-end via the existing TEST_DOCKER_BIN fake-docker harness -- only the pure string-parsing functions (parse_docker_size_mb/parse_mem_usage_mb) are unit tested.

title: hostname-scoped deregistration residual collision + 'host' fallback
description: our_runner_prefix() (docker_backend.rs:143-145) scopes stop-deregistration by hostname, but hostnames are not unique across fleets (cloned VMs, 'ubuntu', Mac.local). Worse: hostname() (docker_backend.rs:24-31) falls back to literal 'host' on failure, so two degraded hosts both use ezgha-host- and can deregister each other's runners. Residual of the fixed fleet-wide-stop critical. Fix: include machine-id/uuid persisted in config at init. MISSED by workflow review fix-verification (Workflow 3).
close_reason: Stale — resolved by commit 627be9e (2026-07-03 19:08, 'stable numeric naming for ephemeral runners') before this bead was filed. hostname()/our_runner_prefix() no longer exist in src/docker_backend.rs; runner name prefix is now fully config-driven via cfg.runner.name_prefix (docker_backend.rs:338), further hardened in 51a5b35 'use cfg.runner.name_prefix for orphan sweep + stop_all'. Verified: grep -rn hostname src/*.rs returns only a stale comment (docker_backend.rs:330), no hostname-derived prefix/collision path remains. Each fleet member has a distinct configured prefix (ez-mac-runner-b, ez-runner-c, etc per doctor.sh), eliminating the 'host' fallback collision this bead described.

title: Process guardrail: prevent reactive-cascade / house-of-cards fix accretion
description:
2026-07-07/08 session: agent responded to a runner-fleet incident with a cascade of reactive point-fixes -- deployed po2 respawn-pacing gate to stop watchdog reboots, then relaxed the watchdog (removing po2's reason to exist), then po2's stale hardcoded ceiling (validation rejects >=24.0, tied to the OLD watchdog max-load-1=24) throttled fleet recovery even after the watchdog was raised to 96. The gap was correctly identified and captured as bead ez-gh-actions-rfk but filed as P2 "tune later" instead of treated as an urgent same-breath follow-up, so the fleet ran needlessly throttled. Net effect: a daemon that originally had zero respawn throttles accreted ~69 lines of interacting load-gate/ceiling/batch/budget logic in one day, and reactive fixes made the system more fragile, not less. Also involved repeated mis-diagnosis under pressure (declared po2 deployed-and-working while fleet was actually draining; blamed po2, then found GH rate limit; blamed budget, then found secondary rate limit) and premature success declarations without ground-truth verification.

Tracking bead for applying the harness fixes recommended by the /harness meta-analysis (see conversation for full 5-Whys + proposed rule text): a "second-fix-means-stop-and-reassess" rule, an "audit and delete obsolete workarounds when you fix a root cause" rule, a "verify with ground truth before declaring success" rule, and a complexity-budget self-check for incident-pressure changes. Proposed homes: ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md (global agent-behavior rule, not repo-specific), a Claude memory entry, and a self-invoked checklist for 3rd+ consecutive fix to the same subsystem.
close_reason: Merged to main in PR #26

title: Daemon graceful shutdown (SIGTERM handling) — required before watchdog timer re-enablement
description:
GATING CONDITION for re-enabling ezgha-watchdog.timer (currently stopped, bead 2ik ships the
durable watchdog script but does NOT fix this). Per /advice Reviewer A (PR#34 review,
2026-07-08/09): even with the watchdog's new guardrails (3-consecutive-miss, load gate, 30-min
cooldown), a LEGITIMATE watchdog-triggered restart still orphans in-flight GitHub runner
registrations, because the ezgha daemon has ZERO SIGTERM handling today — any restart (watchdog,
manual, deploy) kills it mid-flight with no graceful drain.

REQUIRED FIX before the watchdog timer is re-enabled: implement a SIGTERM handler in the ezgha
daemon's serve loop that: (1) stops accepting/starting NEW runner registrations, (2) allows any
in-flight start_one() (JIT registration → docker run) to finish (or cleanly abort + release the
slot if it can't finish within a bounded grace period, e.g. 10-15s), (3) does NOT kill already-
running/registered runner containers (those should survive a daemon restart since ezgha reconciles
state from GitHub + docker on next start), (4) exits cleanly once safe.

Complements bead ez-gh-actions-7og (slot-lifecycle state machine consolidation) — a systemd/
daemon lifecycle contract is prerequisite context for that broader consolidation, not a
duplicate of it.

Source: /advice panel Reviewer A (opus), PR #34 review, relayed via sidekick-churn.
notes:
FACTORY ROUTING: target_repo=jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions. MANDATORY for every coder/worker: commit + push ONLY — do NOT run cargo install --path ., do NOT systemctl --user restart ezgha.service, do NOT run ./docs/verify-exit-criteria.sh, do NOT run ./doctor-runner against the live fleet (single-writer deploy rule, repo CLAUDE.md). Work in an isolated worktree. cargo test must pass (220+ baseline). Spec for 30p/k5yk/29v1: docs/spec-reliability-hardening-20260709.md (committed? verify; else see dark-factory evidence bundle evidence/7fffdced606f).

DEPENDENCY-ORDER UPDATE 2026-07-11: PR 53 is the implementation vehicle and is 14 commits behind current main. Its contract drains in-flight/container-less registrations within a bounded grace period while preserving active container-backed jobs for reconciliation; it does not wait for all running jobs to finish. Rebase after PR 56 containment and before any restart-capable watchdog activation.
close_reason: done

title: doctor.sh sections 7/8 can hang indefinitely under sustained Actions-API rate limit (undocumented bucket, not shown by gh api rate_limit)

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: Discovered live during ez-gh-actions-g3o verification (2026-07-08): repos/{repo}/actions/runs/{id}/jobs returned {"message":"API rate limit exceeded for user ID ...","status":"403"} for 10+ minutes straight, while gh api rate_limit core bucket showed remaining=3569/5000 (not exhausted). This is a separate, undocumented Actions-API-specific throttle not exposed by any resource in the /rate_limit response. doctor.sh sections 7 (real job-execution proof) and 8 (queue-health.sh) both call this endpoint in a loop; under set -euo pipefail + process substitution, the script silently produces no further output for the remainder of the run once this bucket engages (observed: 3 separate full runs each stopped exactly at the section 7 header with 90s/300s/600s timeouts). Fix ideas: (1) wrap each per-run gh api call with a short timeout + explicit 403-rate-limit detection that skips remaining runs in the loop rather than hanging/silently truncating; (2) surface 'rate limited, N/6 runs checked' instead of going silent. Root-caused as external/orthogonal to the g3o fix -- section 9 (this session new per-slot proof) is immune since it is LOCAL-ONLY (docker top, no gh api calls).

title: fix(src/main.rs): wait_for_backend in-process retry around choose_backend
description: Currently src/main.rs:201-220 (Commands::Serve arm) calls choose_backend(cfg)?; — if it returns Err (e.g. Selection::None because lima isn't up yet), main() exits code 1, systemd restarts at RestartSec=10. The Jul 6 incident produced 1381 of these. Replace the immediate ? with wait_for_backend(cfg)?; which loops with capped exponential backoff (15s, 30s, 60s, 120s, 240s, 300s-cap) and only returns Ok once choose_backend succeeds. Once the daemon is in its main 30s loop, transient docker-socket blips are already contained; this change specifically protects the cold-start path. Concrete Rust patch in advisor review Q7. Verify with cargo test, then rebuild + restart per Gate 0.
notes: Implemented a9a7ab1: wait_for_backend() retries backend::select() for up to 120s, sleeping 5s between attempts. serve subcommand uses this instead of choose_backend().

title: REST+GraphQL bucket split for gh API calls -- fix registration starvation
description:
ROOT CAUSE (confirmed via journal 2026-07-07 22:47-22:51): github.rs routes ALL
calls through `gh api` (REST). REST and GraphQL are separate 5000/hr rate-limit
buckets. Heavy read-side monitoring (queue_monitor's queued-run enumeration,
per-run job listing, invariant_sampler's runner/job polling) exhausts the REST
bucket, starving the critical write path (generate_jitconfig / runner
registration, also REST) -- ephemeral runners can't register, exit immediately,
fleet bleeds toward 0 even though load/po2 are fine.

FIX:
1. Move READ-HEAVY monitoring to GraphQL: queue_monitor's queued-run + per-run
   job enumeration, and runner status listing. One GraphQL query replaces many
   REST calls -- more efficient AND a separate quota bucket.
2. KEEP registration/mutations on REST: generate_jitconfig has no GraphQL
   equivalent, must stay REST.
3. Add bucket-aware fallback: on a 403 in one transport, call `gh api
   rate_limit` (returns both buckets' remaining) and route to whichever bucket
   has budget; log which bucket is exhausted.

PROCESS (per user directive 2026-07-07): implement via codex (zero Claude
quota, Claude 429 pressure), small Claude swarm (2-3 agents max) for
adversarial review only, not a big fan-out. Branch: sidekick/rest-graphql-split.
TDD. Commit+push after every green unit of work, never hold >30min
uncommitted. Do NOT merge/deploy without main's review + a low-load window.
notes: RE-SCOPE APPROVED (main session, 2026-07-12 ~03:0x UTC) after live verification: GitHub Actions has NO GraphQL surface (Repository type has zero Actions fields; GitHub staff confirmed REST-only in community discussion #24493) — the original 'move read calls to GraphQL' fix item is unimplementable. NEW SCOPE: REST-budget-aware deprioritization. (1) track rate_limit.core.remaining via quota-exempt gh api rate_limit; (2) queue_monitor's read-heavy calls (queued-run enumeration, per-run job listing, invariant_sampler polling) SKIP/DEFER when REST budget below a floor; (3) generate_jitconfig/registration (critical write path) is NEVER gated by the budget check; (4) optional: surfaces that DO exist in GraphQL (CheckRun/CheckSuite conclusions) MAY move there for real bucket relief, but run/job/runner enumeration cannot. TDD: (a) low budget defers reads, (b) registration never gated, (c) rate_limit JSON parsing extracts core.remaining. 2026-07-19 /green audit: PR https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/pull/65 at 52cdc3b47ea99bfe2311cbcea328fe9e29faa443 is /advice APPROVED but /er PARTIAL. Add a safe monitor-only production entrypoint or equivalent Layer 2 daemon-callstack proof showing low-budget read suppression while JIT registration remains available; then refresh /er PASS and all six green gates before merge.

title: ensure-grace-window-on-online-registrations
description: Production followup: release_stale_slots Path 4 (offline+!busy self-heal, src/docker_backend.rs:370-422 at d6c366d) needs a registered-recently grace window. Track each successful registration's registered_at timestamp in slot_assignments.toml with a 60s grace window; release_stale_slots skips any registration whose registered_at is within the window. Without the grace window, ensure_count respawn (5s jitconfig propagation) races release_stale_slots (every 30s tick) and Path 4 reaps the freshly-registered id. Same race likely exists for qbl Path 2 (cancel-then-delete) racing Path 1 (slot release) since both run in the same tick. Tests: 5 new tests: (1) ensure_count respawn -> release_stale_slots within window does NOT reap; (2) after window elapses, release_stale_slots reclaims if conditions still met; (3) Path 2 cancel-then-delete timing vs Path 1; (4) cross-tick racing-registration same id; (5) the adafa19 + d6c366d regression test that would have caught this.
notes: SKEPTIC RE-PRIORITIZATION (2026-07-08 22:28 audit): this grace-window fix is the DOMINANT fix by churn volume, not a hardening nice-to-have. title: doctor-runner DOWN persistence window (25-30s) is shorter than a real respawn cycle under load — busy fleet still measures as dead
description: Evidence 2026-07-09 21:35-21:45, queue hot (5-17 queued, 21-24 in-progress): three consecutive doctor-runner runs each flagged a DIFFERENT slot persisted-DOWN (c-3, then c-7, then Mac b-2/b-5), and each flagged slot was observed 'Up <60s' immediately after — the daemon self-healed every one. Live docker ps at 21:44 showed c-9 absent and c-7 'Up Less than a second': under load, 1-2 slots are ALWAYS legitimately inside their respawn window (job finish -> deregister -> JIT token -> docker run takes 30-60s+, serialized through the 20s serve tick). The b895 2-sample persistence (25-30s apart) is SHORTER than that cycle, so a busy fleet still measures as dead — the exact defect class doctor-runner was built to eliminate. ALSO marginal: heartbeat gap measured 99s vs threshold 100s (5x20s tick) in the same run — the tick stretches under load; threshold will flap. FIX DIRECTION (one mechanism, not two): a slot should count DOWN only if containerless for longer than a full respawn cycle (e.g. 3+ samples spanning >=2-3 min, or cross-check the daemon journal for a respawn of that slot inside the window = CYCLING). Heartbeat threshold should tolerate observed busy-tick stretch (e.g. 8-10x tick or p99-based). NOTE: 4 doctor-runner truthfulness fixes shipped 2026-07-09 (b895, nj2j, wxfl/udzf, n9py/zlsz) — this is the 5th in the same theme; per complexity-budget rule consider whether section-9 local proof and section-10 inventory should be ONE code path before adding more per-mechanism fixes (they disagreed tonight: totals said 0 down while gates said 1-3 down).
close_reason: Fixed by 06e4299 (journal-crosscheck DOWN classification, 8x heartbeat threshold, section 9/10 consolidation); mirror bead closed by fix agent; verified live — first exit-0 doctor-runner runs 2026-07-10.

title: fix(lima-vm@colima.service): Type=oneshot + RemainAfterExit=yes + drop --foreground + TimeoutSec=300
description: Current unit at ~/.config/systemd/user/lima-vm@colima.service has Type=simple + TimeoutSec=10 and runs 'limactl start colima --foreground' in the foreground. On warm boot (VM already running) this completes in 16ms and seems fine; on cold boot, limactl needs minutes to bring up the QEMU guest, gets SIGKILLed by TimeoutSec=10, and the unit fails. Verified empirically with 'limactl start colima' on running VM (16ms 'Using the existing instance') and watching the cold-boot get killed. Fix unit to: drop --foreground (limactl daemonizes itself, returns when VM is SSH-able), Type=oneshot + RemainAfterExit=yes (so unit is 'active' after limactl returns, until asked to stop), TimeoutSec=10 → 300 (bound worst-case cold-boot cloud-init). Type=notify is NOT viable because limactl doesn't emit sd_notify. This single change is the highest leverage in the fix set; without it, no consumer-side Requires= will work on cold-boot.
notes: Implemented f202638: lima-vm@colima.service patched to Type=forking, drop --foreground, RemainAfterExit=yes, TimeoutStartSec=300, Restart=no, ExecStop=. Deployed + checked in to docs/lima-vm-colima.service.

title: incident: 1381-restart storm 2026-07-06 — root cause lima VM mis-target + bailing on absent backend
description: On 2026-07-06 09:02:57–12:59:06 PDT, ezgha.service entered a 3h55m loop of exit-1 systemd restarts because (a) the user's docker CLI is pinned to lima-colima context but ezgha.service only declares After=docker.service, and (b) lima-vm@colima.service has Type=simple + TimeoutSec=10 which kills limactl start before the QEMU guest boots. ezgha.service then bails on Selection::None (src/main.rs:93) and is hammered by systemd RestartSec=10. Service self-healed in 2 minutes once operator manually ran 'limactl start colima' at 12:59:06. Forensic timeline + git/diff details in PR description. title: Design periodic GitHub Actions stuck-run reaper
notes: Design target: add a periodic, dry-run-first stuck-run reaper for jleechanorg/worldarchitect.ai and other QUEUE_REPO values. It must be job-aware: do not classify a run as stale only by workflow_run.created_at because reruns/current attempts can have old run.created_at but new job.started_at/run_started_at. Proposed policy: every 10-15 minutes, scan queued and in_progress runs with REST; fetch jobs for candidates; compute current_attempt_age from max(job.started_at, run.run_started_at, run.updated_at) for queued jobs. Actions: (1) cancel-then-delete only queued run objects whose run.created_at > 8h AND current_attempt_age > 60-90m, (2) cancel superseded queued runs only when a newer queued or in_progress run exists for same repo/workflow/branch and candidate current_attempt_age > 20-30m, (3) never touch in_progress by default; report in_progress jobs over policy separately with runner/job URL, (4) label-mismatch detector reports queued jobs whose labels have no matching online runner labels, e.g. agent-orchestrator or warp-*; it does not cancel them. Install path: systemd user timer on Linux host using ~/.cargo/bin/ezgha or scripts/reap-stuck-runs.sh, with log file under ~/.local/state/ezgha/reaper.jsonl and alert() on mutations/failures. Safety: dry-run default, --apply only for timer after config enables it, max mutations per run, REST rate-limit floor, lock file, exact URLs in logs, no tail cancellation without explicit allowlist.

title: Consolidate slot-lifecycle state transitions into one owned module with explicit state machine + regression tests
description:
CROSS-INCIDENT ESCALATION per CLAUDE.md rule: "If 2+ incidents share a pattern in the last 2
weeks, escalate to a systemic fix instead of another targeted fix." 7 P0/P1 beads hit the same
slot-lifecycle/release_stale_slots subsystem in ~24h (2026-07-08/09): u3w (add Path 4),
0jo (revert-or-grace-window decision), lyf (Path 4 race with ensure_count), 5ki (grace window
fix), g3i (independent audit confirming the above + PR#32 process bypass), 2ik (watchdog restart
churn interacting with the same subsystem), and PR #33 / B1+B2 (Path 1 truncated-list mass-reclaim
+ fail-open None-container-list bug). Every one of these is a distinct edge case bolted onto the
same ~600-line release_stale_slots()/start_one_with_generate() region in src/docker_backend.rs,
with each fix (Path 1 / Path 2 qbl / Path 3 orphan sweep / Path 4 u3w) implemented as a separate
ad-hoc function with its own predicate logic, its own warning strings, and its own test file
section — no single source of truth for "what state is this slot/runner-id pair actually in, and
what transitions are legal."

PROPOSAL (design-only until scoped by an owner — do NOT start implementation preemptively per the
complexity-budget checkpoint; this bead exists to hold the systemic-fix commitment, not to trigger
immediate work):
- Define an explicit enum for slot lifecycle state (e.g. Empty, Reserved, Registering,
  RegisteredNoContainer, RegisteredWithContainer, OfflineIdleNoContainer, OfflineBusyNoContainer
  (422-zombie), StaleUnknown (docker ps failed / API truncated)) and the legal transitions between
  them.
- Move Path 1 (release_stale_slots_from_with_containers_for), Path 2 (qbl cancel-then-delete),
  Path 3 (orphan forward sweep), and Path 4 (offline_not_busy_owned_missing_container_registrations)
  into one module that computes the CURRENT state per slot once per tick, then dispatches to exactly
  one handler per state — replacing today's pattern of 4 independent predicate functions each
  re-deriving overlapping conditions (offline? busy? local container present? live in GH list?)
  from the same raw inputs.
- Add regression tests keyed by STATE, not by historical bug number, so a future incident in this
  subsystem adds a test to an existing state's table instead of a 5th sibling function.
- Explicitly test the "unknown/degraded" case (docker ps failed, GitHub API truncated/unreachable)
  as ITS OWN first-class state (StaleUnknown) with a fail-safe (keep, don't reclaim) transition
  rule, rather than each Path independently getting this right or wrong (B2 in PR#33 was exactly
  this bug recurring for the 2nd+ time in the same subsystem).

Absorbs: g3i (closing g3i as absorbed into this bead — see g3i for the full audit trail this
escalation is based on).

title: Execute kdump one-liner from ops session (low-risk, parallel to other sudo work)
description: OWNER: user (sudo). From the ops session's residual recommendations (already delivered, NOT yet executed). Single sudo block to arm kdump on this box (low blast radius, doesn't touch the ezgha runtime, doesn't touch the swapfile work — can run in parallel with the T1 sudo block; ordering between them is unimportant). COMMAND: ! sudo sed -i 's/crashkernel=512M,high/crashkernel=512M,high\ncrashkernel=256M,low/' /etc/default/grub.d/kdump-tools.cfg && sudo update-grub && sudo kdump-config load && cat /sys/kernel/kexec_crash_loaded. Acceptance: kexec_crash_loaded reports 1 after kdump-config load (the kernel has reserved the crash kernel). Failure mode: if kdump-tools.cfg doesn't contain the pattern (newer versions may differ), fall back to /etc/default/grub editing the GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX line directly per the ops session's recommendation; report and file a bead.

title: repo-wide audit: every deadline/budget parameter must propagate into blocking child-call timeouts

title: DECISION: jeff-ubuntu memory architecture — Colima VM 48GiB of 62GB host leaves ~14GB for 28 agent processes; choose remediation mix
description: Decision bead; outcome will be recorded here. GROUNDED PICTURE (2026-07-10): host 62GB total. Colima VM allocated 48GiB/24cpu (containers+runner budget live INSIDE it — daemon budget 48163MB == VM memory). Host side: 28 claude CLI processes + dark-factory daemon + system squeezed into ~14GB -> swap (16GB file, swappiness=10) pegged 99-100% Jul 8-10; CI burst -> D-state pileup -> load 218 -> watchdog reboot. OPTION SPACE (panel evaluating): (a) bigger/NVMe swapfile 32-64GB (ops lane, in motion); (b) host-aware ezgha budget (bead yz6b); (b2) RESIZE COLIMA VM down (e.g. 48->36GiB) + matching runner budget — frees host RAM directly, likely the highest-leverage single change; (c) reduce runner count (gated on user, changes 22/22 standard); (d) host-side protections: zram, systemd-oomd/earlyoom, PSI-based agent throttling, cgroup memory.high for agent sessions; (e) cap concurrent CI jobs. /research + /advice panel running 2026-07-10 to pick the mix.
notes: DECISION RECORDED (panel 3/3 synthesis, 2026-07-10 eve): TIER 1 DO-NOW (host-side, ops/sudo): (1) systemd-oomd or earlyoom with PSI thresholds — MUST land before/with any swap expansion (unanimous: more disk swap alone deepens the thrash cliff; the incident was thrash/D-state, oomd fires in seconds vs watchdog reboot — self-outage principle); (2) agent CLIs into a systemd slice with memory.high (~20GB) — the agents are the uncontrolled workload; (3) ops swapfile demoted to backstop, only WITH (1); NO zram until oomd verified (thin-provisioning brownout class, Cloudflare Mar 2026). TIER 2 PR-PIPELINE: yz6b refined — budget = VM_total - guest_reserve (~4GB), fail-loud, keep ~3GB/runner floor (research: 2GB bare minimum, 3GB practical for jest+playwright+rust; do NOT shrink runners to reclaim host RAM); optional CI concurrency ramp cap. TIER 3 DEFERRED PENDING DATA: Colima VM resize — QEMU Lima has NO ballooning (lima#4220), shrink returns nothing without stop/start; documented image-loss footgun (colima#1393); requires per-runner peak-RSS telemetry + planned maintenance window + user sign-off. Runner count 16 unchanged. Full lane reports in session 2026-07-10.

title: factory-overlay: live daemon and interactive /af tick fight over bead states (HUMAN_HELD park + QUEUED reset observed twice in one drive)
description: FACTORY ROUTING: target_repo=jleechanorg/dark-factory. Evidence 2026-07-10 drive: (1) ~20:04 the daemon moved ez-gh-actions-30p and mw5a from DISPATCHED to HUMAN_HELD while interactive coders were mid-work (recover-held reset them to QUEUED, breaking the pr-opened chain); (2) ~20:45 mw5a was back in QUEUED when the verifier tried gate-assessment (expected ATTESTED), requiring a manual dispatch-record->pr-opened->gate-assessment replay. Root cause hypothesis: the daemon's autonomy-tick/park logic doesn't distinguish beads dispatched by an interactive /af session (subagent coders, no AO session to attach) from its own AO-spawned beads, so its liveness checks see 'no session' and park/reset them. FIX direction: dispatch-record should tag the dispatch mode (ao|subagent|interactive); daemon park/reap logic must skip beads whose mode isn't ao, or use a lease/owner field so two orchestrators never fight over the same row. Acceptance: an interactive /af drive completes intake->READY with a running daemon and zero state resets.

Peak load1=18.02 (~02:38-39, above the 15 alert line, well under 24 ceiling, self-reported); one 3-sample dip to 9 containers at 02:42-43 self-recovered in 14s with confirmed active ensure_count respawning (churn, not drain). Load settled to steady 10-11 1/5/15-min averages by 02:50, containers oscillating 11-16 (healthy). No sustained drain, no restart needed, watchdog.timer confirmed still stopped throughout.

title: Gate 0 should ignore docs/fmt-only HEAD advances (false-red on every non-functional commit)
description: Gate 0 in docs/verify-exit-criteria.sh compares deployed binary SHA to current git HEAD SHA verbatim, so ANY commit after a build (including pure docs or cargo-fmt-only commits) reds the gate even though nothing functional changed. Incident 2026-07-08: deployed the reaper zombie-slot self-heal + App-token fix (bfddf83/5f0374a) cleanly, then a STATE-mirror doc commit and a pure-whitespace cargo-fmt fixup each independently re-broke Gate 0, forcing 2 extra rebuild+restart cycles chasing SHA parity for zero functional benefit -- a textbook reactive-cascade trap (team-lead explicitly flagged this and told the deploy-owner to stop chasing it). Fix: Gate 0 should compare either (a) a source-tree hash that excludes docs/**, goals/**, *.md, and .beads/issues.jsonl, or (b) skip the check entirely when the only diff between deployed-SHA and HEAD is non-functional (docs/fmt-only), so cosmetic HEAD advances don't misreport a real deploy as broken. Tie to the 2026-07-08 reaper+App-token deploy incident; see goals/2026-07-07-1920-runner-truly-healthy/STATE-merge-driver.md for the full sequence.

title: Independent 24h review verdict: churn regression confirmed (revised root cause), PR#32 process bypass, recommend 5ki+watchdog fix together
description:
Independent sidekick audit (4 tracks + adversarial skeptic, all read-only, model sonnet) re-verified main's churn-regression claim from raw evidence tonight. Full doc: goals/2026-07-07-1920-runner-truly-healthy/runner-24h-review-20260709.md

VERDICT SUMMARY:
1. Daemon IS still actively churning as of check time (11-13 respawns + 5-6 reclaims per 3-5min window, confirmed at 3 independent checkpoints ~20min apart). 16/16 containers nominally present but 94% under 6min old -- throughput-degradation, not outage. Load 2.28, no watchdog/crash risk.
2. Root cause REVISED from main's original "~5s GitHub API propagation race" framing: Path 4 (release_stale_slots, offline_not_busy_owned_missing_container_registrations, docker_backend.rs:631-662) has zero grace-period as originally found -- confirmed. But adversarial skeptic found this fires INDEPENDENT of any restart for 92% of reclaim volume (recomputed inside a single uninterrupted daemon lifetime) -- NOT primarily a restart-interaction race as Track A's first pass suggested. A SEPARATE real bug also exists: ezgha-fleet-watchdog.sh restarts ezgha.service on 75% of "BELOW TARGET" ticks (15/20 in 3h, verified two ways), which is a Self-Outage-Prevention-Principle violation in its own right (daemon has zero SIGTERM handling, restart can orphan an in-flight docker run), but explains only a MINORITY of total churn volume.
   RECOMMENDATION: ship 5ki (reaper grace-window, dominant driver by volume) together with a watchdog cooldown fix, not sequentially with watchdog first -- watchdog-only fix would likely leave fleet still failing CLAUDE.md 22/22-executing bar.
3. PROCESS-INTEGRITY: PR #32 (bead u3w, commit d6c366d) merged with ZERO approving reviews (human or bot) -- independently re-verified twice (Track B + skeptic, no overlap in method). CodeRabbit's one real finding's fix was merged 5s after push with no re-review. No PR-#32-specific skeptic verdict artifact exists anywhere. Bead 4kv (the PR's own prescribed deploy-authorization gate) remains OPEN/unexecuted -- PR merged anyway, bypassing its own process. u3w bead never actually closed despite commit claiming to close it. Two mission-brief claims REFUTED: "8gj merged twice" (only one PR/commit ever touched it) and "u3w closed->reopened->closed" (bead was simply never closed at all).
4. Beads lyf/0jo/5ki: well-formed, non-duplicate, actionable -- confirmed via independent bead audit. Gaps: no bead owns "deploy 5ki once written"; none state current container count (throughput vs outage ambiguity). u3w/s9d status drift flagged. 7 P0/P1 beads in 24h all touch same respawn/reap subsystem -- systemic-fix escalation candidate per CLAUDE.md cross-incident rule, not yet acted on.
5. OVERALL GRADE: C+. Strong technical/investigative quality (qbl, nuk App-token fix both confirmed real/deployed/well-verified, unrelated to this incident). But PR #32 bypassed every review gate simultaneously and is the prime suspect in a live P0 regression -- caps grade below B-tier.

RECOMMENDED NEXT ACTION (0jo A/B/C tree): NOT (B) stop-daemon (no safety emergency, real work happening per docker top). NOT (A) full-revert-only (would not fix watchdog issue). (C) fast-path grace window -- but ship 5ki AND a watchdog cooldown fix together, not watchdog-first as a literal reading of the first-pass analysis might suggest. This is the user's/deploy-owner's call; this bead does not authorize deployment.

Full track evidence at /tmp/ez-gh-actions/sidekick/runner-24h-review/tracks/{A,B,C,D,skeptic}.md (ephemeral /tmp, condensed into the committed synthesis doc).
close_reason: RESOLVED via this session's re-verification + fix. This bead's churn-still-happening claim was independently RE-CONFIRMED live 2026-07-09 ~18:30 PDT (not just trusted from the original review) -- 306 respawns + 177 reclaims in a 3h restart-free jeff-ubuntu window, comparable-to-worse than this bead's original 11-13/3-5min finding, well after PR #33-#37 had already shipped. Fixed via commit d0e814e (bead 5ki's grace-window spec, closed with full evidence chain there). Adversarial skeptic independently verified the fix's correctness and found one residual gap (forward-sweep orphan reaper unprotected, follow-up jleechan-64tl, narrower crash-window class not the live-tick race this bead measured). This bead's PROCESS-INTEGRITY finding (PR #32 merged with zero reviews) is a historical process-audit finding, not an open defect -- no further action from this session; flagging for whoever owns process-gate enforcement to review separately if desired. NOT yet deployed -- fix is on origin/main, deploy is a separate main-session action.

title: fix(src/main.rs Init): refuse runner.count above daemon capacity
description: Companion to ez-gh-actions-vmz (effective_limits over-commit). After fixing effective_limits() in src/docker_backend.rs:241-254 to clamp per-runner share to capacity/count, update src/main.rs:120-124 Init arm to refuse runner.count > max(1, ncpu / cfg.limits.cpus.max(0.5)) rather than refusing only on memory. Today a user can set count=16 on a 4-CPU daemon with no per-CPU check. After patch: 'error: cfg.runner.count=16 exceeds capacity max(1, 4 cpus / 0.5 cpus/runner) = 8 runners; lower cfg.runner.count, raise cfg.limits.cpus (but not above ncpu), or provision more CPUs.' Modifies only the Init arm of src/main.rs; cargo test + Gate 0-10 re-verify.
close_reason: Stale/done: init capacity bail and aggregate clamp are implemented in src/main.rs and src/docker_backend.rs; roadmap already treats this as closed.

title: Blast-radius review gate for threshold/health/monitor/restart changes (self-outage class)
description: Failure class: a safety/health/monitoring mechanism caused the very outage it was meant to prevent because a config/threshold change's blast radius and interactions were never evaluated. Real 2026-07-07 instances: (1) /etc/watchdog.conf max-load-1=24 on 32-thread box rebooted the host twice during normal Gate-0 mass respawns (~71 1-min load); (2) E1 invariant sampler drained the fleet to zero by starving the single-threaded serve loop; (3) slack_webhook_url unset + primary webhook 404 -> alerting silently dead all mission; (4) careful-restart rule lived in one session's context only. Deliverable = durable guardrails: (a) CLAUDE.md 'blast-radius / interaction review' rule for ANY change to a threshold/health-check/watchdog/restart-policy/resource-limit/monitor-cadence, requiring the change to state the normal peak of the bounded metric and the remaining margin; (b) principle 'a safety/health/monitoring mechanism must not be able to cause the failure it guards against'; (c) doctor.sh gate flagging watchdog max-load-1 vs observed respawn peak margin; (d) version-control /etc/watchdog.conf + margin rationale in-repo. Related: ez-gh-actions-po2 (daemon respawn pacing), ez-gh-actions-2ik (commit external watchdog script), queue_monitor::SERVE_LOOP_TIME_BUDGET (serve-loop starvation test).

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: RESEARCH (cited): watchdog(8) best practice = per-CORE thresholds (~10x/core 1-min LOOSE, ~5-7x/core 15-min tight; on 32 threads ~320 at 1-min, so our 96 is conservative/safe). Review MISSED: (1) require SUSTAINED breach over full window not instant sample; (2) SOFT-REPAIR before reboot (repair-binary ~/.local/bin/watchdog-load-repair.sh exists — verify wired); (3) REBOOT-LOOP PROTECTION: cooldown on repeated reboots — mass-respawn after reboot is itself a spike that can re-trigger + loop (documented anti-pattern). doctor.sh gate should also assert repair-binary present+executable.

title: INCIDENT: runner fleet performance degradation and reconciliation churn — 2026-07-10
description: Adversarial 24-hour review on 2026-07-10 found the fleet below its required 22/22 Runner.Worker standard. Final snapshot: Linux 14/16 executing with c-10 and c-15 down; Mac 5/6 executing with one idle; seven queued runs and oldest run-level queue age 30.7 minutes. Linux journal recorded 2,526 respawns, 1,430 stale-slot reclaims, 59 failure lines, six timeouts, partial refills, and repeated service restarts. Mac logs recorded 1,301 respawns, 22 backend runtime restarts, recurring resource-unavailable failures, and sustained host load above core count. This parent tracks remediation and proof of sustained recovery, not merely an instantaneous green snapshot. Exit: 22/22 executing under backlog, job-level queue tail below 20 minutes, no unexplained stale-reclaim loop or backend restart storm, and historical evidence over a representative sustained window.
notes: DEPENDENCY-ORDER UPDATE 2026-07-11: Factory prerequisites first: dark-factory jleechan-v2wv (LaunchAgent br activation) then jleechan-x7ky (fail-closed cross-repo routing). Then refresh PR 56 on main as phase-zero backend containment; land PR 53 / 30p; serialize ghd2.1 then ghd2.2; establish resource envelopes; perform ghd2.6 only in a drained maintenance window; use ghd2.7 as the integration owner; then r8od, 29v1, and jleechan-yuia; activate watchdogs last after PR 51, 30p, lxn, and liveness/activation proof. Do not dispatch ghd2.7 in parallel with its component work on the same runtime surfaces.

title: Fix Linux stale-reclaim/respawn amplification under load and partial API/container snapshots
description: Observed over 24 hours: 2,526 respawns and 1,430 release_stale_slots reclaims, with repeated same-minute reclaim/refill cycles, partial ensure_count outcomes, Docker CLI timeouts, and gh API timeouts. The 60-second registration grace guard from PR 33 exists but does not prevent sustained production churn. Investigate each reclaim reason, API/list consistency, pre-refill and post-refill reconciliation, and container visibility under host load. Acceptance: reason-coded metrics prove legitimate completed-job respawns are separated from destructive reclaims; degraded or partial snapshots fail safe; fleet sustains 16/16 executing under backlog without repeated reclaim of fresh capacity; add an integration/failure-injection regression that spans consecutive ticks. Link systemic design ez-gh-actions-7og and chaos bead ez-gh-actions-29v1.

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: 2026-07-15 Mac live proof on clean deployed e197313: bounded-convergence defect reproduced under 90-97 queued jobs. JIT containers are one-job --rm. ensure_count_outcome snapshots alive once, computes one missing count, refills that many, reconciles, then returns without re-counting; other jobs exit during the serialized refill. Exact stderr chain lines 5323-5346: busy=4/registered=6; reclaim to local=2; start 4; next pass sees local=1; start 5; next sees 4. Zero 'partial snapshot' logs, so detected API truncation was not the cause. With serve_tick_seconds lowered 30->5, live gaps improved but still reached 4/4 and up to ~28s below 6/6 because one pass itself took ~16s plus sleep/monitor work. Acceptance addition: bounded re-count/refill within one ensure call with pass/deadline cap; RED fixture where an existing container exits during first refill; retain partial-snapshot no-mutation test. Exact post-restart local proof at 2026-07-15T09:46:20Z showed all six Runner.Worker PIDs on binary e197313 before capacity fell again.

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: 2026-07-15 live refill evidence on Mac exact socket: during recurring local 6->5/4 gaps, /tmp/ezgha-launchd-stderr.log repeatedly records 'no runner slot available to attempt: all 6 runner slot(s) are currently in use' then 'ensure_count failed (will retry)' even though docker ps/top proves a slot container/Runner.Worker is absent. Therefore the root cause is not only one-snapshot/serve-loop cadence: slot_assignments can retain all six reservations while an ephemeral --rm job/container has exited and GitHub registration is still lagging/busy. Fix must model this transition without treating it as a backend failure/restart, preserve the local-container guard for partial API snapshots, hard-cap starts per call, and test no-free-slot during legitimate JIT turnover. Prior PR #83's repeated start loop does not solve this reservation state. Evidence on deployed https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/commit/8624d3c7f02bbaace94dbcd418af3293693c8635.

Capacity workflow https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/actions/runs/29412689236 produced six simultaneous local Runner.Worker PIDs at 11:45:16Z. First turnover wave: 0/6 containers at 11:48:34Z, 3/6 at 11:48:39Z, 5/6 at 11:48:41Z, 6/6 replacement IDs at 11:48:43Z; 0-to-6 refill took 9 seconds. title: Fix offline-busy 422 registration wedges without backend restart storms
description: Linux c-10/c-14 and Mac b-4 repeatedly had no usable local container while GitHub reported offline and busy. Runner deletion returned HTTP 422, slot refill failed, and Mac escalated into backend restart attempts. Existing jleechan-yie documents the GitHub-side lock but current behavior still sacrifices capacity and restarts healthy runtimes. Acceptance: represent the state explicitly; defer or quarantine the affected slot without restarting Docker/Colima; continue filling all other slots; bound retry/API volume; alert with runner id and age; recover automatically after GitHub releases the lock; regression test the 422 sequence across multiple reconciliation ticks.

comments.author: sidekick-memarch
comments.text: CROSS-LINK (T3, sidekick-memarch): related to ez-gh-actions-rqb9 (c-3 incident, 2026-07-10) and jleechan-yie (GitHub-side wedge mechanism) — all three are variants of the same 'stale/wedged registration' bug family, but distinct sub-cases, do not collapse into one PR: (1) rqb9 = container UP + IDLE (no Runner.Worker) but GH registration GONE -> daemon's keep-slot guard wrongly preserves it -> fix is RECYCLE (docker rm+respawn) when idle+registration-absent. (2) ghd2.2 (this bead) = runner reported offline+BUSY by GH with no usable local container -> DELETE returns 422 (GH-side lock) -> current behavior sacrifices capacity or triggers backend restart storms (Mac escalation) -> fix is DEFER/QUARANTINE the slot (not recycle, not restart) + bounded retry + alert, auto-recover once GH releases the lock. (3) jleechan-yie documents WHY the 422 lock persists (GitHub-side heartbeat timeout after runner VM death; cancel/force-cancel return 200 but job stays in_progress) — the root mechanism ghd2.2's quarantine window should be sized against. Recommend ghd2.2's acceptance criteria explicitly reference rqb9's recycle path so the two don't regress each other (a wedged-but-idle container should recycle per rqb9; a wedged-and-busy-per-GH container should quarantine per ghd2.2 — the daemon needs to distinguish these two states, not just 'container exists, GH says something else').

title: Enforce Mac host and Colima resource envelope before runner refill
description: Mac observations: load averages reached 16.83/36.17/50.74 and ended at 25.69/28.04/41.38 on 14 cores; a Docker CLI spawn failed with Resource temporarily unavailable; launchd recorded 15 daemon runs, 22 backend runtime restarts, and 1,301 runner respawns in about 25 hours. Colima reports about 3.9 GiB VM memory while runner limits are derived from a larger daemon/host figure. No direct jetsam/OOM kill was found, so distinguish proven fork/resource exhaustion from suspected memory overcommit. Acceptance: derive aggregate CPU/memory limits from the actual Colima VM envelope; refuse impossible count-times-limit configurations; apply host load/process pressure backoff without restarting a healthy backend; prove 6-slot or right-sized capacity under sustained workload. Link sizing bead jleechan-tan.
notes: INCIDENT + CONTAINMENT 2026-07-12 ~03:30 UTC (Mac): deploying merged main (eac8e5b) surfaced the yz6b fail-loud memory guard rejecting the Mac config — 6 runners x 3072MB floor + 4096 reserve = 18432MB required vs VM 15957MB (shortfall 6571MB). The OLD daemon had been silently clamping to 2659MB/runner (the exact under-floor jest-OOM class yz6b outlawed; also listed in jleechan-83c systemic diagnosis). CONTAINMENT: runner.count 6 -> 3 in BOTH config paths (~/.config/ezgha/config.toml + ~/Library/Application Support/org.jleechanorg.ezgha/config.toml, .bak-yz6b backups saved), daemon restarted clean (PID 94423, version eac8e5b, no budget errors, supervising 3). DECISION NEEDED (this bead's scope): restore Mac capacity by resizing Colima 16GiB -> 24GiB (host has 48GB; 6x3072+4096=22528 fits) — requires colima stop/start = kills in-flight jobs + full cold respawn, so schedule in a drained window; alternatively accept 3-runner Mac permanently and raise Linux count. Fleet standard doc (22 total) is violated at 19/22 until resolved.

title: Reduce re-push cancellation waste and self-hosted final-gate queue latency
description: A 500-run sample over about 118 minutes had 41 cancellations; among non-skipped runs about 32 percent were cancelled. One branch cancelled 11 of 13 Green Gate attempts, estimated near 90 self-hosted runner-minutes wasted. In a 78-job sample, queue latency was p50 66s, p90 570s, p99/max 956s; final Green Gate jobs commonly waited 11-16 minutes. The diagnostic likely counts timestamp entries as independent reservations. title: Linux dual Lima namespace after reboot: converge to one backend owner
description: Discovered by parallel read-only 48h audit on 2026-07-11. Since the 2026-07-10 12:39 reboot, jeff-ubuntu has both managed ~/.config/colima/_lima/colima (4 CPU/12 GiB) and legacy ~/.lima/colima (24 CPU/48 GiB); Docker context lima-colima uses the legacy socket. Boot trace shows managed colima start nonzero followed by unscoped limactl start colima exit 0, the same namespace-confused recovery class as Mac. Host is resource-critical (~57/62 GiB used, swap 15/15 GiB). Acceptance: choose/document one lifecycle owner and canonical Docker endpoint; drain jobs before live cleanup; remove/retire duplicate VM without data loss; no unscoped limactl fallback; Docker postcondition required; 16/16 sustained proof; exact merged deployed SHA and rollback evidence.
notes: DEPENDENCY-ORDER UPDATE 2026-07-11: Live Linux proof shows both VMs running, but Docker context lima-colima points to the legacy ~/.lima/colima 48 GiB VM; managed ~/.config/colima/_lima/colima is 12 GiB. Do not promise approximately 36 GiB reclaimed: stopping the active legacy VM would take down the fleet, while stopping the managed VM reclaims much less resident memory. Choose and migrate to a viable canonical owner/socket under job drain, then measure actual RSS reclaimed and prove rollback plus 16/16 execution.

title: Implement singleton backend-aware recovery controller for Mac and Linux
description: Cross-platform implementation owner for the 2026-07-11 harness design. Ezgha runtime repo must own the exceptional recovery controller; worldarchitect.ai must not duplicate it. Acceptance: one declared backend lifecycle owner per host; atomic singleton lock and boot-scoped state; classify GitHub/API, per-slot 422, Docker-down, and host-pressure failures before mutation; canonical Docker context/socket identity; command rc never equals recovery; bounded attempts/cooldown/manual lockout; reversible exact-path stale-state quarantine only after no busy runners/live disk holder; whole-process-tree cleanup; Docker succeeds twice then configured containers+GitHub registrations+job pickup converge; structured transition telemetry; Mac and Linux reboot/namespace/pressure/422 tests; committed launchd/systemd templates and install/activation proof.
notes: 2026-07-19 sidekick (jleechan-plkw): PR #70 (branch factory/ez-gh-actions-ghd2.7-r1) closed as rejected, NOT merged -- confirmed real safety defect via independent verification + 2 bot reviews (Cursor Bugbot High, chatgpt-codex-connector P2), both converging on the same root cause: maybe_restart_backend() in src/main.rs calls the real attempt_backend_restart() BEFORE calling RecoveryController::attempt(), so the controller's internal lockout/cooldown gate (correctly implemented in isolation, recovery_controller.rs ~L579-587) never actually gates the mutation -- engaging manual lockout does not stop a concurrent restart. Second defect: Docker health probe in recovery_controller.rs ~L725 validates the socket via open(2) instead of a real connect, so it can never observe success (ENXIO on a live listening socket), breaking the "Docker succeeds twice then converge" acceptance criterion. Recommended fix for the next implementation pass: (1) move the actual restart command inside the closure passed to controller.attempt(...) instead of before it, (2) fix the socket probe to use AF_UNIX connect or Docker CLI/API, (3) add an integration-level regression test asserting an engaged lockout prevents the restart COMMAND from running, not just that telemetry records a refusal. Bead intentionally left OPEN -- singleton recovery controller acceptance criteria are not yet met on main.

title: fix-mac-token-refresh-launchd-idle
description: Mac token-refresh service is idle. If the App-token expires, the Mac daemon won't refresh the token automatically.
notes:
REMEDIATION COMPLETE except item 5 (2026-07-08 23:40 PDT):

title: doctor-runner: unescaped runner-name interpolation into grep regex could misclassify on metacharacter prefixes
description: codex adversarial review 2026-07-10 (not fixed in this pass, scope discipline). journal_has_respawn_evidence() in doctor-runner (~line 179, was ~168 pre-review) does `grep -q "respawned ephemeral runner ${name}\$"` — the runner name/prefix is interpolated directly into a grep BRE/ERE pattern unescaped. If RUNNER_NAME_PREFIX or MAC_RUNNER_NAME_PREFIX (config-driven, operator-settable) ever contains a regex metacharacter (., *, [, ^, $, etc.), the pattern could match unintended log lines (false CYCLING classification, masking a real DOWN) or fail to match the intended one (false DOWN). Fix: escape $name for literal grep matching (e.g. `grep -qF` for fixed-string match, or sed-escape metacharacters before interpolation) before shipping to production names with special characters.

title: graceful daemon re-exec (ezgha reload) — zero-respawn-wave code rollouts
description:
User's idea (2026-07-07, via main during po2 deploy). Currently every ezgha
daemon code update requires systemctl --user restart ezgha.service, which
kills and respawns the daemon process -- runner containers survive (separate
Docker procs) but the daemon re-adopts them cold via ensure_count on the new
process's first tick, and if timed wrong (load>12, containers<12) this has
tripped the host watchdog reboot twice in one day (2026-07-07).

Proposed: an `ezgha reload` command/signal that does an in-place exec() of
the new binary between serve-loop ticks (like nginx/systemd graceful reload),
re-adopting the already-running containers via the existing slot_assignments
+ ensure_count reconciliation path rather than a full process kill+restart.
Combined with the po2 gate-primary respawn pacing (merged 2026-07-07,
commit d612ad7/deployed 6a3138d), this would eliminate the restart-driven
reboot risk entirely -- code rollouts would never need the careful-restart
load<12/containers>=12 gate because there's no respawn wave to pace, just a
process re-exec with the existing container fleet immediately re-adopted.

Do NOT implement yet -- P2, capture only. Implementation routes through
/sidekick (sidekick3) or a swarm when picked up, not direct main coding,
per the 2026-07-07 delegation directive.

description: THE real P0 per advice-p0 root-cause analysis. Today's mechanism of harm: N concurrent agent sessions across 2 machines each build+cargo-install+restart ezgha racing each other, with NO serialization. This is HOW unreviewed code reached prod (bead jleechan-qo8: Mac ran uncommitted ccbd10e-dirty, Linux 6a3138d, NEITHER on main — Gate 0 violated) and how the po2 pacing-deadlock bug went live. title: Watchdog churn-guard masks a genuine stuck-serve-loop stall — pre-arm bead before timer re-enablement
description:
CONFIRMED DESIGN GAP from /advice Reviewer C cold-review pass (PR #34, 2026-07-09), architectural
reasoning to be verified in code as part of this bead's work:

The watchdog's "ephemeral churn, don't restart" guard (actual managed-container count below
target BUT reserved-slot count >= target -> assume the daemon is just mid-respawn-cycle, skip
restart, do not even count it as a miss) is correct for the common case but MASKS the genuine
"daemon's single-threaded serve loop is fully stuck" scenario: release_stale_slots runs inside
that same serve loop, so a truly stuck loop means no reaping happens, the slot file stays frozen
at its last-known state (typically == configured count, satisfying the churn guard's condition),
and the guard therefore reads as permanently true — the watchdog would never fire, believing
"ephemeral churn" indefinitely while the daemon is actually dead in the water.

Why tonight's original (pre-2ik) watchdog DID trip during the real incident: the daemon was ALIVE
and actively reaping/respawning (slots dropping below configured, not frozen at it) — this is a
DIFFERENT failure mode than a genuine full stall, and the pre-arm gap only applies to the stall
case, not the actively-churning case this PR's guardrails already handle well.

REQUIRED before the watchdog timer is ever re-enabled (in addition to the reboot-stale-state
guard bead ez-gh-actions-xfw and the SIGTERM/graceful-shutdown bead ez-gh-actions-30p /
ez-gh-actions-uh2): the churn guard needs an independent STALENESS SIGNAL that can distinguish
"slots frozen because healthy churn just settled" from "slots frozen because the loop is dead" --
e.g. journal/log freshness (has the daemon logged ANY reconciliation-tick activity in the last N
minutes?) or a "last successful respawn timestamp" the daemon itself writes and the watchdog can
read independently of the slot file. Verify the architectural reasoning above against the actual
serve-loop code (src/main.rs or wherever the reconciliation loop lives) as the first step of this
bead's work -- confirm release_stale_slots really does run inside the single-threaded loop with
no independent liveness heartbeat before designing the fix.

LOWEST PRIORITY, note only (Reviewer C#7/8, do not action unless trivial when this bead is
worked): (a) exit-code aggregation across check_mac/check_linux could be clearer than the current
EXIT=$? accumulation pattern; (b) watchdog.log has no rotation/size cap, could grow unbounded over
long uptimes.

Source: /advice Reviewer C (cold review), PR #34, relayed via sidekick-churn.
notes: DEPENDENCY-ORDER UPDATE 2026-07-11: Watchdog activation is last. It remains blocked on PR 51 / ez-gh-actions-xfw, graceful shutdown 30p, shell CI r8od, chaos 29v1, doctor proof jleechan-yuia, and an independent serve-loop heartbeat that cannot be masked by frozen slot state.

comments.text: Convergent creation - kept local (newer)

title: release-stale-slots-races-respawn-grace-window-needed
description:
CRITICAL REGRESSION in PRODUCTION. The new u3w 4th sub-pass (commit d6c366d, src/docker_backend.rs release_stale_slots + the new offline_not_busy_owned_missing_container_registrations helper) is racing with ensure_count in a churn loop: daemon respawns slot 15 (~5s to register via jitconfig) → release_stale_slots runs in that ~5s window → sees the freshly-registered id as offline-not-busy-not-in-local-containers list → calls github::remove_runner on the brand-new registration → next ensure_count tick respawns again → loop. Live evidence: 17 respawns in 5 min, slot ez-runner-c-15 respawned 4 times in 5 min, slot_assignments.toml shows slot 15 at runner-id 141563 but GitHub registry no longer has it (or is replacing it constantly). CodeRabbit's dedup fix 8368b7e only prevents double-reaping inside ONE tick — the dedup HashSet is local, ticks race across the boundary.

Root-cause class: the synthesis §6 recommendation was NO grace window in v1, and that proved wrong in production — there is no grace window for JIT registration propagation. The qbl 422-zombie path (lines 406-410) has the same potential race against Path 1.

FIX SKELETON:
- Add a registered-recently grace window (e.g. 60s) tracked in slot_assignments.toml with a registered_at timestamp per id; skip any registration whose registered_at is within the window.
- OR: ensure_count should mark each respawn's runner_id as 'fresh, skip-reap' for the next 60s, and release_stale_slots should consult that flag.
- Tests: 5 new tests covering: (1) ensure_count respawns → release_stale_slots within window does NOT reap; (2) after window, release_stale_slots reclaims; (3) graceful missed reaping; (4) qbl-cancel-then-delete timing vs Path 1; (5) cross-tick racing runner.

DEPLOY BLOCKER: until fixed, every restart of the daemon triggers this loop, and the fleet cannot hold 22/22 because slot 15 (and probably others) is being chomped every cycle. ALSO: the auto-factory's 4kv deploy plan would RE-INTRODUCE this race on every restart cycle even on a clean binary. Recommend rolling back d6c366d + 8368b7e to a known-good state (adafa19+qbl-fix on origin/main pre d6c366d, which DID NOT have this race because Path 4 didn't exist yet) before further restarts.

P0 - root-cause bug, production regression
close_reason: Duplicate/companion of ez-gh-actions-5ki, same fix. Closed together: commit d0e814e implements the registered_at grace window this bead's FIX SKELETON described (option 1: registered_at timestamp per id, grace window in release_stale_slots). See 5ki's closure note for full evidence chain (live re-measurement, implementation detail, test coverage, skeptic verification). This bead's DEPLOY BLOCKER note is resolved by the fix landing in source; actual fleet deployment is a separate main-session action per single-writer rule, tracked via the standing Gate 0 deploy checklist, not by this bead.

title: install.sh / install-launchagents.sh: sed replacements + worktree guard not robust to & / backslash / literal 'worktree' path segments
description:
codex adversarial review 2026-07-10 (not fixed in this pass, scope discipline). Two related robustness gaps in the placeholder-substitution + guard pattern shared by install.sh (systemd units, ~line 350: `sed -e "s|@SCRIPTS_DIR@|${SCRIPTS_DIR}|g" -e "s|@HOME@|${HOME_DIR}|g"`) and launchd/install-launchagents.sh (~line 94: same pattern for plists):
1. sed replacement text is not escaped for `&` or backslash. If HOME or SCRIPTS_DIR ever contains a literal `&` or `\\`, sed treats `&` as "whole match" and backslash sequences specially, corrupting the substituted path instead of inserting it literally.
2. The anti-drift guard (`grep -qi worktree <<<"$scanned"`) rejects ANY rendered unit/plist whose SUBSTITUTED VALUES happen to contain the literal substring "worktree" anywhere in a legitimate path segment (e.g. a username or project dir literally named ...worktree...), not just an actual leftover repo-worktree checkout path — a false-positive refusal to install for those users/paths.
Fix: escape sed replacement values for `&`, `\\`, and the delimiter before interpolating (e.g. via a small sed-escape helper), and consider scoping the worktree guard to the SCRIPT_DIR-relative substituted path components rather than a blanket substring match across the whole rendered file.

title: commit adversarial cold-skeptic review transcripts as PR evidence artifacts (recurring gap: 0725, yz6b)
description: Two consecutive PRs (ez-gh-actions-0725 PR #54, ez-gh-actions-yz6b PR #55) went through multi-round adversarial cold-skeptic review, but the review transcripts themselves were never committed as artifacts -- only narrated in the PR body/bead comments by the same dispatching agent. Evidence-reviewer flagged this both times as the same recurring anti-pattern ('document citing itself' / self-referential claim chain), each time as low-severity because the underlying technical claims were independently re-verifiable and WERE re-verified -- but the pattern itself is a harness gap per CLAUDE.md's 'escalate to systemic fix on 2+ recurring incidents in 2 weeks' rule. FIX: when a sidekick/orchestrator dispatches an adversarial verifier sub-agent, capture its full verdict text to a committed artifact (e.g. evidence/<bead>/verify-round-N.md, or a PR-attached gist) alongside the PR, not just a paraphrased summary in the PR body or a bead comment. This closes the 'document citing itself' gap without requiring a full canonical evidence-standards bundle (evidence.md/metadata.json/checksums) for every host-ops or small Rust PR -- a committed raw transcript is enough per the evidence-standards rubric's 'raw artifact not assertion' principle.

title: REVERT the fail-loud unmapped_repo behavior — make /af repo-agnostic with sensible defaults
description: USER DIRECTIVE 2026-07-12 (jleechan): 'i dont want any configs or anything in any repo i just want factory to be able to handle any repo.' CURRENT BEHAVIOR (baked into jleechan-35y4 Stage B, merged in dark-factory PR #245): when a bead's resolved repo is not in [repos] AND not target_repo, the overlay parks it HUMAN_HELD with reason=unmapped_target_repo (fail loud, never guess). This means every NEW repo requires a daemon.toml edit before /af can drive it — friction the user explicitly rejected. NEW BEHAVIOR (this bead): for any bead whose target_repo resolves to a repo the daemon hasn't seen, DERIVE defaults and WARN-log rather than fail-loud: ao_project = repo-slug (e.g. jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions -> ez-gha), push_remote = origin (the documented exception is worldai/wa; everything else is origin). Config-table [repos] entries remain authoritative when present (overrides the derived default). Fail loud ONLY when: (a) ao_project name collision across multiple mapped repos (genuinely ambiguous), or (b) target_repo is not parseable as owner/name. EFFECT: any repo with factory-labeled work Just Works on first tick. Acceptance: (1) update daemon/factory-overlay.sh unmapped-repo branch to derive + warn + dispatch (default vs human-held), (2) add config [repos.*] detection that an existing entry overrides the derived default, (3) tests for the three branches (mapped, derived-default, fail-loud), (4) update daemon.toml.example's [repos] comment block to document the new precedence: explicit entry > derived default > fall through to daemons's prior behavior for owner/parse errors only, (5) doc the user-visible contract change in docs/multirepo-dispatch-investigation-2026-07-11.md (which the bead notes is currently missing from the repo). Drive via factory: coder in /tmp worktree, full gate ladder (cold skeptic + codex secondo + /er + /advice). Existing PR #248 lifecycle is the template; same /af pipeline that handled PR #228 merge.

title: peak-RSS telemetry parses fragile docker stats text output — switch to --format '{{json .}}' or cgroup-native memory.peak
description: PR ez-gh-actions#55 (bead yz6b) added per-runner peak-RSS telemetry via shelling out to 'docker stats --no-stream' and regex-parsing its human-readable MemUsage field. Adversarial /advice-panel websearch validation found: (1) docker's own docs point automation consumers to --format Go-templates or the Docker Engine API, not the default table -- the human-readable format is not a stable parse contract; (2) documented moby/moby bugs in exactly this field include an observed MEM USAGE of '16EiB' (uint64::MAX overflow, moby/moby#42140), malformed Windows template output (#28301), and cross-version/cross-API semantic drift in what the number even measures (cache-inclusive vs exclusive, tmpfs handling: #35295, #10824, #40727, #40415); (3) point-in-time docker stats sampling can miss true peaks between polls -- cgroup-native memory.max_usage_in_bytes (v1) or memory.peak (kernel >=5.19, v2) give a genuine peak read with zero sampling-interval loss, at the cost of v1/v2 detection complexity and no Podman portability. NOT BLOCKING for PR #55: this is informational telemetry only (does not feed the fail-loud safety guard or the live docker-run --memory clamp), and the current parser already fails gracefully (Option-returning, no panic on malformed input) per independent codex-secondo verification. FIX (fast-follow, low priority): switch to 'docker stats --no-stream --format {{json .}}' (near-zero-cost, removes text-table fragility) as a first step; consider cgroup-direct reads if this telemetry is ever promoted from observational logging to feeding an actual scheduling/resize decision (e.g. the deferred VM-resize question on bead ah94 tier 3).

title: doctor-runner container-count gate flaps under ephemeral churn — consolidate onto the resampled per-slot inventory
description: Found 2026-07-09 21:20 after nj2j made the gate loud: verdict '[BAD] container-count gate FAILED (observed=15, expected>=16)' while the per-slot proof in the SAME run showed 0 down (16 slots accounted, some CYCLING). Live docker ps oscillated 16->15->16 within 15s — ephemeral runners finishing jobs and respawning under a 17-job queue burst. The count gate takes ONE docker ps sample; the per-slot gate already solves this exact transience with 2-sample DOWN persistence (b895). Two mechanisms measure the same thing and the weaker single-sample one vetoes the stronger. CONSOLIDATION FIX (not another threshold): derive the verdict's container-count check from the per-slot inventory totals (executing+idle_ok+cycling counts, which are already resample-hardened) and DELETE the independent docker-ps count sample from the verdict block. A slot counted CYCLING is a live slot, not a missing container. Keeps fail-loud message format from nj2j.

title: doctor-runner false-BAD: EXPECTED_CONTAINERS from grep -c '=' double-counts slot file sections; critical increment is silent
description: Found 2026-07-09 post-sa1t deploy: doctor-runner verdict said '[BAD] fleet unhealthy: 1 critical check(s) failed' on a PROVEN-healthy fleet (16/16 containers, 14 executing, all gates OK/WARN). Root cause chain: (1) line ~199 ASSIGNED=$(grep -c '=' slot_assignments.toml) counts EVERY '=' line; the file now has BOTH [assignments] AND [registered_at] sections (registered_at added during churn remediation), so ASSIGNED≈28 for a 16-slot fleet. (2) line ~929 EXPECTED_CONTAINERS defaults to ASSIGNED, so 16<28 trips the critical. (3) line ~930 increments CRITICAL with NO printed FAIL line — the verdict names a count but no check, violating fail-loudly. Likely contributed to earlier unexplained exit-1s attributed to transient DOWN/WARN. FIX: derive EXPECTED_CONTAINERS from config.toml runner.count (authoritative), fallback to counting lines within the [assignments] section only; AND make every CRITICAL increment print a named [FAIL] line (audit all ~10 increment sites at lines 921-966, several are silent).
close_reason: Fixed in ae62934 (pushed to origin/main): EXPECTED_CONTAINERS now derives from config.toml runner.count (authoritative), fallback is section-scoped [assignments] count via new count_assignments_section() helper, never a whole-file grep of slot_assignments.toml. All CRITICAL increments in the verdict block now print named bad() lines with observed-vs-expected values (service-state, colima, runners-online, container-count, ensure_count-loop, real-execution, canary, queue-tail, per-slot proof, gh_token freshness). Regression test added: tests/doctor_runner_expected_containers_test.sh (extracts real code via sed, not reimplementation). cargo test: 220 passed. bash -n + shellcheck clean.

title: fix(watchdog): reboot-stale-state guard for miss_count/last_restart
description: State files under $STATE_DIR persist across host reboots (plain files, not tmpfs) but OnBootSec=30s fires the watchdog almost immediately after boot. A stale miss_count>=threshold or a stale-but-recent last_restart from before a reboot can wrongly trigger/block restarts. Fix: unified mtime-freshness check on state-file reads (get_miss_count/get_last_restart), state older than EZGHA_WATCHDOG_STATE_FRESHNESS_SECONDS (default 600s) treated as absent. Also folds in /advice cold-review findings on PR#34 (check_linux host-mismatch guard symmetry, probe/ssh timeouts, --help sed range, --host arg validation).

title: P0 LIVE INCIDENT: single stuck slot starves entire fleet — next_slot() re-picks same failed slot in start_missing_runners retry loop
description:
ACTIVE INCIDENT, discovered during Lane 1 post-deploy churn watch, 2026-07-08 23:2x-23:3x PDT,
jeff-ubuntu, immediately after the Gate-0 deploy of PR#33+PR#34 (SHA 1a9baf4).

SYMPTOM: fleet collapsed from 16 -> 1 managed container within ~7 minutes post-deploy, slot
assignments file empty, and the daemon's ensure_count loop is retrying ONLY "ez-runner-c-2"
(90+ attempts in 60s, ~1/sec, ZERO attempts on any other slot number) with a hard 409
"Already exists" conflict against GitHub-registered runner id 141673 (status=offline, busy=true
-- a classic same-host zombie registration, likely orphaned by tonight's coordination collision
between two near-simultaneous `systemctl restart` invocations on this host ~10s apart, or by a
transient colima/docker-backend hiccup around the same window that killed several containers
mid-flight).

ROOT CAUSE (code-level, confirmed by reading src/docker_backend.rs):
- `start_missing_runners(cfg, backend, missing)` (line 1108) loops `for _ in 0..missing { ...
  start_one(cfg, backend) ... }` -- calling `start_one` blindly `missing` times with NO tracking
  of which specific slot numbers have already failed THIS call.
- `start_one` -> `next_slot(cfg)` (line 215) always allocates the LOWEST slot number (1..=count)
  not currently present as a key in slot_assignments.toml.
- When `start_one` fails (e.g. the 409 conflict here), the error path calls
  `release_slot_for(cfg, slot)` (line 257), which REMOVES that slot's key from the assignments
  file -- making it immediately available again.
- Net effect: if slot N is PERMANENTLY blocked (e.g. an unresolvable 409 zombie), every
  subsequent iteration of the SAME `for _ in 0..missing` loop calls `next_slot()` again, which
  re-picks the SAME just-released slot N (the lowest free number) instead of a DIFFERENT slot --
  so all `missing` iterations retry the one broken slot, and NONE of the other genuinely-fillable
  slots ever get attempted. One permanently-stuck slot poisons the entire respawn cycle, not just
  its own slot.

WHY THE ZOMBIE COULDN'T BE CLEARED QUICKLY: the daemon's own 409-collision handler in
`start_one`/`generate_jitconfig` explicitly refuses to delete a same-named runner it can't prove
is locally dead ("presumed to belong to a live sibling host and will not be deleted") -- correct
conservative default in general, but this instance genuinely was a same-host zombie. Manual
remediation attempted (sidekick-churn): `gh api -X POST .../jobs/{id}/cancel` (404, per-job
cancel not supported by the REST API), `gh api -X POST .../runs/{id}/cancel` (202 accepted but
the run/job status stayed "in_progress" and the runner stayed "busy=true" for 2+ minutes with no
sign of clearing -- self-hosted-runner jobs assigned to a now-silent runner do not cancel
promptly through the normal graceful path), `gh api -X DELETE .../runners/141673` (422, blocked
by the still-"busy" job, tried 3x over ~2min, still blocked as of incident escalation).

REQUIRED FIX (two parts):
1. IMMEDIATE/URGENT: `start_missing_runners` must track slot numbers it has already attempted
   AND FAILED within the current call, and exclude them from subsequent `next_slot()` calls in
   the same loop (e.g. pass an exclusion set into a `next_slot_excluding(cfg, &excluded)`
   variant, or have `start_missing_runners` call a lower-level start-at-specific-slot primitive
   directly once it knows which slots are free, rather than relying on `next_slot`'s blind
   lowest-free selection after every failure). This prevents ANY single permanently-stuck slot
   from starving the other 15.
2. FOLLOW-UP (not urgent): consider whether a slot that fails multiple consecutive ensure_count
   ticks in a row should be flagged/alerted distinctly (this repo already has
   `ensure_fail_streak`/`notify_ensure_failure` at the whole-ensure_count level in main.rs, but
   nothing per-slot) so a single stuck slot doesn't require someone to notice via a full fleet
   collapse before it's investigated.
