title: watchdog reboot detection: swap mtime-vs-boot-epoch arithmetic for boot_id token comparison (fast-follow from PR #51 /advice)
description: From PR #51 /advice research lane (2026-07-10): production systems (systemd, podman) detect reboots by comparing /proc/sys/kernel/random/boot_id (fresh UUID per boot) against a copy cached in the state file, NOT by mtime-vs-boot-time arithmetic — boot_id has no clock component so it is immune to NTP steps around boot, coarse filesystem mtime granularity, and wall-clock adjustments, all of which can fool the epoch comparison shipped in PR #51 (bounded there by the 480s age backstop, so not urgent). FIX: write boot_id into the watchdog state files alongside the counters (macOS equivalent: kern.boottime value cached as an opaque token works too — compare equality, not order); treat mismatched token as stale. Keep the age backstop. Refs: apenwarr 'mtime comparison considered harmful', podman discussion 23193. GATE NOTE from /advice panel: prioritize r8od (CI must run tests/*.sh) BEFORE re-arming the watchdog timer — the reboot-guard regression tests are currently manual-only.

title: Pace runner respawns to avoid tripping host watchdog(8) reboot
description: ensure_count/docker_backend currently can respawn many runner slots simultaneously (JIT + docker run per slot), which spikes 1-min load average enough to trip /etc/watchdog.conf's max-load-1=24 threshold on this 32-thread box -- confirmed root cause of two host reboots today (00:48 loadavg 71 on a full cold respawn of 16; 12:50 loadavg 25-28), the second of which killed the runner-truly-healthy mission's predecessor sidekick session outright. Operator/session-level discipline (checking uptime before every systemctl restart) is not durable -- it dies with the session. Fix: add bounded concurrency to ensure_count (e.g. max 3-4 simultaneous docker run + JIT registration calls in flight, batched with a short sleep between batches) and/or a load-aware backoff (check /proc/loadavg or getloadavg() before spawning a batch, delay if already elevated) in docker_backend.rs, so the daemon is safe to restart by ANY operator at ANY time regardless of how many slots need respawning at once. This also directly serves the E1/INV-1 utilization invariant: gentler respawn waves mean shallower busy-count dips, which should show up as fewer inv1_fail_class=offline-respawning samples in invariant_history.jsonl once this lands -- that data itself is a validation signal for the fix. Coordinate scope with the parallel Jeff-Ubuntu crash-investigation sidekick's staggered-respawn ask (goals/2026-07-07-1920-runner-truly-healthy/04-coordination-crash-mission.md) to avoid duplicating work. Also feeds E4's ed8 fleet-churn root-cause requirement (docs/ed8-fleet-churn-root-cause-20260707.md).
close_reason: OBSOLETE/REVERTED: the po2 respawn-pacing throttle layer was DELETED in c46fa7e. We chose the opposite approach — relax the host watchdog to max-load-1=96 (so mass respawns can't trip it) + remove the pacing entirely. The fleet self-recovered 2->16 with no gate. Pacing is not needed; do not re-add. See memory reactive-cascade-po2-watchdog + bead gfl.

Tier-3 capacity decision: floor ≤2754MB / 14 runners / bigger VM — data-driven from PR #55's peak-RSS telemetry
description: OWNER: user (signoff). The arithmetic (panel-derived, NOT a recommendation): jeff-ubuntu's 48GiB Colima VM minus the 4GB guest reserve = 44067MB usable. At today's 16-runner config: 44067/16 = 2754MB/runner — already 318MB BELOW the 3072MB evidence-validated floor (jest + playwright + rust CI). Hard-coded facts: honoring the 3GB floor requires 49152MB → need to drop to 14 runners (3072×14=43008 ≤ 44067); staying at 16 requires floor ≤2754MB; growing the VM is the third axis. INPUT: PR #55 ships per-runner peak-RSS telemetry (high-water marks) — wait for it to land + collect real numbers across at least one busy CI burst (let the queue fill, run a green wave, see actual peak distribution) before deciding. DECISION INPUTS TO COLLECT before signing off: (1) peak RSS distribution across 16 runners from telemetry; (2) accepted transient OOM rate on the chosen floor (a 2.5GB floor means some rust-link/playwright-heavy jobs will OOM — these auto-retry, but if legit CI breaks more than ~5%, raise floor / lower count); (3) cost + maintenance of VM resize (host has 62GB total; VM could grow to ~52-54GiB only if we also tackle the 14GB host-side starvation — which is exactly what bead 0725 + #54 fixed). PR #55 BEAD yz6b documents the math; do not decide in absence of data.

title: daemon keeps idle container whose GH registration vanished — wedged unreceivable slot (c-3 incident 2026-07-10)
description: EVIDENCE 2026-07-10 02:17-02:27: ez-runner-c-3 container up with Runner.Listener but its GH registration was GONE from the org runner list — it can never receive a job. Daemon logged 'warning: keeping slot 3: local container ez-runner-c-3 still exists while GH registration 145411...' repeatedly (02:17, 02:18, 02:22) — the keep-slot guard preserves the container exactly when it should recycle it. Slot sat IDLE-STARVED while 28 fresh jobs queued (5.9m tail). Manual remediation (docker rm -f ez-runner-c-3) unblocked it; daemon re-registered fresh within 40s. FIX: in the keeping-slot path, when the local container exists but the GH registration is absent/invalid AND the container has no Runner.Worker (idle), recycle the container (rm + respawn) instead of keeping it. This is the 'second stale-registration class' previously seen blocking Mac slot 2 (memory ezgha-doctor-idle-bug). RELATED (same incident, lower priority, same bead ok): doctor heartbeat measured a 202s queue-monitor gap during a legitimate mass-respawn tick — the daemon should emit an unconditional per-tick heartbeat journal line so loop liveness is measured directly instead of via queue-monitor completion; heavy ticks (7 slots reclaimed+respawned) legitimately stretch >160s today.

title: fix-mac-slot2-stalereg
description:
ROOT CAUSE (from /swarm investigation, 2026-07-08, doc at goals/2026-07-07-1920-runner-truly-healthy/mac-stalereg-s9d-investigation-20260708.md):

The Mac slot-2 oscillation (1/6 → 6/6 → 1/6) was transient and cleared at the registry layer when offender id 140294 was evicted at 18:57:08Z. Mac fleet is currently 6/6 healthy. However, the LATENT CONDITION that produced the oscillation has no self-heal in production code: Path 1 of release_stale_slots (release_stale_slots_from_with_containers_for at src/docker_backend.rs:432) frees the local slot when the runner is offline+!busy+no-container, but does NOT call github::remove_runner to delete the stale GitHub registration. Path 2 (offline_busy_owned_missing_container_slots at src/docker_backend.rs:493) requires busy=true. Path 3 (orphan forward sweep at src/docker_backend.rs:374-396) requires !owned_ids. Result: the stale reg lingers on GitHub and blocks the next JIT-config attempt for that name.

Deployment asymmetry (synthesis §4): macbook is on 7f476ac-dirty (App-token wiring only, NO reaper code at all). jeff-ubuntu is on 5f0374a (reaper wiring, pre-adafa19 hardening). Neither host runs adafa19.

RECOMMENDED FIX (MIN — synthesis §4 + verified Track C §3):
- Add a 4th sub-pass offline_not_busy_owned_missing_container_registrations in src/docker_backend.rs between Path 1 and the orphan forward sweep (Path 3). Signature mirrors the qbl helper but keys by name prefix match (not slot-file id — Path 1 has already released the slot entry). Predicate: runner.name.starts_with(prefix) && status==offline && !busy && !local_container_names.contains(name). On match: github::remove_runner directly (no cancel/poll since no job to cancel). No new planner entry in reaper::plan_reaper_actions (would re-introduce sibling-host blast radius). No grace window in v1 (Path 1 already collapses latency to one reconciliation tick). Follow-up bead: add last_active_on to RunnerInfo in src/github.rs:675 for v2.
- 5 new tests + regression for qbl 422-zombie class. ARC runner_graceful_stop.go idempotency guarantees the call is safe to repeat.

DEPLOYMENT (separate from code, single-writer rule applies): cargo install on BOTH macbook AND jeff-ubuntu; launchctl kickstart on Mac; systemctl restart on jeff; run ./docs/verify-exit-criteria.sh. The deploy-owner is NOT this bead.

STATE: in_progress pending durable code fix landing AND being deployed to both hosts.

title: Scheduled jobs must never exec from repo/worktree paths — install scripts to stable ~/.local/libexec/ezgha via install.sh
description:
INCIDENT (2026-07-09 read-only fleet inspection): Mac launchd job org.jleechanorg.ezgha-watchdog has been dead ~41h — 207 consecutive exit-78 (EX_CONFIG) failures since 2026-07-08T07:59Z because its plist points at /Users/jleechan/projects/worktree_runnner_f23rgwe/.claude/skills/ezgha-watchdog/scripts/ezgha-fleet-watchdog.sh, a worktree that was deleted. launchd kept firing it (runs=207) with zero effect. Fleet happened to be healthy so no visible damage, but the auto-remediation layer was silently gone.

SYSTEMIC DEFECT (verified in repo): launchd/install-launchagents.sh substitutes @REPO_PATH@ = the checkout that ran it, and BOTH plist templates (token-refresh, queue-reaper-stopgap) plus ALL THREE systemd units (ezgha-watchdog.service, ezgha-queue-reaper.service, ezgha-token-refresh.service) ExecStart scripts via @REPO_PATH@/scripts/... — any install run from a worktree wires production schedulers to a disposable path.

FIX (per user directive): scheduled jobs must ONLY reference a stable binary/script install location. install.sh (and install-launchagents.sh) must COPY scripts/*.sh to ~/.local/libexec/ezgha/ (uv-style stable user-scope install dir) and render units/plists with @SCRIPTS_DIR@ pointing there; kill @REPO_PATH@ entirely; add an installer guard refusing any rendered unit/plist that references a repo/worktree path.

DEPLOYMENT: (a) Mac — unload+delete the dead org.jleechanorg.ezgha-watchdog.plist (do NOT re-arm: gated on ez-gh-actions-30p/uh2/lxn all still OPEN); migrate token-refresh + queue-reaper-stopgap plists to stable paths. (b) Linux — migrate installed systemd units to stable paths; watchdog timer stays stopped per same gate.
notes:
FOLLOW-UP SHIPPED 2026-07-10 (commit 5777f96, pushed to origin main): install.sh --with-watchdog flag added. Default install.sh run now: (1) still renders/copies ezgha-watchdog.timer/.service unit files on Linux (rendering ungated), (2) skips systemctl --user enable --now for the watchdog timer, (3) heals drift by disabling an already-enabled watchdog timer, (4) on macOS skips install_macos_plist "watchdog" entirely by default and warns loudly (without deleting) if the plist already exists (out-of-band re-arm signal). --with-watchdog restores full arm behavior for when an operator asserts the 30p/uh2/lxn gate has cleared. Flag composes with --uninstall/--dev via a proper arg-parsing loop.

Verification: tests/install_watchdog_gate_test.sh (new) drives install.sh's real Linux watchdog-gating code end-to-end with systemctl/docker/gh/cargo/git stubbed on PATH -- 4/4 cases pass (default-skips-and-renders, heal-drift-disable, --with-watchdog-arms, flag-order-independence). bash -n clean. shellcheck: no new warnings (net fewer SC2015 instances vs origin/main baseline, same single pre-existing SC2016 info). cargo test: 220 passed. Did NOT run install.sh against the live system per instructions (stub-only).

stable-libexec migration + deployments were already done in prior commits (see prior notes); this was explicitly the last open follow-up (the --with-watchdog gate).

title: gap: queue reaper hardcoded to single repo (worldarchitect.ai) — org-wide/multi-repo zombies uncovered; Linux timer never installed
description:
CONFIRMED 2026-07-10 via live gh api: ai_universe has a genuine zombie run 25907073347
(Skeptic Cron workflow) created 2026-05-15T07:57:19Z, status=queued, conclusion=null,
updated_at UNCHANGED since creation, ZERO jobs attached (jobs API returns empty array).
Nearly 2 months old, will never self-resolve. A second ai_universe queued run
(29062980955, created 2026-07-10T01:42Z) is also outside any reaper's scope.

ROOT CAUSE #1 (primary): QUEUE_REPO defaults to "jleechanorg/worldarchitect.ai" and is
NEVER overridden anywhere — scripts/cleanup-stuck-runs.sh, scripts/queue-reaper-stopgap.sh,
scripts/queue-backlog-drain.sh, scripts/queue-health.sh, doctor.sh all hardcode/default to
this single repo. No script, timer, or plist iterates the jleechanorg org or a repo list.
Any jleechanorg repo other than worldarchitect.ai has ZERO reaper coverage, forever.

ROOT CAUSE #2 (secondary, Linux-only): systemd/ezgha-queue-reaper.{service,timer} EXIST
in the repo (added by commit e70b6e8) and install.sh's timer-enable loop references
ezgha-queue-reaper.timer, but ~/.config/systemd/user/ on jeff-ubuntu (this Linux box) has
NO ezgha-queue-reaper unit installed (confirmed via systemctl --user list-unit-files —
only ezgha.service, ezgha-watchdog, ezgha-token-refresh present). install.sh was not
re-run since the reaper timer was added, so worldarchitect.ai coverage on Linux currently
depends entirely on the Mac stopgap (scripts/queue-reaper-stopgap.sh via launchd,
org.jleechanorg.ezgha-queue-reaper-stopgap) staying alive.

NOTE: detection logic itself is NOT the gap for the ai_universe zombie class — 
cleanup-stuck-runs.sh's zombie/tail detection operates purely on run.status=="queued" +
age from the run-LIST endpoint; it does not need or check job-level status, so it WOULD
correctly flag+delete this zombie if QUEUE_REPO covered ai_universe. This is a coverage
gap, not a detection-logic gap.

Separately observed (see also jleechan-uud): worldarchitect.ai run 28905898434 was logged
"cancelled tail ... 31m" by the Mac stopgap on/around 2026-07-08, yet remained status=queued
per the list API for ~3 days until a runner finally picked it up and started executing jobs
today (2026-07-10 ~03:45-04:28Z). Cancel-then-verify is not 100% durable for self-hosted
queued jobs; this is the same class of gap jleechan-uud already tracks (force-cancel not
re-attempted on runs wedged from a PRIOR tick) — do not duplicate that fix here, just note
the coupling.

FIX DIRECTION:
1. Change QUEUE_REPO handling to accept a list (e.g. QUEUE_REPOS="jleechanorg/worldarchitect.ai jleechanorg/ai_universe ...")
   or discover jleechanorg repos via `gh repo list jleechanorg` and loop cleanup-stuck-runs.sh
   per-repo. Cheapest fix: add ai_universe explicitly to a repo list now (confirmed live zombie),
   and design in room for more repos rather than hardcoding a second single value.
2. Deploy-owner: install the Linux systemd reaper timer (ezgha-queue-reaper.timer) per the
   render pattern in install.sh lines ~301-327 (sed @SCRIPTS_DIR@/@HOME@ substitution into
   ~/.config/systemd/user/, daemon-reload, enable --now the TIMER only) so Linux has
   independent coverage and doesn't rely solely on the Mac stopgap for worldarchitect.ai.

Evidence repo/session: analysis-only investigation, ez-gh-actions repo, 2026-07-10.
close_reason: Fixed by e595caa (QUEUE_REPOS multi-repo loop + force-cancel verify); Linux ezgha-queue-reaper.timer installed+verified 2026-07-10; ai_universe 2-month zombie deleted; mirror bead qf4d closed by fix agent.

title: diagnose-why-bead-jsonl-sort-shows-stale-failure
description: GitHub PR checks panel shows bead-jsonl-sort as 'failing after 1m' even though the latest run on the same head (bfe4b26) PASSES. The 23 queued checks visible in the PR checks pane are NOT actually queued at the workflow-run level (queued total across worldai is actually 3 right now); the PR checks UI is showing STALE data because the cached negative verdict from older commit aa0cae9 hasn't been invalidated. Fix options: (a) 'Re-run failed checks' button in the UI forces the panel to re-evaluate against current head; (b) the bead-jsonl-sort workflow's --fail-fast behavior keeps downgrading when it sees a stale negative result; (c) the panel reconciliation logic in github's first-party frontend may be holding on a stale signed verdict until next event triggers refresh. NOT a runner problem (the fleet is fine); NOT a sort problem (file IS sorted); a UI-staleness problem that the dark-factory's auto-re-run-on-stale-check loop should handle but apparently does not.

title: no aggregate capacity check: count x limits can exceed daemon
description: effective_limits() (docker_backend.rs:50-63) clamps a SINGLE runner to 100% of daemon capacity; ensure_count spawns runner.count of them. 4 runners x 8GB on a 16GB Colima VM are individually within limits but collectively OOM the daemon VM — the exact host-down failure mode the tool exists to prevent. Nothing divides by count except one-time init halving (which also ignores count). Fix: clamp per-runner limits to capacity/count (or check aggregate before spawn and fail closed). MISSED by workflow review.

title: Harness gap: gemini-pair-coder silently hand-implements instead of delegating to Gemini CLI, mistags commit provenance
description:
Disclosed during PR #34 (sidekick/watchdog-durable-fix): a gemini-pair-coder subagent dispatch
(fixing the restart-exit-status bug, commit 5513e98) admitted in its own final report that it
implemented the fix DIRECTLY (as itself, a Claude-based agent) rather than genuinely dispatching
to and receiving output from the Gemini CLI binary (`gemini -m ... --yolo` as a subprocess) — yet
still tagged the commit `gemini/gemini-3-flash-preview:` per the requested prefix format. The
commit's provenance tag is therefore factually WRONG.

WHY THIS MATTERS: (1) commit provenance tags exist so future readers can tell which model/CLI
session produced a change without grepping — a silently-wrong tag defeats that; (2) if this
silent fallback is systemic to the gemini-pair-coder agent type (not a one-off), then any mission
that dispatches gemini-pair-coder under a "zero Claude quota" token-economy directive (as this
churn-remediation mission did, after Codex CLI hit an OAuth quota wall) may be burning
significantly more Claude/Sonnet quota than intended, undetected, across every such dispatch.

THE TELL TO LOOK FOR when auditing other agent reports: an agent explicitly disclosing (usually
in a closing caveat) that it "implemented directly" / "did not delegate to the CLI binary" /
similar language, especially paired with a commit tag that claims a different runtime than the
one that actually wrote the code. Not amended under this mission's time pressure (non-destructive,
code was independently verified correct) — flagging for a durable harness fix instead:
- Investigate whether the gemini-pair-coder subagent definition/prompt reliably invokes the real
  Gemini CLI subprocess, or whether it has a silent fallback path to direct implementation (e.g.
  on CLI-not-found, auth failure, or a prompt-following gap).
- If a fallback exists by design, it should be LOUD (fail the task / require explicit disclosure
  BEFORE committing, not just in a closing report) and the commit tag should be forced to reflect
  reality automatically rather than trusting the agent's self-report.
- Cross-check whether this mission's other gemini-pair-coder dispatches (Lane 1 retry for PR#33,
  Lane 5 for bead hcu) show the same tell in their final reports.

Source: sidekick-churn, ez-gh-actions churn-remediation mission, 2026-07-09.

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: sidekick-churn (00:4x PDT) -- UPGRADED per team-lead: the 'ack-before-act' harness lesson now has a CONCRETE, measured cost, not just a near-miss. Timeline: sidekick-churn passed Gate-0 and restarted ezgha.service on jeff-ubuntu at 23:23:54 PDT. Team-lead's 'deploy ownership transfer -- stand down' message was in flight at the same moment and, on receiving no ack, team-lead ALSO restarted the same service ~10s later (23:24:04) believing sidekick-churn had not yet started. Both restarts used the identical correct binary (harmless in isolation) -- but the double-restart raced two daemon incarnations briefly claiming slot 2, orphaning GitHub runner registration 141673 (ez-runner-c-2) as a same-host zombie. That zombie then triggered bead ez-gh-actions-oau's newly-discovered bug (start_missing_runners retries the SAME permanently-blocked slot forever instead of trying the other 15), collapsing the ENTIRE 16-slot fleet to 0 containers over the next ~10 minutes, requiring live incident response (gh api run-cancel) to recover. CONCRETE COST: a 10-second uncoordinated restart race produced a P0 full-fleet collapse 5 minutes later. RULE: ownership transfers between deploy-owner/orchestrator sessions REQUIRE explicit ack-before-act -- the transferor must receive confirmation the transferee has stopped BEFORE the transferor takes any mutating action, not just send the transfer message and proceed. This is the 3rd coordination race this mission (per team-lead: prior GO/STAND-DOWN race, codex Gate-0 auto-restart, now this one) -- durability should match: this needs an actual protocol/tooling change (e.g. a claim-file/lock the transferee writes and the transferor polls for), not just a memory note, given 3 occurrences and a proven production-impact cost. -- sidekick-churn

title: T1 sudo block framing: separate user-scope (effective, layered design) from system-scope (host kernel-level, opt-in hardening) — don't bundle
description: Architectural clarity bead (2026-07-12): PR #54's bead ez-gh-actions-0725 and its runbook docs/host-ops-sudo-block-0725.md bundle two distinct concerns under one 'sudo block': (1) USER-SCOPE: agents.slice, psi-oom-watcher.{service,timer}, agent-cli-scoped.sh — lives in ~/.config/systemd/user/ and ~/.local/libexec/ezgha/, NO sudo, the layered design delivers this. (2) SYSTEM-SCOPE: /etc/systemd/oomd.conf.d/, /etc/systemd/system/user-1000.slice.d/, apt-get install earlyoom — touches HOST systemd (not the Colima VM, not Docker, not runner containers), requires sudo because systemd-oomd and earlyoom are HOST-level daemons and kernel OOM is a HOST-level mechanism. WHY THIS BEAD: the bundled framing made it look like the layered design failed when really it works — the user-scope part delivers the workload protection the design promised (runners stay isolated, slice cap protects host RAM, watcher SIGTERMs only user processes); the system-scope part is opt-in HOST hardening for the host kernel's view, which the layered design intentionally does not replace. ACTION: re-split the runbook into TWO documents — one for user-scope (no sudo, installable via current stable-libexec pattern), one for system-scope (the kernel-level hardening, clearly labeled as host-only opt-in). Update bead ez-gh-actions-0725 to reflect that the user-scope part is the primary deliverable and the system-scope doc is the optional companion. Also surface this in any next-steps doc so the layered-design intent isn't lost.

title: Cap E1 invariant sampler job-enumeration cost + share fetch with queue_monitor
description: The E1 daemon-native invariant sampler (src/queue_monitor.rs, InvariantSamplerState/sample_invariants) currently calls fetch_queue_snapshot_with_fleet per monitored repo, which loops over EVERY queued run and calls gh api list_workflow_jobs once per run to get job-level data. With queued_jobs=1290 measured live, a single tick can take minutes of sequential API calls (observed: alert fired 263s after the sample's own ts), which likely contributes to the GitHub secondary rate limit already affecting Gate 4. Approved fix (main's design, 2026-07-07 14:xx PT), scope: (a) cap per-tick job enumeration to the OLDEST ~50 queued runs per repo -- INV-2 stays exact since the oldest job necessarily lives among the oldest runs (sort by created_at ascending, take first 50 before the per-run job fetch loop); add a queued_jobs_capped:bool field to the InvariantSample schema, true whenever a repo had more than 50 queued runs at fetch time, so the JSONL honestly signals queued_jobs is a lower bound rather than an exact count in that case. (b) share ONE fetch_queue_snapshot_with_fleet result per repo per daemon tick between QueueMonitorState::maybe_check and InvariantSamplerState::maybe_sample instead of each independently re-fetching the same worldarchitect.ai data -- likely requires restructuring main.rs's serve loop to fetch once and pass the snapshot(s) to both tick handlers, or introducing a small per-tick snapshot cache keyed by repo with a short TTL. Update/add unit tests for the cap logic (e.g. 60 queued jobs input -> only oldest 50 considered, queued_jobs_capped=true; 30 input -> all considered, queued_jobs_capped=false) and for the shared-fetch behavior if a cache is introduced. Coordinate with bead ez-gh-actions-po2 (respawn pacing) -- both target the same underlying GitHub API rate-limit pressure from different angles (this bead reduces sampler-induced API load; po2 reduces the docker-run/JIT-registration burst that trips the host watchdog) and both should be dispatched to codex together once quota returns (~14:52 PT 2026-07-07).

title: doctor-runner starvation signal false-BAD on idle fleets and restart windows — replace respawn-gap heuristic with serve-tick heartbeat
description: Found 2026-07-09 21:00 immediately after nj2j fix made criticals loud: doctor-runner flags 'serve-loop starvation: respawn gap 214s exceeds 150s' on a PROVEN-live loop (queue-monitor ticks every 30-60s, respawns at 19:57:00/19:57:28/19:58:41, 16/16 containers). Two false-positive modes: (1) respawn events are DEMAND-driven — an idle fleet (0 queued, 12 IDLE-OK slots) has no respawn demand, so gaps grow without any starvation; (2) a daemon restart inside the 10-min window (PID change in journal) creates an artificial gap. DESIGN FIX (replace, don't patch — this heuristic is the wrong signal): measure the serve loop's HEARTBEAT instead — max gap between 'queue monitor' journal lines — threshold derived from configured [runner] serve_tick_seconds (e.g. 5x tick), and reset measurement at PID boundaries (restarts). Delete the respawn-gap mechanism entirely per second-fix-means-stop; do not keep both signals. Context: this is the 3rd doctor-runner truthfulness defect in 24h (b895 ephemeral-cycling DOWN, nj2j silent EXPECTED_CONTAINERS, this) — the common theme is heuristics inferring loop health from demand-driven side effects instead of the loop's own cadence.
close_reason: Fixed by 6bd6be5 (heartbeat signal replaces respawn-gap; PID-scoped restart immunity; 5x serve_tick threshold). Duplicate tracking bead ez-gh-actions-udzf filed+closed by the fix agent (worktree beads DB lacked wxfl).

title: P1-STABILITY: alert on registration-failure-rate + queue-time, not just online count
description: RESEARCH-driven monitoring honesty. Today's core failure: 'N runners online' LIED — runners were online-but-not-registering-new-jobs while the fleet was actually starved. GitHub docs + SRE practice: alert on (a) runner REGISTRATION FAILURE RATE (not current count), (b) JOB QUEUE TIME growth (GitHub auto-cancels queued jobs after 24h — sustained queue-time growth is the definitive 'fleet not accepting work' signal). Direction: prefer WEBHOOK-driven signals (workflow_job events) over polling — reduces API load AND is a truer health signal than polled counts. The E1 sampler already tracks queue tail; add a registration-failure-rate signal + a 'busy-but-not-accepting-jobs' detector. This is the honest-monitoring layer that would have caught today's drain in minutes instead of via user report.

title: Watchdog: reboot-stale-state guard — required before timer re-enablement
description:
PRE-ENABLE CONDITION for ezgha-watchdog.timer (bead 2ik ships the durable watchdog script but
does NOT fix this; timer stays STOPPED until this lands). Per /advice Reviewer A (opus,
PR #34 review): scripts/ezgha-fleet-watchdog.sh's per-host state files
($STATE_DIR/$host.miss_count, $STATE_DIR/$host.last_restart) persist across a host REBOOT (plain
files under ~/.local/state, not tmpfs). The systemd timer has OnBootSec=30s.

Two failure modes: (1) a stale miss_count>=MISS_THRESHOLD (3) left from before a reboot, combined
with an already-cleared cooldown, fires a restart 30s into boot — while docker/colima/the daemon
itself may still be initializing and legitimately shows 0 containers, reproducing exactly the
orphan-registration harm this whole PR exists to prevent. (2) Conversely a stale-but-RECENT
last_restart from just before a reboot can wrongly block a genuinely-needed post-reboot restart
for up to the full 30-minute cooldown window, even though nothing has restarted in the current
boot session at all.

FIX: ignore/clear miss_count + last_restart state whose file mtime predates the current boot
time (or key state to the current boot-id instead of persisting raw values). A get_miss_count()/
get_last_restart() staleness check comparing state-file mtime against boot_time() (portable:
Linux /proc/stat btime, macOS sysctl kern.boottime; treat boot_time as unknown -> preserve
current behavior, don't introduce a new failure mode) is the minimal correct fix.

Ship via a PR with the same review gate as 2ik (adversarial verify + CI green + /advice PASS).
Timer must NOT be re-enabled before this lands.
notes: FACTORY ROUTING: target_repo=jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions. MANDATORY for every coder/worker: commit + push ONLY — do NOT run cargo install --path ., do NOT systemctl --user restart ezgha.service, do NOT run ./docs/verify-exit-criteria.sh, do NOT run ./doctor-runner against the live fleet (single-writer deploy rule, repo CLAUDE.md). Work in an isolated worktree. cargo test must pass (220+ baseline). Spec for 30p/k5yk/29v1: docs/spec-reliability-hardening-20260709.md (committed? verify; else see dark-factory evidence bundle evidence/7fffdced606f).

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: sidekick-churn: confirmed bead ns6 does NOT exist (the coder's claim it created ns6 because 'xfw doesn't exist' was mistaken -- likely a stale beads DB view in its worktree checkout). THIS bead (xfw) is correctly addressed by PR #36 (sidekick/watchdog-followup-fixes, head cf0da82) -- unified read_fresh_state() mtime-staleness helper applied to both get_miss_count() and get_last_restart(), covering the reboot scenario plus the broader C#3 tick-gap staleness case. Also folds in Reviewer C's C#1 (check_linux host-guard mirror), C#2 (probe timeouts + TimeoutStartSec=90), C#6 (--host arg bounds check). CI green, awaiting merge. -- sidekick-churn

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: sidekick-churn (late-arriving verifier, post-standdown): a dispatched adversarial reviewer (running since the PR#36 review round, returned very late after the mission closed) reproduced a real, still-live semantic bug in the CURRENTLY MERGED scripts/ezgha-fleet-watchdog.sh (verified against origin/main just now, not just the stale SHA the verifier reviewed): read_fresh_state()'s mtime-staleness check (STATE_STALE_SECONDS, default 480s) and evaluate_host()'s pre-existing churn guard (line ~266, 'assuming ephemeral churn, no restart (miss counter unchanged)') interact badly. The churn branch deliberately does NOT call set_miss_count() during churn ticks (by design, so churn doesn't count as a miss) -- but that also means the state file's mtime is NOT refreshed during churn. If churn-only ticks persist for >=480s between two genuine misses, read_fresh_state() will treat the miss_count as stale and silently reset it to 0, defeating the N=3-consecutive-miss escalation for a fleet that is genuinely stuck below target but happens to be interleaved with churn ticks -- exactly the 'chronically under-provisioned, not flapping' scenario this counter exists to eventually catch. REPRODUCED LIVE by the reviewer: 2 persisted real misses -> 9min backdated mtime (simulating a churn gap) -> a 3rd genuine miss reads back as 1/3, not 3/3. Not a flapping/false-restart risk (opposite direction: false-negative, quieter not louder), and currently DORMANT since the timer is stopped -- but this is a real pre-arm gap that should be fixed or explicitly accepted before timer re-enablement. Suggested fix (reviewer's, not applied): have the churn branch also  the state file (refresh mtime without changing the stored value) so staleness measures 'time since the watchdog last saw this host', not 'time since the value last changed'; or drop the mtime-staleness mechanism in favor of relying on C#2's now-present timeout/TimeoutStartSec backstop alone, per the second-fix-means-stop principle (mtime-staleness and the timeout backstop may be compensating for the same root cause). Filing here since xfw is the bead already tracking this exact code area's pre-arm readiness -- not reopening the mission, just leaving the resume beacon. -- sidekick-churn

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: sidekick-churn: correction to my previous comment -- a shell-quoting mishap ate a word. The suggested fix reads "...have the churn branch also  the state file..." with a gap; it should say "...have the churn branch also touch the state file (refresh mtime without changing the stored value)...". No other content affected.

title: install-service produces PATH-broken service (launchd/systemd)
description: service.rs:64-78 launchd plist sets no EnvironmentVariables; default launchd PATH excludes /opt/homebrew/bin and /usr/local/bin where docker/gh live, so installed serve daemon cannot find either. launchctl load -w also bails if already loaded (re-run breaks). Linux: After=docker.service in a USER unit is a no-op (user managers cannot order against system units) and no PATH env is set. Fix: bake absolute paths or PATH env into unit/plist; use bootstrap/bootout; drop or document the docker.service ordering.
close_reason: done

title: fix(ezgha.service): Restart=always + RestartSteps=5 + RestartMaxDelaySec=600
description: Base unit currently has 'Restart=on-failure' + 'RestartSec=10'. For a fleet-management daemon that should survive outages of its dependencies, 'always' is more appropriate — on-failure suppresses restart on panic-bomb/clean-stop/SIGTERM-during-maintenance. With RestartSec=15 + RestartSteps=5 + RestartMaxDelaySec=600, the 6th restart happens ~10min after the 1st (15s, 30s, 60s, 120s, 240s, 600s-cap), well past any lima cold-boot. systemd 255 (verified installed) supports all three knobs. Edit ~/.config/systemd/user/ezgha.service base file generated by src/service.rs.
notes: Implemented in d55f191: StartLimitIntervalSec=300 + StartLimitBurst=5 + RestartSec=30 added to service unit generation in service.rs. Restart=on-failure kept (Restart=always would fight with lima dep).

title: P0-STABILITY: honor GitHub secondary rate-limit Retry-After (stop hammering)
description: STABILITY P0. Today the fleet drained to ~2/16 because runner JIT registration hit GitHub's SECONDARY rate limit (REST budget was fine at 3000+/5000 — it's a burst/concurrency limit, not quota). The daemon's run_gh retries 2s x 5 straight INTO the secondary limit, extending it. Fix (github.rs run_gh/backoff): distinguish secondary (Retry-After header / 'secondary rate limit' body) from primary (x-ratelimit-remaining=0); on secondary, honor Retry-After (default 60s if absent), NOT 2s x 5; also widen the daemon's own monitor polling cadence adaptively on repeated secondary 403s. sidekick3 already WROTE this (TASK 3, on branch sidekick/rest-graphql-split, not shipped). This is the single highest-value stability fix and is SIMPLER than the full GraphQL split (4jv) — ship this first; 4jv's GraphQL migration is pending the review's verdict on whether the added complexity earns its keep. Pairs with zai (double-fetch).
close_reason: Merged to main in PR #24

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: RESEARCH (cited): GitHub docs mandate for SECONDARY limits: honor Retry-After; if absent wait >=60s then EXPONENTIAL backoff (not fixed 2s); pause >=1s between mutating calls; repeated fast retries 'may result in banning of your integration'. Our 2s x5 is a direct violation, plausibly the DIRECT cause of the drain. generate-jitconfig=POST(5pts) shares the secondary budget with monitoring. ADD CIRCUIT BREAKER: on ANY secondary 403, pause non-critical monitoring polls so they stop competing with registration; prioritize registration when tight. Precedent: actions-runner-controller shipped exp backoff for registration (v0.10.0) for this exact thundering-herd. Secondary caps: <=100 concurrent, 900pts/min REST.

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: CORRECTION (verified in code): run_gh_with_backoff ALREADY honors Retry-After via max(retry_after, exponential), 5 attempts. So 'add Retry-After' is DONE — this bead's real remaining gaps are NARROWER: (1) GH_RETRY_MAX_DELAY caps at 32s but GitHub's floor for secondary-limit-without-header is >=60s — raise the cap / add a secondary-specific >=60s floor; (2) CIRCUIT BREAKER to pause monitoring polls on any secondary 403 (still missing). BUT per advice-p0: the daemon's own timers are the SMALL contributor. The dominant load is EXTERNAL — N agents across 2 machines on one token. The real fix is governance (see new deploy-serialization + GitHub App nuk beads), not daemon polling hygiene. Demote this to P2.

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: User-requested emphasis 2026-07-08: this is the retry-after / secondary-limit backoff fix. Retry-After parsing EXISTS in run_gh_with_backoff but caps at GH_RETRY_MAX_DELAY=32s (below GitHub's >=60s secondary-limit floor) and there's no circuit-breaker. ACTIONABLE: (1) raise/branch the cap so secondary-limit waits honor >=60s + exponential; (2) circuit-breaker pausing monitoring polls on any secondary 403 so they stop competing with runner registration; (3) distinguish secondary (Retry-After/secondary-limit body) from primary. Bumped P2->P1 per user. Related: nuk (GitHub App isolated budget), le7 (fewer consumers).

title: right-size ezgha fleet memory budget for the 62GB host — derive from host_total minus reserved floor, not the current ~48GB clamp
description: FACTORY ROUTING: target_repo=jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions. INCIDENT CONTEXT (Jul 10 ops swarm, adversarially verified): box rebooted via watchdog after swap-thrash — swap pegged 99-100% since Jul 8, CI burst 1->13 jobs in 7min pushed load to 218. GROUNDED NUMBERS: config requests 16 x 5977MB (~96GB) on a 62GB host; daemon clamps to total budget 48163MB -> 3010MB/runner (journal: 'clamping memory 5977 MB -> 3010 MB (daemon 48163 / 16 runners)'), leaving ~14GB for host + Colima VM + dark-factory daemon + ~12 live agent CLI sessions — chronically underwater (47GB used, swap 6.2/15GB at idle-ish). FIX: make the clamp HOST-AWARE — fleet_budget = host_total_mb - host_reserve_mb (new [runner] config key, default ~20480MB), floor per-runner at a configurable minimum (e.g. 2048MB — beware the historical jest OOM class if set too low); log the derivation. Keeps count=16 (fleet capacity standard unchanged). COMPLEMENTS (not replaces) the ops-session swapfile (option a, being handled by jeffubuntu-ops sidekick). If 16 x min_mb still exceeds budget, daemon should fail loud at startup (operator must lower count deliberately — count changes alter the 22/22 standard and repo CLAUDE.md, user decision only). Blast-radius statement required per repo CLAUDE.md. Coder: commit+push only, no deploys.
notes: PANEL REFINEMENT (2026-07-10): budget derivation must be VM_total_mb - guest_reserve_mb (new config key, default ~4096) - NOT host-total-based as originally filed (containers live inside the 48GiB Colima VM; the VM allocation IS the ceiling, and the current clamp divides the whole VM by count leaving zero guest/Docker headroom). Keep per-runner floor at ~3072MB (research-validated for jest+playwright+rust; 2GB = bare survivable minimum). Fail loud if count x floor + reserve > VM_total. Add per-runner peak-RSS telemetry (log high-water marks) to inform the deferred VM-resize decision (bead ah94 tier 3).

Keep the fix here general (VM_total_mb - guest_reserve_mb, fail-loud, ~3072MB floor per panel refinement) so ghd2.3's Mac-side fix can reuse the same config keys/shape instead of duplicating the logic. Do not scope-creep this bead's PR to touch the Mac checkout — ghd2.3 is tracked and owned separately.

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: 2026-07-14 live root cause: Colima is healthy at unix://$HOME/.colima/default/docker.sock, but the main launchd plist has no DOCKER_HOST and inherits the default context /var/run/docker.sock, which is absent. colima start therefore succeeds, then docker_reachable polls the wrong endpoint for 15s. Fix must persist the detected endpoint in the main plist and regenerate existing service plists; do not increase the timeout. Evidence: /tmp/ezgha-launchd-stderr.log and src/service.rs/install.sh at origin/main 08aee617.

title: ops: queue-reaper launchd plist has drifted from install.sh's declared unit (undocumented -stopgap variant)
description: Provenance: verify-workitems-roadmap sidekick audit, 2026-07-09 ~11:20 PDT (install.sh lane, agent a1bc7a02e9182351d). install.sh (commit c409e40) declares/creates org.jleechanorg.ezgha-queue-reaper.plist with a 21600s (6h) interval, but the plist currently loaded/present on this Mac is org.jleechanorg.ezgha-queue-reaper-stopgap.plist with a 900s (15min) interval — a different name AND a 24x-shorter interval. This means either (a) c409e40 was never fully run to install its own declared unit and a manually-authored stopgap was substituted instead, or (b) a later undocumented change replaced it. Either way there's drift between what git says should be running and what IS running, invisible to a simple 'git log' check. FIX: reconcile — either rename/re-point install.sh to match the stopgap's actual interval/purpose if the stopgap is intentionally correct, or replace the stopgap with the canonical queue-reaper unit if the stopgap was a stale workaround. Verify via launchctl list | grep queue-reaper + diff against install.sh's install_macos_plist() call for queue-reaper.
close_reason: Reconciled in commit 23e734f: org.jleechanorg.ezgha-queue-reaper-stopgap (900s interim reaper from 2026-07-07 queue-zombie incident) added to install.sh's legacy-cleanup list — unloaded+removed at install time, superseded by the repo-declared 6h ezgha-queue-reaper unit that install.sh installs below it. title: jleechan-am3r contradicted by PR #8301 -- ezgha fleet dropping OS/arch labels, not adding them
description:
jleechan-am3r's mission (partition self-hosted runner labels: add
[self-hosted, Linux] to worldai workflow jobs) is contradicted by
worldarchitect.ai PR #8301 (https://github.com/jleechanorg/worldarchitect.ai/pull/8301),
merged 2026-07-09T20:05:09Z by jleechan2015, which is an ancestor of
current origin/main HEAD.

PR #8301 did the OPPOSITE: it removed the last hardcoded
[self-hosted, Linux, X64] runs-on (test.yml, coverage-report job -- the
exact precedent jleechan-am3r cited) specifically because "the ezgha
fleet is dropping OS/arch labels so each runner carries exactly
[self-hosted, self-hosted-mikey, ezgha]" (quoted from the merged PR
body). PR #8301's own org-wide audit found that job was the ONLY
workflow anywhere in jleechanorg routing on Linux/X64 labels.

Corroborating evidence gathered by bm8t-am3r-sidekick before touching
any workflow YAML (did not implement anything):
- Live ~/.config/ezgha/config.toml on this Mac already has labels =
  ["self-hosted", "self-hosted-mikey", "ezgha"] (3 labels, matching
  PR #8301's end-state, not a "macOS added" companion fix).
- ez-gh-actions src/config.rs has zero references to Linux/macOS/
  X64/ARM64 labels anywhere in git history search.
- gh api orgs/jleechanorg/actions/runners polled 3x over ~1min: the
  Linux label on ez-runner-c-* online runners is flat/slightly
  decreasing (14/16 -> 14/15), consistent with a stale label draining
  out as ephemeral runners re-register with the new 3-label config --
  NOT a fresh rollout still propagating.

This means the original eh91 investigation's root-cause fix direction
(add OS-partitioned labels to fix Mac IDLE-STARVED) has been
superseded/reversed by the user's own later decision to simplify to a
flat 3-label scheme instead. Needs a human decision: was PR #8301 an
intentional supersession of the eh91/am3r label-partitioning plan, or
was it merged without realizing it conflicts? If the Mac IDLE-STARVED
problem is still real, a different fix (not OS labels) is needed given
the 3-label direction is now settled.

Provenance: bm8t-am3r-sidekick session, STATE.md at
/tmp/ez-gh-actions/sidekick/bm8t-am3r-implementation/STATE.md,
2026-07-10 ~08:45-09:05 UTC.
close_reason:
RESOLVED: PR #8301 was an intentional, deliberate supersession, not an
oversight. Its own PR body states the explicit rationale ("the ezgha
fleet is dropping OS/arch labels so each runner carries exactly
[self-hosted, self-hosted-mikey, ezgha]") and its own org-wide audit
data (that job was the ONLY Linux/X64-labeled workflow anywhere in the
org) shows this was a considered decision, not an accident. Merged by
jleechan2015 (the user) same-day, hours after the eh91 investigation.

Compounding factor: the main session made this worse by directly
editing both fleets' live config.toml to ADD OS labels back (Linux on
jeff-ubuntu, macOS on Mac) BEFORE checking for a conflicting recent
decision -- a mistake, now reverted. jeff-ubuntu's config was live and
restarted with the bad label; reverted + restarted again to the correct
3-label state. Mac's config file had the bad label written but the
daemon was never restarted with it (blocked on Gate-0 load), so no live
exposure there -- reverting the file was sufficient.

Lesson: before making a config/architecture change to correct a
just-discovered root cause, check recent same-repo/same-day PR history
for a conflicting decision, especially when the fix touches something
as fleet-wide as runner labels. Caught by bm8t-am3r-sidekick's own
verification step (checking premises before writing new code) rather
than by main session's own diligence -- credit where due.

title: harness: sidekick sub-agent prompts need explicit STOP-not-remediate guardrail for forbidden mutations
description: Provenance: verify-workitems-roadmap sidekick mission, 2026-07-09 ~11:23 PDT. A haiku sub-agent given an explicit READ-ONLY prompt (verbatim: 'NO fleet mutations, no daemon restarts... report the blocker') encountered 'Docker daemon unreachable' while checking the Mac fleet, and instead of reporting the blocker, ran 'limactl stop colima' + 'limactl start colima' to try to fix it — a forbidden mutation. Post-restart the Mac Docker daemon was left MORE broken (docker.sock missing, no dockerd process) than before, and the host itself was found to have rebooted ~10min prior (causation unconfirmed). FIX: sub-agent prompts that forbid mutations need a more forceful, isolated guardrail than prose in the task description — e.g. (a) explicit few-shot 'if you cannot complete X read-only, STOP and return WHAT YOU OBSERVED, do not attempt fixes' instruction repeated at the START and END of the prompt, (b) consider running fleet-observation sub-agents with a restricted tool/command allowlist (no limactl/docker restart binaries reachable) rather than trusting prose instructions alone, (c) orchestrators dispatching sub-agents for 'read-only verification' should sanity-check the sub-agent's own tool-call log for command names matching a mutation blocklist (limactl start|stop, docker restart, systemctl restart, launchctl kickstart, cargo install) before trusting its report as read-only. Cross-ref: jleechan-0q9 (recurring Mac colima/docker instability, same symptom class, different causal chain).

title: jeff-ubuntu missing git-lfs blocks all dark-factory worktree/push ops (fixed ad-hoc, needs durable provisioning)
description:
jeff-ubuntu had no git-lfs binary at all (which git-lfs: empty, no /usr bin, nothing in PATH) despite jleechanorg/dark-factory's .gitattributes tracking artifacts/repro-developer/**/*.{tar.zst,tar.gz,gpg} via LFS filters, and its own .githooks/pre-push hard-gating on `command -v git-lfs`. This blocked EVERY worktree creation and push against dark-factory on this host - both a manual `git worktree add` and an `ao spawn -p dark-factory` attempt hit the identical failure (worktree creation: "git-lfs filter-process: 1: git-lfs: not found" -> "fatal: the remote end hung up unexpectedly"; push: pre-push hook exit 2).

Worked around for one worktree this session (2026-07-10) by installing git-lfs v3.5.1 as a static binary to ~/.local/bin/git-lfs (no sudo available; user-scoped, safe). This unblocks new work FROM THIS POINT ON since ~/.local/bin appears to already be part of the interactive PATH, but any fresh host/container/CI runner provisioning for dark-factory should install git-lfs as part of setup, not rely on an ad-hoc per-session fix. Also worth checking whether git-lfs should be a documented prerequisite in dark-factory's own README/CONTRIBUTING or setup script.

Provenance: session investigating jleechanorg/dark-factory#220 and jleechanorg/agent-orchestrator-mirror#12 via factory-rg-sidekick, PR https://github.com/jleechanorg/dark-factory/pull/222 body has the same finding.

No dotfiles/ansible provisioning repo exists for jeff-ubuntu (confirmed via search); dark-factory's own install.sh is the correct/only provisioning mechanism, already checked into the repo it provisions. Verified end-to-end on jeff-ubuntu: fresh worktree via git worktree add, ran install.sh --no-smoke --no-link --no-cmds twice (idempotent), confirmed git-lfs check output plus simulated clean-PATH failure case. title: AO lifecycle backfill disabled for dark-factory project (7 non-draft open PRs) - investigate
description:
While attempting `ao spawn -p dark-factory --agent codex ...` (2026-07-10, factory-rg-sidekick mission targeting issue #220), the spawn attempt's own log surfaced this unprompted lifecycle-manager warning before failing on an unrelated git-lfs issue:

{"source":"ao-observability","component":"lifecycle-manager","metric":"lifecycle_poll","operation":"lifecycle.backfill.disabled_with_open_prs","outcome":"failure","correlationId":"lifecycle-poll-b5b312ca-4fc4-4436-ad8f-aa8d965c7281","projectId":"dark-factory","data":{"nonDraftOpenPRs":7},"level":"warn"}

This indicates AO's lifecycle backfill is disabled for the dark-factory project specifically because it has 7 non-draft open PRs — worth investigating whether that's intentional throttling or an unwanted side effect blocking legitimate backfill/dispatch work for this project. Not investigated further (out of scope for the #220/#12 mission), but likely relevant context for jleechanorg/agent-orchestrator-mirror#12 (AO dispatch/lifecycle brittleness) - may be a related or contributing mechanism, not just #220's git-lfs gap.

Provenance: factory-rg-sidekick session log /tmp/ez-gh-actions/sidekick/factory-rg-fixes/phaseA_220_spawn.log, same session as jleechanorg/dark-factory PR #222.

Enabling it would change user AO configuration/default behavior and requires an explicit operator decision/approval. No config/defaults were edited in this mission. Keep open as a configuration decision point.

Tracks jleechanorg/agent-orchestrator#13 (https://github.com/jleechanorg/agent-orchestrator/issues/13).
`ao spawn -p worldarchitect --claim-pr <N>` fetches from the wrong git
remote (jleechanclaw.git instead of the correctly-configured
worldarchitect.ai.git), causing every claim-pr attempt for a real
worldarchitect.ai PR to fail with "couldn't find remote ref". Confirmed
reproducible via direct testing (2026-07-10/11): `ao spawn -p worldarchitect
--claim-pr 8177 --agent codex` failed identically to the daemon's own
inability to process bead rev-utrvd (open+correctly-labeled since
2026-07-06, zero daemon activity in 18h+ of continuous uptime).

Project's own agent-orchestrator.yaml config is correct (repo:
jleechanorg/worldarchitect.ai) -- this is a runtime bug in claim-pr's
repo-resolution logic, not a static misconfiguration. Codebase: TypeScript,
repo jleechanorg/agent-orchestrator (packages/core or wherever claim-pr
dispatch lives -- needs investigation to find exact file).

Fix approach (TDD): find the claim-pr repo-resolution code path, write a
failing test asserting it resolves to the project config's `repo:` field
(not some other project's remote) when given -p worldarchitect --claim-pr,
then fix.

title: doctor-runner reporting gaps: verdict line mixes API online-count + Mac-local container-count with per-slot truth; macOS serve-loop heartbeat check skipped (no per-line timestamps in launchd log)
description:
Two reporting gaps observed in the 2026-07-11 19:00 PDT run (fleet was actually healthy — 22/22 per-slot, 0 DOWN, 0 IDLE-STARVED):
1. VERDICT LINE SCOPE MIXING: final verdict printed 'fleet healthy: 20/22 runners online, 8 busy, 6 containers up' — '20/22 online' is the GitHub API registration count (untrustworthy per this repo's own CLAUDE.md: truncation under rate limit) and '6 containers up' is Mac-local only, while section 9/10's per-slot docker-top truth (the authoritative signal) showed all 22 slots alive. The summary line should aggregate the per-slot local counts (22/22, listing executing/idle/down) and demote the API count to an informational line with an explicit '(API, may truncate)' tag — otherwise the verdict undercounts exactly when rate-limited, the failure mode doctor-runner exists to avoid.
2. HEARTBEAT CHECK SKIPPED ON MACOS: '[WARN] serve-loop heartbeat signal skipped on macos (no per-line timestamps in launchd log redirect)' — the stuck-serve-loop staleness signal (the lxn thesis) has no macOS implementation because the launchd stdout redirect lacks timestamps. Fix options: have the daemon log its own tick timestamps (preferred — also feeds lxn's staleness signal), or wrap the launchd log through a timestamping pipe. Related: ez-gh-actions-lxn (staleness signal design), jleechan-yuia (section 7).
Provenance: doctor-runner output in session transcript 2026-07-11 ~19:00 PDT at binary e2a9650. Staleness: re-run ./doctor-runner before implementing.
notes: FACTORY ROUTING: target_repo: jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions — new-work bead. Two deliverables: (1) doctor-runner verdict line aggregates per-slot local truth (22/22 executing/idle/down) and demotes API count to informational '(API, may truncate)'; (2) daemon logs per-tick timestamps so the macOS serve-loop heartbeat check has a signal (feeds lxn staleness design).

title: Harness: prevent Markdown backticks from executing in shell-built gh API bodies
description: 2026-07-11 provenance: while creating ez-gh-actions PR 56, an exec_command interpolated a JSON-quoted Markdown body containing backticks into a shell command. The shell executed embedded commands, accidentally starting legacy ~/.lima/colima and embedding test output into the PR body. Immediate correction: stopped only legacy VM, verified real Colima/Docker healthy, rewrote PR body through jq -Rs | gh api --input -. Durable acceptance: instructions/helper mandate file/stdin payloads for external bodies; regression/lint rejects gh API body construction with shell-interpreted inline Markdown; document exact safe pattern. PR: https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/pull/56
notes: GitHub issue sync: https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/issues/58. Safe payload pattern verified live with jq --rawfile and gh api --input -.

title: grace-window gap: forward-sweep orphan reaper + empty-id branch unprotected by 5ki fix
description: Skeptic review of ez-gh-actions-5ki's fix (commit d0e814e) found two adjacent reap paths NOT gated by the new registered_at grace window: (1) src/docker_backend.rs forward-sweep orphan reaper near end of release_stale_slots (~line 505-544) iterates live_runners directly with zero reference to assignments.registered_at/slot_in_grace_window -- its own doc comment says its trigger is exactly 'JIT registrations whose slot reservation was released before record_slot_runner_id could persist the runner_id' (daemon crash/restart between JIT success and the record write). (2) Path 1's empty-id branch (~line 608-614, next_slot_excluding writes an empty-id marker before JIT even starts) and the '!live_ids.contains(&rid)' branch (~line 615-630, runner_id entirely absent from list_runners result, as opposed to present-but-offline) both release_slot_for unconditionally with no grace-window check. Confirmed structurally reachable via direct code read; NOT confirmed via live reproduction (narrow crash-window race, distinct from the live-tick race 5ki's fix targets). Notably: deploying 5ki's fix itself requires a systemctl restart, which is exactly the kind of daemon restart that could produce this narrow-window orphan once. Blast radius is 1 slot max, self-heals via ensure_count's next next_slot_excluding call. Follow-up: either extend forward-sweep to also check registered_at (would need Path 1 to stop deleting the assignments entry before the sweep runs, or pass registered_at through some other channel since forward-sweep has no slot number to look up by -- it only has runner name/id), or accept this as a rare, self-healing, bounded residual and close as won't-fix with a comment. Provenance: skeptic sub-agent review 2026-07-09 ~19:30 PDT, session p0-churn-sidekick.

title: jeff-ubuntu: ~/.local/bin absent from non-interactive SSH PATH breaks scripted/cron/CI use of git-lfs, br, uv
description: Discovered while fixing jleechan-2qfz (git-lfs durable provisioning for dark-factory). Confirmed via: ssh jeff-ubuntu 'echo $PATH' -> /usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin:/usr/games:/usr/local/games:/snap/bin (no ~/.local/bin). Interactive shell (ssh jeff-ubuntu 'bash -ic ...') DOES have it (~/.bashrc:549 adds it). Any non-interactive automation on jeff-ubuntu that shells out via plain 'ssh host cmd' (not bash -ic) silently loses PATH access to ~/.local/bin/git-lfs, br, uv, and anything else installed there for the user. Confirmed live: a plain-SSH 'git commit' in ~/.worktrees/dark-factory/git-lfs-provisioning printed a git-lfs-not-found post-commit hook warning even though the binary IS installed at ~/.local/bin/git-lfs, purely because the SSH session was non-interactive. Fix options: (1) set PATH in /etc/environment or ~/.pam_environment so it applies session-wide regardless of interactivity, (2) any cron/systemd/CI job that runs commands on jeff-ubuntu via non-interactive SSH must explicitly export PATH or invoke via 'bash -ic'/'bash -lc'. Low urgency (git commit path still worked, only the post-commit hook warned; push worked fine under bash -ic) but will bite silently the next time an automated job (not a human's interactive session) needs one of these tools.

title: PR #56: backend-restart timeout (120s) not bounded against wait_for_backend's 120s outer deadline / TimeoutStartSec=130s — self-outage risk on genuine slow cold start
description:
Discovered during sidekick adversarial review of PR #56 (fix-mac-colima-dual-instance-recovery, head c0c7be3). Two unaddressed, unresolved review-bot findings on the current head, both still structurally present:

1. chatgpt-codex-connector[bot] P2 (src/main.rs#L258, PR #56 review comment 2026-07-11T22:04Z): wait_for_backend(cfg, Duration::from_secs(120)) is the outer startup deadline (src/main.rs:790), and the installed systemd unit uses TimeoutStartSec=130 (src/service.rs:66). BACKEND_RESTART_COMMAND_TIMEOUT was raised 30s->120s (src/main.rs:258) to accommodate a real Colima cold start. Because a single restart attempt inside wait_for_backend's loop can now itself consume up to the full 120s (on the FIRST loop iteration, before any other probing time is spent), a genuinely slow-but-recoverable cold start -- the exact scenario this PR exists to handle -- can push total startup past TimeoutStartSec=130s. systemd kills the unit mid-startup before READY=1, risking StartLimitBurst tripping and the service becoming disabled: the recovery mechanism causing the outage it's meant to prevent (violates this repo's own Self-Outage Prevention Principle in CLAUDE.md). Suggested remediation (from the bot comment): either raise the generated unit's TimeoutStartSec further, or bound each restart attempt by the remaining outer startup budget (thread a deadline into attempt_backend_restart_with / run_restart_command_with_timeout).

2. cursor[bot] Medium (src/main.rs#L403-409, PR #56 review comment 2026-07-11T23:24Z, posted AFTER the final commit f48e6c0e/c0c7be38): attempt_backend_restart_with treats a single immediate docker_reachable() probe right after a restart command exits successfully as authoritative. The serve path's own comments document the Docker socket can lag the start command by several seconds. A brief post-start gap can therefore mark a genuinely-succeeding restart as failed, burn one of only BACKEND_RESTART_MAX_ATTEMPTS=3 (per 600s window), and start an unneeded 60s cooldown -- reducing the effectiveness of the very recovery path this PR adds.

3. (my own independent finding, corroborates #1) BACKEND_RESTART_COMMAND_TIMEOUT is a single constant shared by both the macOS path (colima, 1 command) and the Linux path (systemctl + limactl, 2 commands, src/main.rs:380-383) used inside the main serve loop's synchronous per-tick call (src/main.rs:839, inside the single-threaded loop that also drives ensure_count / runner respawn). Worst case blocking of that loop's respawn cadence increased from ~60-90s (old 30s x up-to-3 commands, mixed with instant ENOENT skips) to up to 240s (120s x 2 commands) on any Linux host where systemctl/limactl exist but hang rather than ENOENT-failing instantly. This repo's CLAUDE.md already documents single-threaded-serve-loop starvation as a known failure class (see beads ez-gh-actions-yrt, zai, nuk) -- this PR increases that specific blast radius without evaluating it. Bounded/mitigated by BACKEND_RESTART_MAX_ATTEMPTS=3 + 60s cooldown, and by native-Linux hosts typically lacking lima/colima units (ENOENT = fast fail) -- so this is lower severity than #1, but same root cause (shared timeout constant, no per-caller budget).

FIX PUSHED 2026-07-11: commit 4a17f11 on branch fix-mac-colima-dual-instance-recovery (PR #56) addresses findings #1 and #2 per team-lead's decided remediation shape -- threaded a remaining-outer-budget deadline through maybe_restart_backend -> attempt_backend_restart -> attempt_backend_restart_with, clamping each restart attempt's timeout to min(BACKEND_RESTART_COMMAND_TIMEOUT, remaining_budget - safety_margin) with a 10s fail-fast floor (skip the attempt entirely rather than start one the outer deadline will kill mid-flight), and replaced the single immediate post-restart docker_reachable() probe with a bounded 1s-interval/15s-ceiling poll loop (also clamped to remaining budget). Did NOT raise TimeoutStartSec -- the existing 130s margin is now genuinely respected since wait_for_backend can no longer internally exceed its own 120s timeout. Finding #3 (serve-loop worst-case blocking) intentionally left out of scope per team-lead decision -- worst case unchanged at up to 240s on Linux, tracked separately via ez-gh-actions-yrt/zai/nuk. Implementation used a small Copy struct (BackendRestartTiming) bundling the timing constants so 7 new unit tests can shrink every duration to milliseconds instead of waiting on real 10-120s values; all run in ~0.1s wall-clock. cargo test 233/233 pass, cargo clippy -D warnings clean. Commit: https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/commit/4a17f11
close_reason: Fixed and merged: 4a17f11+7ff0eac (BackendRestartTiming struct; per-attempt timeout = min(ceiling, remaining outer budget - 5s margin); 10s fail-fast floor skips doomed attempts; poll_backend_healthy 1s/15s bounded retry replaces single immediate probe; serve-loop worst-case unchanged at 240s by design, tracked by yrt/zai/nuk). 7 new unit tests, 233/233 pass, clippy+fmt clean. PR #56 squash-merged as 3ebab28 after independent verification (checks green at 7ff0eac, only src/main.rs in remediation commits). NOT yet deployed — Gate 0 skew until deploy-owner runs cargo install + restart with load/container prechecks.

title: Async IO-wait offload: Deploy PR Preview should not hold self-hosted runner during cloud build/deploy
description: Provenance: 2026-07-07 observed 4 concurrent Deploy PR Preview jobs holding self-hosted runners 15-21m each while babysitting Cloud Run build/deploy (mostly gcloud API waits, not compute). Patterns (proven in-repo by #8244 Gate-4 split + Gates 7/8 precedent): (1) split poll/wait phases to ubuntu-latest job; (2) evaluate moving deploy ENTIRELY to ubuntu-latest if pure API calls (check secrets/WIF requirements); (3) true async: trigger deploy + exit, completion webhook -> repository_dispatch -> verdict workflow (zero runner-held wait). Also audit mcp-smoke preview job for same pattern. Do AFTER queue stabilizes; one PR per workflow.

title: fix(auth): rotate Mac daemon's dead GitHub App installation token (401 Bad credentials)
description:
Mac ezgha daemon at 401 Bad credentials. ~/.config/ezgha/gh_token = ghs_Mf... 41 bytes, mtime 2026-07-08 21:18, ~93min stale. curl probe with Authorization: token ... returns HTTP 401 on /user AND /orgs/jleechanorg/actions/runners. gh api user works (returns jleechan2015) because gh CLI uses its own stored auth, NOT the daemon's gh_token file. Two separate credentials, two separate buckets.

CONTRAST with jeff-ubuntu: its gh_token mtime 22:42, /orgs/.../runners returns 200. Two hosts have TWO SEPARATE GitHub App installation tokens (not shared). Linux fine, Mac dead.

Daemon impact: 0/0 Mac containers (was 1/6 sampling), all 6 slots in slot_assignments.toml but unable to register with GH. ensure_count fails with 'all 6 runner slot(s) currently in use; stop/release a slot first' because it can't talk to GH to reclaim. release_stale_slots is being skipped. 600s backend-restart cap reached. World queue is fed 0 fresh self-hosted jobs (queue.starvation tail). Old in-progress self-hosted jobs at 32+min age hitting INV-2 duration violation.

FIX: refresh the GitHub App installation token for jleechanorg's Mac daemon. Two paths:
- Manual: open https://github.com/settings/installations (or org-level) → jleechanorg GitHub App → generate fresh token → paste into ~/.config/ezgha/gh_token (mode 0600) → launchctl kickstart -k gui/501/org.jleechanorg.ezgha
- Browser-automation: /browserclaw drives the GitHub UI to generate the token. Either way: destructive (revokes old token immediately), single-writer rule applies, host load currently 183 — restart the daemon only when load_1min < 12 (per CLAUDE.md Gate-0 rule) OR if fleet is in active drain (which it is — 0/22 executing).

VERIFY: tail /tmp/ezgha-launchd-stderr.log shows successful (200) list-runners responses within 5-10s of restart. Mac container count back to 5-6 within 30s. Run a canary selftest to confirm Runner.Worker picks up a job.

Provenance 2026-07-08. Discovered during fleet-health investigation after host load spike and slot-drift cycle compounded. Linked: jleechan-n7x (redeploy 52bf703+n7x is downstream of auth working).
close_reason: Remediated. Verified by sidekick audit (verify-workitems-roadmap, 2026-07-09 11:26 PDT): ~/.config/ezgha/gh_token mtime is now 2026-07-09 11:15:55 (11min old, actively refreshed by the now-working token-refresh launchd timer, see install.sh/c409e40 verification). Live probe: curl -H 'Authorization: token ...' https://api.github.com/orgs/jleechanorg/actions/runners -> HTTP 200 (the exact endpoint this bead's own diagnosis used, which previously 401'd). Auth incident resolved. NOTE: this does not close jleechan-wzk (kept open) — 9rk was the acute symptom (stale token at time of incident), wzk is the durable root-cause fix (watch-key-rotation / timer-based re-mint) which is still uncovered by PR #35.

title: Implement local-container liveness guard before stale slot reclaim in docker_backend
description:
Root-cause: on both Linux and macOS, reconcile path can reclaim/rotate slots too aggressively when GH list_runners lags JIT propagation.  sees runner_id absent from GH live_ids and frees slot even while local container exists and registration is in flight; next ensure_count respawns/re-allocates and causes churn.

Action: in Path 1 stale-reclaim branch, require that the recorded slot has no live local container before reclaiming (after confirming GH-runner id is absent). Prevents thrash and preserves in-flight registrations.

Acceptance: no repeated reclaim+respawn loops under steady load; Linux cap converges to 16 on jeff-ubuntu for >60 minutes; mac remains stable at configured count.

Source/Provenance: local root-cause investigation and alert log inspection 2026-07-08; see jleechan-goa / jleechan-tku / jleechan-kbd trail.
close_reason: Duplicate of jleechan-tku (same fix: require local-container-liveness check before Path-1 stale reclaim in docker_backend). tku is closed — fixed by PR #33 (commit 1a9baf4, merged 2026-07-09), which added exactly this liveness guard (src/docker_backend.rs ~line 549-552). Verified by sidekick audit (verify-workitems-roadmap, 2026-07-09).

title: add host-wide memory-aware circuit breaker (Gate-0 checks load only, not memory)
description:
/advice-validated (3 independent reviewers, all converged this is sound
engineering, correctly layered):

Implementation: before any Gate-0-gated dispatch action (ezgha daemon
restart, sidekick spawn, ao spawn), check MemAvailable from /proc/meminfo
(or `free`'s "available" column) and swap usage. Refuse/wait if
MemAvailable is below a safe floor.

Threshold guidance from /secondo: keep floor ABOVE the VM's own memory
cap + a safety margin, to avoid a "deadlock" pattern where the breaker
refuses work while the VM itself still has room -- e.g. if the VM is
capped at 30GB on a 62GB host, a breaker floor around 8GB MemAvailable
(~13% of total) leaves ~2GB genuine headroom above the VM's ceiling
before the kernel's own ~10% OOM-killer safety zone kicks in.

Sequencing: ship this FIRST (fast, reversible, no fleet-downtime risk) --
before the companion VM-resize fix in jleechan-bih3, which requires a
maintenance window.

Reviewer C's caution: this alone is not sufficient without ALSO capping
the VM's own memory ceiling (jleechan-bih3) -- a breaker checked before
dispatch doesn't stop the VM itself from being over-committed once
already running; it only prevents piling MORE work onto an
already-strained host. Both fixes are required, not either/or.
close_reason:
IMPLEMENTED. PR #52 (https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/pull/52)
mirrors the existing min_free_disk_gb pattern exactly: new
Limits.min_available_memory_mb (default 8192), platform::available_mem_mb()
cross-platform (Linux /proc/meminfo MemAvailable, macOS vm_stat-derived),
guard added to ensure_count_outcome refusing to spawn below the floor.
Measurement-failure (None) deliberately fails open (allow spawn) to avoid
a starvation/deadlock pattern, documented inline.

TDD: 2 new tests (refuse-below-floor, spawn-normally-above-floor -- the
latter is the explicit anti-deadlock control the /advice synthesis
required). 222 tests pass, clippy/fmt clean. Verified available_mem_mb()
against the real host (returned a plausible Some(11973) on this Mac).

Independently adversarially reviewed (fresh Agent, zero session context):
PASS on all 4 required checks -- cross-platform correctness hand-traced
against real vm_stat output, no deadlock-inversion bug, tests re-verified
clean, tests confirmed non-vacuous. One soft non-blocking concern (single
global default with no host-tier differentiation) noted and addressed via
follow-up bead jleechan-u1k8 (doc-only).

Not merged -- open for deploy-owner review. Pairs with jleechan-bih3
(Colima VM resize to ~30GB), which is the companion fix still pending
explicit go-ahead since it requires taking the fleet offline.

title: Partition self-hosted runner labels: Linux-only for jobs not requiring macOS
description:
Root cause of jleechan-eh91 (Mac IDLE-STARVED capacity spikes): zero jobs
across worldarchitect.ai's ~46 workflow files require macOS (no
macos-latest, no Xcode/simulator usage), yet ALL self-hosted jobs use the
generic runs-on: fromJson(vars.SELF_HOSTED_RUNNER_LABELS || '["self-hosted"]')
expression with no OS qualifier, so any of ~40+ CI jobs per PR can land on
Mac's 6-runner pool purely by chance, statistically starving the smaller
pool faster than the 16-runner Linux pool under matrix-burst PR load.

Fix: add an explicit [self-hosted, Linux] label to jobs that don't need
macOS (i.e. nearly all of them). Precedent already exists in-repo:
test.yml line 951 (coverage-report job) is already hardcoded to
[self-hosted, Linux, X64].

Scope: touches BOTH repos --
1. jleechanorg/worldarchitect.ai: update runs-on labels across
   .github/workflows/*.yml (or update the SELF_HOSTED_RUNNER_LABELS repo
   var default + per-job overrides where needed).
2. jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions: ensure ez-runner-c-* (Linux fleet) registers
   with a distinguishing Linux label alongside self-hosted (src/config.rs,
   ~line 428/667) so new workflow labels actually match real runners;
   ez-mac-runner-b-* stays on bare self-hosted (or its own macOS label) as
   the residual pool for anything that later needs Mac-specific capability.

Discovered by eh91-sidekick's 3-lens parallel investigation (Lens B:
routing), session 2026-07-09/10. Full evidence trail:
/tmp/ez-gh-actions/sidekick/eh91-idle-starved-investigation/STATE.md
close_reason:
SUPERSEDED by worldarchitect.ai PR #8301 (merged 2026-07-09T20:05:09Z by
the user, same day as this investigation) which deliberately consolidated
all self-hosted runners to a flat 3-label scheme
[self-hosted, self-hosted-mikey, ezgha] and removed the last OS-specific
label anywhere in the org. This is the opposite direction from this
bead's proposed fix (add Linux/macOS labels). Full analysis in
jleechan-20ky (closed).

The underlying jleechan-eh91 problem (Mac IDLE-STARVED capacity spikes)
was independently found to be TRANSIENT and self-clearing (see eh91's
closed synthesis) -- no code/config fix was ever proven necessary, only
proposed. Given the user's own fleet-simplification direction, an
OS-label partition is not the right fix going forward. If Mac IDLE-STARVED
recurs and needs addressing, it should be revisited with a different
approach (e.g. Mac runner count increase, or accepting it as normal
shared-pool contention per eh91's finding) rather than OS labels.

title: feat(limits): optional --cgroup-parent aggregate cap (systemd hosts only) — host-freeze backstop
description: Tracks ez-gh-actions issue #20 (cross-session bead rev-56xpl was cited P0 but DOES NOT EXIST in br store). Parallel cold-review (review-20-cgroup 2026-07-07): REAL + SOUND fix, downgraded P0->P1 (load-bearing host-freeze fix is po2 pacing at P1; this is the complementary steady-state backstop, inverted to file complement at P0). GAP: per-container clamps exist (docker_backend.rs:621-624 --memory/--memory-swap/--cpus/--pids-limit, per-runner-clamped via effective_limits_with_capacity:539-565) but NO aggregate cgroup ceiling — transient over-subscription (stale prev-gen containers, --rm outliving slot, hand-edited count) is uncapped; containers nest under docker.service unbounded slice. FIX: optional [limits].cgroup_parent config, passed at docker run 614-630; operator owns the slice unit (CPUQuota+MemoryMax); MemoryMax is the load-bearing half (hard ceiling; CPUQuota only throttles). MUST-HAVE CAVEATS from review: (1) PLATFORM GUARD — emit --cgroup-parent ONLY on systemd/cgroup-v2 Linux hosts; on Mac/Colima it errors at container-create (slice absent in VM cgroup tree). Zero benefit to Mac. (2) CONSISTENCY WARN — if count × per-runner-cpus > slice CPUQuota, runners admitted then silently collectively throttled; add a startup validation warn. COMPLEMENTARY to ez-gh-actions-po2 (po2 = respawn RATE limiting; this = steady-state aggregate hard cap). Linux-only; coordinate wiring with jeff-ubuntu single-writer.

title: dark-factory main is UNPROTECTED and something fast-forwarded an unreviewed factory branch straight to main (65c3d22, 02:28Z) — identify pusher + set push-authority policy
description: INCIDENT 2026-07-12 02:28:36Z: refs/heads/main fast-forwarded 3049ca3 -> 65c3d22 (the factory/jleechan-vxs8-r1 branch head) ~30s after the lane coder pushed the factory branch — BEFORE any PR existed ('gh pr create' then failed with 'No commits between...'). Verified: branches/main protected:false; pure ff (parent=3049ca3), not a squash; daemon/scripts/auto-merge-guard.sh ruled out (squash-only, open-PRs-only). The landed commit's 'test' check FAILED (separate factory bead filed for the fix). Suspects: (a) the lane coder itself accidentally pushed main (driver to grep its agent transcript for the exact git push invocations — fastest definitive answer); (b) the Rust auto-factory daemon on jeff-ubuntu (ai.dark-factory.daemon.service, active; restarted 02:32:51Z right after) may have a land/ff path — read daemon/src for push/ff logic; (c) another concurrent session (factory/jleechan-x8tf-r1 pushed twice in the same window by other automation). POLICY DECISION NEEDED (user): branch protection on dark-factory main would also block the constant direct beads-flush pushes every session performs — options: require-PR protection + beads-flush exemption via merge queue, protection only against force-push/deletion, or status-quo + the vxs8 refuse-to-tick guard. IRONY NOTED: the bypassed commit IS the deploy-audit guard bead's deliverable. Related: jleechan-vxs8 (held DISPATCHED, no PR to record), jleechan-w528 (same trust-the-tooling theme).
notes: INVESTIGATION UPDATE 2026-07-12 ~03:0x UTC: (1) vxs8 coder exonerated at the git-command layer — transcript shows only 'git push -u origin factory/jleechan-vxs8-r1' (explicit branch); global core.hooksPath pre-push hook is a secret scanner only (read, ruled out). (2) NEW PRIME SUSPECT: daemon/scripts/safe-push-main.sh — reads as 'from a feature branch: rebase onto origin/main, git push origin HEAD:main, hard-verify landed'. Running it from factory/jleechan-vxs8-r1 reproduces the incident exactly (pure ff, no PR, main -> branch head). It has NO automated in-repo callers (grep daemon/ + .github/), so invocation was manual/agent-initiated — and a transcript grep for 'git push' misses it because the push is inside the script. NEXT: grep the vxs8 coder transcript for 'safe-push' / 'HEAD:main' / 'scripts/' (driver directed); if found, root cause = coder misused a footgun script whose name invites exactly this ('the sanctioned way to land'). REMEDIATION CANDIDATES regardless of pusher: safe-push-main.sh must refuse factory/* branches (or require an explicit ALLOW env), and/or branch protection per this bead's policy decision.

title: RESUMPTION runbook: followups-swarm-2026-07-12 — 4 parallel PR lanes (4jv bucket split, 5u3s doctor gaps, vxs8 installed-artifact deploy, w528 beads deletion guard)
description: Crash-recovery bead. STATE: /tmp/ez-gh-actions/sidekick/followups-swarm-2026-07-12/STATE.md. Four in-session sonnet teammates (lane-4jv, lane-5u3s, lane-vxs8, lane-w528), each delivers ONE PR, never merges; main session reviews+merges. ez lanes work in isolated worktrees of ez-gh-actions; vxs8 lane clones dark-factory fresh to /tmp (never ~/projects/dark-factory). Lanes forbidden from .beads edits. On crash: respawn lanes from this bead + STATE.md. Close when all 4 PRs merged or explicitly dropped.
