Beads factory-labeled with per-repo routing: 4jv, 5u3s, w528 (ez-gh-actions), vxs8 (dark-factory), plus new drive-existing-pr bead for PR #52 reconcile. PR #62 closed as duplicate of merged 9c7l fix. Sidekick-af-driver runs /af ticks from a fresh dark-factory clone at main (post-#248 multi-repo code), never the ~/projects/dark-factory tree. apye (ghd2.6 convergence) deliberately NOT factory-labeled — needs drained maintenance window.

title: Colima VM memory allocation (49GB/62GB=79%) is the root cause of jeff-ubuntu OOM crash
description:
/advice-validated plan (3 independent reviewers: Opus subagent, web research,
multi-model /secondo -- all converged):

1. Target VM size: ~30-32GB (not 24GB without measurement -- consensus,
   roughly "host RAM / 2"). Cutting more aggressively risks moving the OOM
   INSIDE the VM (Runner.Worker gets killed mid-job -> mysterious fleet
   flakiness) instead of a clean host-level event.
2. Companion fix required, not optional: per-container memory limits
   INSIDE the VM for anything the VM hosts beyond ezgha's own runners
   (ezgha already does this for its own managed containers via its
   existing clamp logic -- extend the same discipline, don't rely on VM
   sizing alone).
3. Sequencing: pair with jleechan-aeh0 (host memory circuit breaker) --
   ship that first since it's a safe, reversible code change with no
   fleet-downtime risk, THEN do this VM resize as a deliberate low-traffic
   maintenance window (requires colima stop/start, takes the 16-runner
   Linux fleet offline for the duration).
4. Verify actual VM-internal peak memory usage (`colima ssh -- free -g`,
   `docker stats` across all containers) BEFORE finalizing the exact
   number within the 28-32GB range -- do not guess a single value without
   this check.

Command: `colima stop && colima start --memory 30` (or measured value in
28-32GB range). Verify post-resize: `colima ssh -- sudo cat /proc/meminfo`
shows ~30GB, fleet returns to 16/16 containers, `free -h` on host shows
>=30GB available with swap near 0 under normal load.

title: grace-window skip-reap log lines should report actual elapsed seconds, not just window size
description:
The grace-window skip-reap log lines (Path 1's pre-existing one and the
Path 4 line added by PR #45, jleechan-1dnx) report only the fixed
REGISTRATION_GRACE_WINDOW size (e.g. "within 60s grace window"), not the
actual elapsed seconds since registered_at. This makes it harder to tell
from logs alone how close a given registration was to falling out of the
grace window. Minor, non-blocking: add the actual elapsed-seconds value
to both log lines (src/docker_backend.rs, release_stale_slots).

Discovered by the adversarial-review subagent for PR #45 during
verification of jleechan-1dnx, session 2026-07-09/10. Original bead
(jleechan-6jpa) was lost in a .beads/issues.jsonl merge conflict during
concurrent pushes and is being refiled with a fresh id.
close_reason: PR #47 opened: jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions#47 — added seconds_since_registered helper, both grace-window skip-reap log lines now include actual elapsed seconds

title: systemd WatchdogSec=60 kills serve during ensure_count batch (Linux flapping root cause)
description: Provenance: journalctl jeff-ubuntu 2026-07-07 — ezgha.service: Watchdog timeout (limit 1min)! every ~90s; SIGABRT; restart counter climbing. src/main.rs:320-332 pings WATCHDOG=1 only AFTER ensure_count returns; batch spawn of 4+ runners (JIT gh + docker run) routinely exceeds 60s. Deploying install-service (jleechan-1kl) turned stable 16/16 into 13-16 flapping. Fix: ping watchdog at loop start AND/OR WatchdogSec>=180 AND/OR ping during long ensure_count. Immediate ops: disable or widen WatchdogSec on Linux unit until code fix.
close_reason: done

title: Remove legacy ~/.local/share/worldarchitect-runners mirror (1.0 GB)
description: Provenance: Genesis Coder fleet assessment 2026-07-07. du -sh ~/.local/share/worldarchitect-runners → 1.0G of old org-runner-* data; ezgha no longer uses this path (configs in ~/.config/ezgha). Safe cleanup: rm -rf ~/.local/share/worldarchitect-runners. Related bead jleechan-4if (worldarchitect.ai repo scripts) but this is local Mac runtime mirror.

title: retrospective: 48h runner-problem mining (both hosts) -> failure taxonomy + earliest-detection metric per class
description: P3: swarm-mine the last 48h (2026-07-07T12:00 PDT onward): Mac /tmp/ezgha-launchd-stderr.log + stdout, jeff-ubuntu journalctl --user -u ezgha.service, ~/.local/state/ezgha/alerts.jsonl on BOTH hosts, 'last reboot' both hosts, bead timeline (kbd/tku/wzk/9rk/2a8/0ox/mw9), git log. Known incidents to anchor: slot-drift flap (empty-id), Path-1 reclaim race, key-rotation 401 fleet-dark, colima hang + host reboot 11:13, watchdog exit 78, queue-reaper stopgap drift, load-280 host pressure. OUTPUT: failure-class taxonomy, per class: {frequency, MTTR, which existing metric caught it (if any), which NEW metric would have caught it earlier}. Feeds P4 wiring + existing bead ez-gh-actions-x9n (alert on registration-failure-rate + queue-time). Provenance: user directive 2026-07-09.
close_reason:
Shipped in commit e1c2201 (pushed to origin/main): docs/retro-48h-runner-problems-20260709.md. Swarm-mined via 3 parallel read-only agents (Mac launchd logs + alerts.jsonl, jeff-ubuntu journalctl over ssh, br/git bead timeline), synthesized into 7 failure classes each with {frequency, MTTR, existing metric that caught it, earlier-detection metric candidate}: (1) token staleness/401 -- fixed, wzk closed; (2) API rate-limit/403 -- candidate for r83a (daemon-side push alert); (3) slot-drift/JIT races -- LARGEST class, 5307 respawns over ~11.5h, underlying bug fixed by PR #33 but no doctor metric caught it live -- top r83a candidate (per-slot state-transition log); (4) backend-restart misfire on native-docker Linux hosts -- NEW bug found + filed (jleechan-d4mk): jeff-ubuntu logs colima/limactl restart attempts though neither binary exists there; (5) restart-attempt thrashing -- self-limiting design worked correctly, no fix needed; (6) reboot/watchdog governance -- jleechan-0ox still open; (7) queue starvation -- ALREADY FIXED by hca0/LANE-1's IDLE-STARVED metric this same session.

ADVERSARIAL SKEPTIC SIGN-OFF: PASS with one caught-and-fixed discrepancy. Independent sonnet skeptic subagent re-ran the jeff-ubuntu colima probe, spot-checked journalctl lines verbatim, verified all 7 cited bead IDs via br show, and verified all cited commit SHAs exist with matching content -- confirmed every claim in the doc traces to a real, checkable source. Caught one staleness bug before commit: doc originally described jleechan-wzk as 'open for durable fix' when it was actually CLOSED same-day with a tested fix (23e734f) -- corrected before commit.

Follow-up bead filed: jleechan-d4mk (colima-restart-attempted-on-Linux-where-not-installed, root cause not fully disambiguated between an anyhow downcast_ref bug and binary version skew).

title: daemon: attempt_backend_restart logs 'colima exists but restart returned non-zero' on jeff-ubuntu where colima/limactl are not installed
description: Verified 2026-07-09 during the 48h retro (bead c97c/hca0 mission): jeff-ubuntu's journalctl shows 'colima exists but restart returned non-zero (["start"])' / 'limactl exists but restart returned non-zero' / 'systemctl exists but restart returned non-zero (["--user", "start", "lima-vm@colima.service"])' at Jul 07 12:52:32, 12:54:15, 12:55:27 PDT. But 'which colima limactl' on jeff-ubuntu returns rc=1 (not found), and a filesystem search (find / -maxdepth 4 -iname colima) found no binary anywhere on PATH (confirmed systemd --user's actual PATH via 'systemctl --user show-environment'). Current HEAD src/main.rs:365-389 (attempt_backend_restart) has an explicit io::ErrorKind::NotFound guard (line 383-386) that should silently 'continue' past a missing binary via Command::new(cmd).spawn() ENOENT, rather than reaching the Ok(false) branch that prints 'exists but restart returned non-zero' (line 380) -- so either (a) anyhow's err.downcast_ref::<std::io::Error>() at line 383 fails to see through the .with_context() wrapper at line 340, making the NotFound guard dead code, or (b) the binary actually running on jeff-ubuntu at the time was an older version lacking this guard (see existing memory project_2026-07-07_ezgha_fleet_pacing_deadlock_and_version_skew.md on fleets running non-main binaries). Root cause not fully disambiguated -- needs: (1) check jeff-ubuntu's currently-installed ezgha binary version/SHA vs HEAD, (2) if current, write a unit test spawning a nonexistent command through run_restart_command_with_timeout and assert it hits the NotFound continue path, not the Ok(false)/eprintln path. Either way the observed behavior means attempt_backend_restart wastes 3 restart attempts + ~2s timeout each on platform-inappropriate tooling on native-docker Linux hosts, with a misleading log message implying colima IS installed when it is not.

title: dark-factory af-tick intake fails on GraphQL rate limit — no REST fallback for 'gh issue list' (live, every 240s tick)
description:
Context:
- Evidence: ~/Library/Logs/dark-factory/af-tick.err.log tail at 2026-07-11 17:23 PDT — repeated 'GraphQL: API rate limit already exceeded for user ID 13840161' followed by 'cmd failed (1): gh issue list --repo jleechanorg/worldarchitect.ai --label factory --state open --json number,title,body --limit 50'. Log mtime 17:23:30, tick interval 240s — every current scheduled tick's intake phase fails.
- Deployed tree: ~/projects/dark-factory @ thin-skill-migration-f-factory (03e04c33b).
- Finding: factory intake (daemon/factory-intake-from-gh.sh or inline gh issue list in factory-af-tick.sh) uses gh issue list, which is GraphQL-backed; when the GraphQL bucket is drained the intake fails outright with no REST fallback. REST and GraphQL are separate 5000/hr buckets (see ~/.claude CLAUDE.md 'gh dual-bucket fallback' and ez-gh-actions-4jv for the sibling ezgha fix in github.rs — different code, same pattern).
- Why it matters: factory issue intake silently stops during rate-limit windows; beads/issues labeled factory are not picked up, indistinguishable from 'no work'.

Implementation:
- Check bucket first via quota-exempt 'gh api rate_limit'; on GraphQL exhaustion fall back to REST: gh api 'repos/jleechanorg/worldarchitect.ai/issues?labels=factory&state=open&per_page=50' --jq mapping to the same number/title/body JSON shape. Keep the gh issue list path as primary.
- Non-goals: no retry/sleep loops inside the single-threaded tick (SERVE_LOOP_TIME_BUDGET class of bug); fail the tick fast if BOTH buckets are drained and log which bucket.

Acceptance criteria:
- Simulated GraphQL-drained window (or live occurrence) shows intake succeeding via REST path in af-tick.out.log.
- rg 'rate_limit' daemon/ shows the bucket pre-check.
- Staleness note: log lines and deployed branch verified 2026-07-11 17:23 PDT; re-read before implementation.
close_reason: Already implemented in dark-factory PR #248 (merged f129db88): daemon/factory-intake-from-gh.sh now has GraphQL->REST fallback for both issue-list and PR-view paths (verified on main: 'Fallback to REST API' lines 75-93, 154-155). Production-window proof rides with jleechan-vxs8 deploy + gvdw scheduled-tick verification (deployed tree still on thin-skill branch).

title: Commit+push watchdog ping fix (watchdog.rs, WatchdogSec=180, doctor queue-health)
description: Provenance: /doctor session 2026-07-07. Uncommitted: src/watchdog.rs, src/main.rs, src/docker_backend.rs, src/service.rs (WatchdogSec=180), doctor.sh isolation check, scripts/queue-health.sh, scripts/cleanup-stuck-runs.sh, .claude/commands/doctor.md, ezgha-doctor SKILL. Deployed to jeff-ubuntu via rsync+cargo install but NOT on origin/main. Linux still watchdog-kills when release_stale_slots+gh paginate exceeds 180s.

title: investigate Mac IDLE-STARVED: 2 slots starved, 12 jobs queued oldest 10.4m
description: doctor-runner run on Mac (2026-07-09 ~18:52 PDT, fleet on binary 3c2080e) reported: 12 jobs queued (oldest 10.4m, over the 5m IDLE_STARVED_THRESHOLD_MIN), 2 Mac slots IDLE-STARVED, verdict [BAD] fleet unhealthy: 4 critical checks failed. Also 3 of 6 Mac slots (b-1/b-4/b-5) showed CYCLING (blinked between the doctor's two 25s resamples) in the same snapshot -- comparable proportional rate to jeff-ubuntu's un-patched 4/16 cycling, so this may be a real capacity/distribution problem rather than (or in addition to) reaping churn. Determine: (a) is 6-runner Mac capacity insufficient for current job-type mix, (b) is job routing/labels sending jobs to Mac that could run on Linux instead, (c) is this transient queue pressure that clears on its own. Discovered during grace-window-fix post-deploy verification, session 2026-07-09.
close_reason:
INVESTIGATION COMPLETE (read-only, no fleet mutations). Synthesis:

(a) NO -- 6 Mac runners are not structurally undersized for "Mac-specific
work" because there is no such workload category. All ~46 workflow files in
worldarchitect.ai use the identical generic expression
runs-on: fromJson(vars.SELF_HOSTED_RUNNER_LABELS || '["self-hosted"]') --
confirmed live value ["self-hosted"], zero OS qualifier. Mac's 6 runners
are just 6 of 22 fungible slots in ONE shared queue, not a dedicated pool.
3 doctor-runner samples showed Mac oscillating 6/6 EXECUTING -> 4
executing+2 IDLE-STARVED -> 6/6 EXECUTING within ~1-2 min -- shared-pool
contention (smaller sub-pool statistically starves first under random
scheduling), amplified by test.yml's matrix.test-group fan-out bursting
many concurrent jobs from a single PR into the combined pool.

(b) YES -- root cause. Confirmed via grep across every workflow file: ZERO
jobs anywhere in worldarchitect.ai require macOS (no macos-latest, no
Xcode/simulator usage). Yet ANY of ~40+ CI jobs per PR can land on Mac
purely by chance because nothing partitions by OS. ez-gh-actions runner
registration (src/config.rs:428,667) uses equally generic labels on both
pools -- nothing distinguishes ez-mac-runner-b-* from ez-runner-c-* by OS.
Precedent already exists in-repo: test.yml:951 (coverage-report) is
already hardcoded [self-hosted, Linux, X64]. Fix: add [self-hosted, Linux]
to jobs not needing macOS (nearly all of them) -- filed as follow-up bead
jleechan-sqdt since this mission was investigation-only.

(c) TRANSIENT per-incident, but the exposure is structural. The 18:52 PDT
spike self-cleared within ~90 min with zero intervention (doctor-runner:
BAD -> OK -> fully healthy 6/6 EXECUTING). Zero historical "starv" matches
in ~/.local/state/ezgha/alerts.jsonl (205 lines) -- not a previously-
alerted recurring condition. Org-wide queue depth log swings wildly
tick-to-tick, consistent with transient spikes not sustained
undercapacity. However: since (b)'s root cause (no OS-label partition) is
unfixed, starvation spikes will keep recurring under bursty PR load --
each episode self-clears, but the underlying exposure persists until
labels are partitioned.

Full evidence trail (3-lens parallel swarm, doctor-runner samples, alert
logs): /tmp/ez-gh-actions/sidekick/eh91-idle-starved-investigation/STATE.md

title: Capture GH response payload/exit context on runtime registration failures
description: Validate new response-preview logging in src/github.rs (api_json/api_json_until/api_post_empty, generate_jitconfig, list_runners) by reproducing a failing runner registration path and collecting stderr/stdout bytes and payload snippets to confirm whether 409 collisions return malformed JSON or plain text.

title: feat(reaper): release stale slots whose runner_id is missing from GitHub's live list
description: Add fallback to release_stale_slots so that if a slot's runner_id is not present in GitHub's list of runners at all, it is immediately released to prevent leaks.

title: doctor-runner falsely reports Colima stopped when Docker backend is live
description: On 2026-07-15 after deploying ez-gh-actions main 08aee617, the authoritative ./doctor-runner reported [BAD] lima VM stopped while docker info succeeded, the configured colima context endpoint was live at ~/.colima/default/docker.sock, six managed runner containers were running, and the direct Lima SSH ControlMaster was healthy. Root cause is likely doctor-runner branching on colima/limactl control-plane state without accepting the already-proven functional Docker backend in this Broken-hostagent state. Repro artifact: /tmp/doctor-runner-08aee61.log. Fix should report degraded control plane separately from backend availability and avoid instructing colima start when that could disrupt active CI.

title: fix(diagnostics): split release_stale_slots reclaimed by category (empty / stale-id / offline)
description:
WHY: Commit 52bf703 emits 'info: release_stale_slots reclaimed {N} stale slot(s)' at src/docker_backend.rs:473, but release_stale_slots_from_with_containers_for (src/docker_backend.rs:511-572) increments reclaimed from THREE distinct branches:
- empty-id (line 532): slot reserved by next_slot but record_slot_runner_id never ran (root cause jleechan-kbd)
- stale-id (line 539): recorded runner_id no longer in list_runners (legitimate safety)
- offline/no-container (line 553): status=offline, not busy, no local container (legitimate safety)
After jleechan-kbd lands, operators will see slot-file rewrites and need to know whether the safety path is firing on legitimate stale IDs (expected) or the empty-id path is still active (bug). Today no way to distinguish without reading source.

WHERE: src/docker_backend.rs:511-572 (three reclaim branches) and the log emission at line 473.

WHAT: Split the single reclaimed counter into three: empty_reclaimed, stale_id_reclaimed, offline_no_container_reclaimed. Emit 'info: release_stale_slots reclaimed A empty-id, B stale-id, C offline-no-container stale slot(s) for prefix {prefix} (live GH runners: X, local containers tracked: Y)'. Increment each counter in the appropriate branch.

VERIFY: Deploy with both jleechan-kbd and this split. Expected over a normal hour: empty_reclaimed=0 always, stale_id_reclaimed=0..6 (legitimate), offline_no_container_reclaimed=0..2. If empty_reclaimed > 0 after jleechan-kbd, a spawn path still leaks empty reservations.

RISK: Trivial — purely additive diagnostic, no behavior change.

LINKED: 52bf703 (supersedes its anonymous reclaimed {N} line). Provenance: 2026-07-08, diagnosed by slot-drift-deepdive sidekick via /tmp/worldai/sidekick/slot-drift-deepdive-12h/STATE.md.

title: Mac host disk pressure: APFS container hit 99% (285Mi free) during concurrent af-driver cargo builds 2026-07-12 ~02:5x UTC — self-recovered to 6.1Gi; needs audit + floor guard for factory lanes
description: Transient ENOSPC broke 2 of 5 factory coder lanes (5u3s, 4jv — harness couldn't create output-capture files; even 'true' failed). Self-recovered 285Mi -> 6.1Gi in ~10min (purgeable reclaim). Known large consumers at check: /private/var/folders 13G (OS temp), ~/.cache 3.5G, per-clone cargo target dirs ~0.5-1G each. MITIGATIONS APPLIED: factory lanes now share CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/af-shared-cargo-target and check df >= 4Gi before cargo invocations. FOLLOW-UP: run /disk_magician audit on this Mac; consider a persistent disk-floor guard for runner + factory workloads (ezgha already has free_disk_gb guard but jleechan-mdi says it measures the Colima VM overlay not host FS — related). Provenance: sidekick-af-driver tick-1 report + df verification in main session, 2026-07-12.

title: investigate: Linux fleet missing-registration (ez-runner-c-1 missing; busy=14-17/22)
description: queue_monitor INV-1 violations report busy=14-17/22 registered=14-17 queued_jobs=60-94 with fail_class=missing-registration. Live cross-check (slot-drift-investigator 2026-07-08 19:09 UTC): Mac=6/6 registered, Linux=15/16 registered, missing only ez-runner-c-1. Last alert 18:50 UTC: busy=17/22 registered=18 queued_jobs=66. This is a SEPARATE symptom from the Mac slot-drift flap (jleechan-goa); the missing-registration class is on jeff-ubuntu's side, not Mac. Read-only investigation scope: src/queue_monitor.rs:1037-1070 fleet_runner_stats expected-name set, src/docker_backend.rs ensure_count on jeff-ubuntu daemon, and the cross-host registration API path in src/github.rs. Needs dispatch to a Linux-side sidekick since jeff-ubuntu is single-writer domain. Provenance 2026-07-08.
close_reason: Primary investigation complete. Findings written to /tmp/worldai/sidekick/linux-missing-reg-12h/STATE.md. Root cause: Path-1 race in release_stale_slots reclaims slot whose JIT registration is still propagating through GitHub's API. Fix landed in PR #33 (commit 1a9baf4) on origin/main, which extends Path 1 safety net to skip reclaim when local container is alive (same shape as the jleechan-tku proposed fix). Closed by /nextsteps 2026-07-09.

title: jeff-ubuntu missing gitleaks blocks agent-orchestrator commits (2nd missing-tool gap this session, needs durable provisioning)
description:
jeff-ubuntu also had no gitleaks binary at all (which gitleaks: empty), despite jleechanorg/agent-orchestrator's own husky pre-commit hook hard-requiring it ("gitleaks is not installed! ... Commit blocked until gitleaks is installed."). This blocked every commit in that repo from this host until fixed.

Worked around this session (2026-07-10) the same way as the git-lfs gap (see jleechan-2qfz): installed gitleaks v8.21.2 as a static binary to ~/.local/bin/gitleaks (no sudo available/used, same user-scoped pattern).

This is the SECOND missing security/VCS tool found missing on this host in one session (git-lfs for dark-factory, gitleaks for agent-orchestrator) - suggests jeff-ubuntu's base provisioning doesn't include the common toolchain multiple repos' pre-commit/pre-push hooks assume exists. Worth a single durable fix (a provisioning script or checklist covering git-lfs, gitleaks, and possibly other repo-hook-required tools) rather than discovering + ad-hoc-fixing them one at a time as each repo's hooks get exercised.

Provenance: session fixing jleechanorg/agent-orchestrator-mirror#12 via factory-rg-sidekick, PR https://github.com/jleechanorg/agent-orchestrator/pull/756.

title: worldai CI-B: presubmit.yml 10 jobs with zero per-job path filters + duplicate push:main/dev runs
description: presubmit.yml runs 10 jobs (limit-pr-runs, schema-coverage, prompt-contracts, function-loc-ratchet, agy-json-contract, python-lint, python-typecheck, javascript-lint, +2) on EVERY non-docs PR — a workflow-YAML-only PR still runs python-typecheck (3.8m) etc. FIX: (a) add a detect-changes job (dorny/paths-filter or tj-actions/changed-files) and gate each job on its relevant path family (python jobs on **/*.py, JS lint on **/*.js|ts, schema jobs on schemas/**, contracts on prompts/**); (b) drop the push: branches [main, dev] trigger — post-merge presubmit duplicates what the PR already ran (keep push only if deploy depends on it — verify first). Estimated ~30-40% presubmit compute reduction. Evidence: audit 2026-07-09, p50 4.5m p90 15.1m; /tmp/wf-audit/presubmit.yml.
notes: PR opened: https://github.com/jleechanorg/worldarchitect.ai/pull/8304 (draft, mergeable, 1 file: presubmit.yml +111/-13). SCOPE EXPANDED mid-flight per audit + team-lead steering: added detect-changes job (dorny/paths-filter@v3.0.3 pinned SHA) gating 7/8 jobs on path-relevance, PLUS moved 6 pure-static-analysis jobs to ubuntu-latest (prompt-contracts, function-loc-ratchet, agy-json-contract, python-lint, python-typecheck, javascript-lint). detect-changes/limit-pr-runs/schema-coverage stay self-hosted (schema-coverage flagged as a follow-up host-move candidate in Known Limitations, not unilaterally expanded). Push trigger KEPT — re-verified zero downstream consumers. Gate-script PASS, lint baseline unchanged. Skeptic verdict: PASS on filter-coverage (cross-checked each job's actual run: commands, not just names) and host-move safety. Still OPEN pending human review/merge.
close_reason: MERGED to worldarchitect.ai main 2026-07-09/10 under user authorization 'merge approved for non prod changes' (all 4 PRs 100% workflow-file-only): rnc=#8303, gbs=#8304, huy=#8302, oud=#8305. Est ~5,400 self-hosted min/day freed (~17% fleet capacity). Delete-candidate test-email-notification.yml remains listed in #8305 body for separate human sign-off (not deleted).

description: Investigation complete (2026-07-08 12:10 PDT). Findings written to /tmp/worldai/sidekick/slot-drift-12h/STATE.md. Top finding: empty-id reservation window in start_one_with_generate (docker_backend.rs:769-845) is the root cause of slot-drift flap. notes: Deep-dive COMPLETE 22:00 PDT 2026-07-08. STATE.md updated with F10/F11. KEY CORRECTION: a parallel session filed project_2026-07-08_ezgha_linux_missing_registration_root_cause.md while I was running. It identifies a 3rd bug surface — Path 1 race (docker_backend.rs:538-544) reclaims a slot when runner_id is missing from list_runners WITHOUT checking if the local container is alive. JIT propagates 5-30s through GH API. THIS IS HAPPENING ON THE MAC RIGHT NOW: at 21:59:51 slot file has only slots 2/4/5/6 (141498/141503/141505/141506); slots 1 and 3 are MISSING (reclaimed), and names b-1/b-3 are also missing from GH API. jleechan-kbd (empty-id fix) does NOT cover this — the same patch needed for Linux jleechan-tku also needs to land on Mac. Daemon PID 81517 restarted 21:57 (last-exit 1, watchdog launched). System load 294/245/184 — massively overloaded, correlates with colima instability. FINAL: jleechan-kbd + jleechan-tku (Path-1 race patch) BOTH needed to stabilize at 22/22. Updated second-fix recommendation: behavioral patch on Path 1, not just log-split.

description: Live launchd job ai.dark-factory.af-tick (StartInterval 240s) fails every tick since the jleechan-x7ky fix deployed. Evidence: ~/Library/Logs/dark-factory/af-tick.err.log has 20x 'factory-af-tick.sh: line 229: TARGET_REPO: unbound variable', last at 2026-07-11 16:42 PDT (2 min before check). Deployed tree ~/projects/dark-factory is on branch fix-af-multirepo-dispatch @ c011794 (PR https://github.com/jleechanorg/dark-factory/pull/248). Also invalidates the RUNNER-QUEUE GATE claim that both prerequisites (v2wv, x7ky) passed.

title: fix(docker): format cpus argument to 2 decimal places to prevent float precision error
description: Docker run fails with exit code 125 when cpus is a high-precision float (e.g. 0.8571428571428571). Formatting it to 2 decimal places resolved this.
close_reason: Resolved by formatting cpus to 2 decimal places in src/docker_backend.rs.

title: doctor: rename /doctor to /doctor-ezactions + per-runner activity truth (EXECUTING/IDLE-OK/IDLE-STARVED/DOWN)
description: P1+P2 of the 2026-07-09 measurement/reliability plan. RENAME: .claude/commands/doctor.md -> doctor-ezactions.md (keep a deprecation stub at doctor.md pointing to the new name), update CLAUDE.md /doctor references and .claude/skills/ezgha-doctor/SKILL.md. MEASUREMENT: doctor.sh per-slot section must report exactly one evidenced state per configured runner (6 Mac + 16 Linux): EXECUTING (job name + repo + run URL + elapsed — from docker top Runner.Worker presence + docker logs 'Running job' line, cross-checked vs gh api runs?status=in_progress); IDLE-OK (Listening for Jobs in log AND repo queue empty — healthy, say 'nothing queued'); IDLE-STARVED (listening while queued>0 for >=5min — BAD); DOWN (no container or no listener — BAD). Exit non-zero only on STARVED/DOWN. Motivating defect: 'Listening for Jobs' tail-grep conflates EXECUTING with DOWN — a busy fleet measures as 0/22 listening (observed 2026-07-09 12:15 PDT: Mac 0/4 + Linux 0/15 'listening' while 9 jobs in_progress). Provenance: user directive 2026-07-09.
close_reason:
Implemented + shipped in commit dbcf907 (pushed to origin/main). doctor-runner sections 9+10 now classify every configured slot (6 Mac + 16 Linux) into exactly one of EXECUTING/IDLE-OK/IDLE-STARVED/DOWN, with EXECUTING slots getting real job+repo+elapsed+run-URL evidence via the GitHub jobs API (fixed a real pre-existing bug: the old lookup joined on .runner_id, a field that does not exist on workflow-run objects, so it silently never matched). IDLE-STARVED requires QUEUE_OLDEST_FRESH_AGE_MIN >= 5min, not just instantaneous backlog presence. SLOT_PROOF_CRITICAL (exit code) only fires on STARVED/DOWN. Renamed .claude/commands/doctor.md -> doctor-ezactions.md (deprecation stub kept), updated CLAUDE.md + ezgha-doctor/SKILL.md.

LIVE EVIDENCE (single doctor-runner invocation on the Mac, which also SSHed read-only to jeff-ubuntu for the Linux half):
  macos (jeffreys-macbook-pro) slots: 5 executing, 1 idle, 0 down (of 6 configured)
  linux (jeff-ubuntu) slots: 13 executing, 3 idle/down (of 16 configured) -- transient churn between the two independent probes (tracked as jleechan-laxg, not a defect)
  Sample EXECUTING evidence line: 'ez-mac-runner-b-1 EXECUTING -> job=Directory tests (core-mvp-1(self hosted)) repo=jleechanorg/worldarchitect.ai elapsed=0.8m url=https://github.com/jleechanorg/worldarchitect.ai/actions/runs/29044207618/job/86208697760'
  Sample IDLE-STARVED evidence line: 'ez-runner-c-4/6/14 IDLE-STARVED -- oldest queued 11.9m >= 5m threshold'
Full log: /tmp/doctor-run-2.log (local machine, not committed -- ephemeral evidence).

ADVERSARIAL SKEPTIC SIGN-OFF: PASS. Independent sonnet skeptic subagent traced classify_local_slot, the IDLE-STARVED threshold logic, the SLOT_PROOF_CRITICAL accumulation path, and the new job-evidence lookup against the live-running fleet; confirmed (a) a busy fleet never reads as DOWN/dead, (b) an empty or briefly-queued queue never contributes to the bad verdict, (c) exit code only degrades on STARVED/DOWN. One low-risk finding noted (job index 'head -1' would pick arbitrarily if two in-progress jobs somehow shared a runner_name -- not observed, not defended, judged non-blocking). bash -n clean, shellcheck clean (only 3 pre-existing unrelated warnings remain).

Follow-ups filed: jleechan-laxg (section 9/10 dual-probe transient skew on fast-churning fleet), jleechan-t8l2 (README/GEMINI/AGENTS/.codex doctor doc mirrors still stale, out of hca0 scope).

Claim/fetch succeeded from https://api.github.com/repos/jleechanorg/worldarchitect.ai/pulls/8177 and session metadata wa-3091 had agent=codex with launchCommand containing codex. Before that pane also printed `Command codex not found`, indicating launch environment/path inconsistency before retry. Session wa-3091, its worktree, tmux, and stuck spawn PIDs were cleaned up. title: worldai CI-C: self-hosted-mvp-shard1.yml post-merge push runs duplicate PR CI (4 jobs x up to 30m on self-hosted)
description: self-hosted-mvp-shard1.yml triggers on push: branches [main, dev] with the same paths as pull_request — every merge to main re-runs 4 parallel self-hosted jobs (harness-autonomy + mvp-shard-1/2/3, up to 30m each) that just passed on the PR. Also consider: shards could short-circuit via detect-changes (test.yml already has one). Evidence: audit 2026-07-09; /tmp/wf-audit/self-hosted-mvp-shard1.yml lines 4-24.
notes: PR opened: https://github.com/jleechanorg/worldarchitect.ai/pull/8302 (draft, mergeable, 1 file: self-hosted-mvp-shard1.yml +15/-20). Push trigger removed. Skeptic-verified: squash-merge-only repo (30/30 recent main commits single-parent) means push runs were byte-identical duplicates of PR runs — confirmed redundant. External-consumer check: no live status/badge readers found. No nightly schedule added (not justified per squash-merge finding). Gate-script PASS, lint baseline unchanged (4 grandfathered/0 new), 11 relevant tests passed (test_workflow_runner_policy.py). Still OPEN pending human review/merge.

title: parse_external_ref() cannot parse full GitHub URLs, breaks 34 beads' escalation recovery - tracks dark-factory#238
description:
Tracks jleechanorg/dark-factory#238 (https://github.com/jleechanorg/dark-factory/issues/238).
`parse_external_ref()` in daemon/src/adapters.rs:177 does
`external_ref.split('#')` and requires exactly 2 parts (owner/repo#N
short form) -- but the actual external_ref values for 34 confirmed beads
are full GitHub URLs (https://github.com/.../pull/N), which have zero '#'
characters, so the function always returns None and comment_external()
always errors. Confirmed via the live daemon's own log: 67,079 identical
ESCALATION_NOTIFICATION_FAILED events across these 34 beads, permanently
stuck (human_held_recovery_attempt_cap_reached, unrecoverable).

Fix (TDD): extend parse_external_ref to also accept the full URL form
(https://github.com/<owner>/<repo>/(pull|issues)/<n>) alongside the
existing owner/repo#n short form. File: daemon/src/adapters.rs, function
at line 177, repo jleechanorg/dark-factory (canonical checkout on
jeff-ubuntu: ~/projects/dark-factory).

HIGH CAUTION: this fix touches code used by the LIVE
ai.dark-factory.daemon.service (18h+ uptime, actively managing work
across worldarchitect.ai + dark-factory + other repos). Implement as a
normal PR, cargo test + clippy clean, NEVER restart/redeploy the live
service -- that decision needs explicit human go-ahead given the service's
broad blast radius.
notes:
Implementation complete in isolated jeff-ubuntu worktree /home/jleechan/.worktrees/dark-factory-j74c on branch codex/jleechan-j74c-external-ref-url.

Root cause: deterministic backend parser accepted only owner/repo#N; real full GitHub URL external_ref values returned None before comment_external. No model/prompt/schema involvement.

Strict TDD RED: CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=2 ~/.cargo/bin/cargo test external_ref_tests::parse_external_ref -- --nocapture -> rc 101; short form passed, GitHub pull and issues URL cases failed with left None.
GREEN: identical command -> rc 0, 3 passed.
Full: CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=2 ~/.cargo/bin/cargo test -> rc 0 (all daemon suites, 0 failures). Clippy: CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=2 ~/.cargo/bin/cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings -> rc 0.

Commit: https://github.com/jleechanorg/dark-factory/commit/aa679f72b550445c60b421f9b8b64988e08038b1
PR: https://github.com/jleechanorg/dark-factory/pull/244
Issue: https://github.com/jleechanorg/dark-factory/issues/238

First push failed because non-interactive SSH PATH omitted existing /home/jleechan/.local/bin/git-lfs. Retry with PATH=$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH succeeded and local/remote SHAs matched; tracked separately by jleechan-8v57.

No live daemon restart, stop, redeploy, merge, auto-merge, or force-push performed.

CodeRabbit, Bugbot, Evidence Gate, daemon-tests, and test all SUCCESS; PR https://github.com/jleechanorg/dark-factory/pull/244 is mergeable. Live daemon remained untouched. title: Mac fleet outage: Colima crash plus false-success/timeout recovery loop
description: Authoritative reconciled diagnosis 2026-07-11. The outage was real: Docker at ~/.colima/default/docker.sock was unreachable and no Mac runner could execute. Initiating event: /Library/Logs/DiagnosticReports/ResetCounter-2026-07-11-132649.diag records forced button reset at 13:25:28 PDT (rst btn_rst,btn_seq_reset target_off_restart). On login, Colima and org.jleechanorg.ezgha both attempted recovery. Confirmed amplification defects in src/main.rs: (1) colima start was bounded to 30s and live log recorded timeout; descendant limactl usernet processes survived, (2) macOS then fell through to plain limactl start colima in ~/.lima rather than Colima's ~/.colima/_lima namespace, allowing false-success logs with no Docker, and (3) success was command rc=0 rather than Docker health. Evidence does NOT prove the legacy VM owned the real Colima disk or that a stale in_use_by symlink initiated the outage; do not repeat that causal claim. Old 2026-07-06 Colima /bin/bash Input/output error logs are antecedent host history, not proof of today's stop. Fix is PR https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/pull/56 at commit https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/commit/630bf9539d6d43ef073dc3eafae505253f09339e: OS-specific commands, 120s cold-start budget, process-group kill, Docker-health success. Still required before closure: merge/deploy through Gate 0, deterministic reboot/slow-start chaos proof, 6/6 Mac Runner.Worker proof under load, and watchdog activation. Cross-tracker rev-ssoo4 should be deduped against this bead.
notes: Fix implemented on https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/pull/56, current head https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/commit/a4436accb7ef9f9a6f83cf643103151e5b4d8fd0. Descendant-leak RED reproduced then GREEN; 226 Rust tests, clippy, and prior current-head CI/reviews passed. GitHub issue sync: https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/issues/57. title: fix(docker_backend): record runner_id before docker run to close slot-drift flap
description:
ROOT CAUSE: src/docker_backend.rs:769-845 start_one_with_generate writes the JIT runner_id to slot_assignments.toml AFTER docker run (line 842), but next_slot already wrote an empty reservation '' to the file (line 222) at allocation time. The empty reservation persists for the multi-second window of generate_jitconfig + docker run. The very next release_stale_slots cycle (called at lines 1035, 1098) treats empty-id slot entries as stale (line 473-478) and frees them — even when the JIT registration is in flight. Next ensure_count re-allocates, registers a fresh JIT, writes a new ID. Result: slot file 'flaps' with new IDs every cycle, while GitHub names stay registered (invisible to queue_monitor invariants because names persist; only ID churn).

EVIDENCE: Mac slot_assignments.toml was rewritten 5 times in 15min (slots 1..6 all got fresh IDs 140482/140491/140470/140461/140462/140492 between 11:54 and 12:09 PDT 2026-07-08) while the 6 GitHub names ez-mac-runner-b-{1..6} stayed continuous. Live cross-check via fleet_runner_stats: 6/6 Mac names always registered.

FIX: Move record_slot_runner_id_for(Some(cfg), slot, runner_id)? from line 842 to immediately after generate_jitconfig returns successfully (after line 808, before docker run at line 811). The post-docker-run call at line 842 becomes redundant and can be removed. Container's --label ezgha.runner_id={runner_id} (line 815) remains; only the slot-file write timing changes.

VERIFY: Deploy build with fix, observe slot_assignments.toml mtime for 60min during a Mac job cycle. Pre-fix mtime cadence >= 1/min; post-fix should be <= 1/5min and only when prior ID is confirmed gone via list_runners (not just absent from a stale snapshot).

RISK: Low. runner_id is known after generate_jitconfig returns, before any container work. Worst case (daemon dies between new write and docker run): orphan-sweep at lines 393-409 already reaps stale offline/idle runners with no slot owner, recovery path unchanged.

LINKED: jleechan-goa (parent investigation, will link after creation). Separate Linux-side missing-registration root cause (ez-runner-c-1 missing) needs separate investigation on jeff-ubuntu fleet, not this PR's scope.

Provenance: diagnosed 2026-07-08 by slot-drift-investigator sidekick via /tmp/worldai/sidekick/slot-drift-12h/STATE.md.
close_reason: Fixed by PR #33 (commit 1a9baf4, merged 2026-07-09T06:20:15Z). record_slot_runner_id_for moved from post-docker-run to immediately after generate_jitconfig succeeds (src/docker_backend.rs ~line 982, matching the bead's exact fix spec). Verified by sidekick audit (verify-workitems-roadmap, 2026-07-09): diff read directly, confirmed both the code change location and semantics match this bead's ROOT CAUSE/FIX sections.

title: dark-factory CXDB schema drift: $H init (bash) never applies Rust-side idempotent migrations (park_reason) — bash-only /af sessions crash on park/recover-held against legacy DBs
description: Found by sidekick-af-driver 2026-07-12: 'no such column: park_reason' from $H park / recover-held on the shared ~/.dark-factory/daemon-cxdb.sqlite. Root cause: ensure_park_reason_column (daemon/src/state.rs:444) is a Rust-open-time migration; daemon/factory-overlay.sh init only does CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS so pre-existing tables never gain new columns. Driver hand-applied the identical additive SQL (ALTER TABLE bead_overlay ADD COLUMN park_reason TEXT) after reading state.rs:444-456 — approved retroactively (schema migration the daemon itself owns, idempotent, not business-data mutation; verified only the 2 intended beads changed state). FIX: factory-overlay.sh init should apply the same idempotent column-adds as the Rust side (single source: generate the ALTER list from one shared spec, or have init invoke the daemon binary's migrate subcommand if one exists/gets added). Also audit for other Rust-only migrations. target_repo: jleechanorg/dark-factory

title: doctor-runner: section 9 vs section 10 counting/display probes can disagree on a fast-churning fleet (transient skew)
description: doctor-runner (ez-gh-actions repo) section 9's classify_local_slot/SSH counting pass and section 10's list_slot_work display pass each independently re-probe docker top a few seconds apart. On a fast-churning fleet this can produce disagreeing counts between the two sections in the same run (observed 2026-07-09: section 10 display showed jeff-ubuntu '13 executing, 0 idle, 3 down' while section 9's counting block a few lines later showed '13 executing, 3 idle, 0 down' for the same invocation — see /tmp/doctor-run-2.log). Not a correctness bug in the classification logic itself (both probes are independently ground-truth-correct at their own instant), but violates the bead hca0 requirement of 'exactly one evidenced state per configured runner' within a single doctor-runner run. Fix: have list_slot_work return/export its per-slot classification so the counting block reuses it instead of re-probing, OR merge sections 9+10 into a single probe pass.

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: 2026-07-10 observed again: one doctor snapshot reported Linux 9 executing/1 idle/6 down; minutes later another reviewer saw 16/16; final synchronized run reported 14 executing/2 down. Mac also changed between doctor and direct SSH. Reuse one sampled state per invocation and timestamp it clearly.

title: BUG: free_disk_gb guard measures Colima VM overlay, not host FS (docker_backend.rs:761-780)
description: Swarm adversarial review 2026-07-07 (coldstart, confirmed against real code). ezgha's disk-floor guard free_disk_gb runs 'docker run --entrypoint df' (src/docker_backend.rs:761-780) which measures the Docker/Colima VM overlay filesystem, NOT the macOS/Linux HOST filesystem. On Colima the VM overlay and host are different volumes. Impact TODAY (independent of cold-start): the disk-floor protection can read healthy while the host disk (where ~/.config/ezgha, images, logs, and any future mirror cache live) is critically low, or vice-versa — the guard may be protecting the wrong filesystem. Fix: add/replace with a host-side statvfs check on the actual paths ezgha writes; document which filesystem each guard covers. This is a prerequisite for the cold-start mirror (jleechan-yov P3 BLOCKER A) but stands alone as a current-code defect.
close_reason: Verified already fixed by commit 46dd073 (2026-07-14, 'gate runner refill on host disk pressure'). Confirmed via test run: docker_backend::tests::host_disk_probe_reads_outer_filesystem and configured_host_disk_floor_refuses_space_below_the_floor both pass. Closing as stale/housekeeping per the 2026-07-17 disk-redesign swarm's finding.

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: 2026-07-07 parallel cold-review (review-18-disk agent): ez-gh-actions issue #18 is the SAME bug as this one — ONE cross-platform defect, not two. free_disk_gb() (docker_backend.rs:765-780) has a SINGLE impl with no backend branching; the daemon-view-vs-host-view gap is identical whether the daemon is Colima (Mac) or libvirt/Tart (Linux). The function's own doc comment (lines 761-764) admits it: 'A host-side df would read the wrong filesystem whenever the daemon is a VM.' Confirmed current-code false-safety hazard: guard can read healthy while host disk ~100% — already caused a fleet outage + corrupted containerd store 2026-07-03 (worldai bead rev-g5bwl). Bumped P2→P1. FIX: add a host-level statvfs check (nix/libc::statvfs) on ezgha's real write paths (~/.config/ezgha, docker data-root, logs) as a SECOND INDEPENDENT guard ALONGSIDE the existing daemon-view check (both filesystems can independently exhaust on VM backends). Link GH issue #18 here; the tracking bead 'rev-i2oka' in the cross-session report does NOT exist in the store — no bead to reconcile.

title: queue-reaper-stopgap: harden against gh API rate-limit exhaustion (dual-bucket + backoff + call-budget)
description: Provenance: ~/Library/Logs/ezgha-queue-reaper-stopgap.log ticks 02:41:54Z + 02:48:18Z 2026-07-08 — both failed 'gh: API rate limit exceeded' (HTTP 403, core bucket drained by concurrent pollers: reaper per-run status polls from the force-cancel fallback, sidekick monitor loop, jeff-ubuntu doctor, main session checks — all one token). Result: no sweep for 30+min, 45 runs crossed 20m (queue regression 62->87). Fixes for scripts/cleanup-stuck-runs.sh + queue-reaper-stopgap.sh: (1) check gh api rate_limit (quota-exempt) FIRST, skip tick gracefully if core remaining < ~200 with a log line, (2) REST->GraphQL fallback per the dual-bucket rule (separate 5000/hr buckets), (3) reduce per-run polls: batch the post-cancel status check into one list call instead of N per-run gets, (4) cross-session budget: reaper is the PRIORITY consumer — monitor loops should sample less often when reaper is active. Also consider Rust-native reaper (qbl) inheriting the same protections.
close_reason: Implemented + live-verified 2026-07-07 20:5x PT, commit 071ce2e. (1) Listing 403s: backoff 60s, one retry, then graceful tick-skip exit 0 — pre-check via rate_limit is IMPOSSIBLE for the secondary throttle (core read ~3900 remaining while /actions/runs 403'd), so 403 handling is the gate; (2) per-cancel status polls removed — batched force-cancel verification via ONE re-listing (new stat tail_force_cancelled); (3) zombie delete path keeps per-run poll. Live apply run: 3 cancelled, 1 force-cancelled survivor, 0 failures. Deployed via Gate 0 (cargo install + kickstart). GraphQL fallback listed in the bead was NOT implemented — graceful-skip makes it non-critical; open a follow-up if throttle windows prove long enough to matter.

title: Rotate credentials exposed by dark-factory systemd drop-ins and prevent systemctl cat leakage
description: Security provenance: during read-only /linux verification on 2026-07-11, `systemctl --user cat ai.dark-factory.daemon.service` printed plaintext provider and GitHub credentials from user service drop-in files under ~/.config/systemd/user/ai.dark-factory.daemon.service.d/. Values are intentionally omitted here. Required response: rotate all affected credentials, move secrets to a protected EnvironmentFile or credential store that is not emitted by routine unit inspection, verify file mode/ownership, and add a redacted diagnostic command to the operator runbook. Do not commit secret values or paste them into issues/logs. No rotation performed in this session because credential rotation changes external auth state and needs explicit operator authorization.

title: Bake Playwright webkit/chromium system deps into ezgha-runner image (repo-wide CI blocker)
description: worldarchitect.ai Playwright self-hosted jobs (mobile-auth-regression.yml, auth-browser-tests.yml) fail repo-wide with 'apt-get download libavif16 libgav1-1 libwoff1 libyuv0 -> Cant find a source to download version, exit 100'. Runtime apt fallback fails on version-pin/mirror drift. Pre-existing on main since 2026-07-06 (proven: run 28911764794 byte-identical, 6h before session). Both workflows runs-on SELF_HOSTED_RUNNER_LABELS = ezgha fleet. Root cause: Dockerfile.runner (ezgha-runner:latest) lacks Playwright browser system deps, so workflow falls to broken runtime apt-get. FIX: bake full playwright webkit+chromium system deps into Dockerfile.runner, rebuild image, redeploy Linux fleet (jeff-ubuntu single-writer). Blocks green on #8279 + every Playwright PR. Diagnosed by centralize-8279 sidekick 2026-07-08.

Remaining scope of this bead is DEPLOY ONLY: rebuild ezgha-runner:latest on both hosts. Mac rebuild running now (this session, 2026-07-09); jeff-ubuntu rebuild belongs to the Linux deploy-owner (single-writer). The fix/runner-playwright-deps branch (66b4e9b) is superseded by PR #16 and can be deleted after Linux deploy.

Two independent branches implement the same fix (bake Playwright deps into Dockerfile.runner) — ez-gh-actions PR #16 (branch fix/playwright-chromium-webkit-runtime-deps @ 303f373, opened 2026-07-07, OPEN, has full PR description + build/probe evidence) vs this bead's own branch fix/runner-playwright-deps @ 66b4e9b (2026-07-08, no PR ever opened). title: worldai_claw Jest tests repeatedly OOM-killed on ez-gh-actions Mac fleet (2.6GB/runner clamp)
description:
worldai_claw's backend Jest test suite (packages/backend/tests/*.test.ts)
is repeatedly OOM-killed when running on the ez-gh-actions Mac fleet.
Confirmed via `docker events --filter container=<name>` showing exact-
timestamp-matched `container oom` events for both failures:

- Run 29105511813 (2026-07-10T15:55:56Z, worktree_worldai_2d branch),
  job backend-test on ez-mac-runner-b-4: 2 jest workers SIGKILLed
  (faction_simulator_bugfixes.test.ts, mcp.campaign.test.ts) at
  08:58:34 and 08:59:08 PDT, matching container oom events at the same
  two timestamps exactly.
- Run 29086485404 (2026-07-10T10:29:28Z, feat/mobile-architecture-cleanup
  branch), job backend-test on ez-mac-runner-b-6: same SIGKILL signature
  ~10 min before that.

Root cause: ez-gh-actions' auto-clamp divides host RAM across configured
runner count. Mac (6 runners, ~16GB host RAM): clamps each runner's
Docker memory limit to 2659 MB. jeff-ubuntu (16 runners, ~48GB host RAM):
clamps to 3010 MB per runner -- only slightly higher, same problem class
likely exists there too though not yet confirmed via OOM event grep.
worldai_claw's Jest backend suite (multiple parallel workers, MCP server,
SQLite writes) exceeds ~2.6-3GB under load on this host, causing repeated
CI failures unrelated to actual code correctness.

This is NOT an ez-gh-actions daemon bug -- the clamp is working as
designed (protect the host from a runaway job). It's a capacity/config
tradeoff: either (a) increase the per-runner memory ceiling (reduces
headroom if multiple memory-heavy jobs land concurrently, risk of host
OOM if not careful), (b) reduce Mac's configured runner COUNT so the
divisor shrinks and each runner gets more RAM (fewer parallel job slots
in exchange), or (c) worldai_claw could reduce jest worker parallelism
for CI (e.g. --maxWorkers=2) to fit within the current ~2.6GB ceiling --
this is the lowest-blast-radius fix since it doesn't touch fleet capacity
at all, just the test runner's own resource usage.

Needs a deploy-owner decision per this repo's Blast-Radius & Interaction
Review principle (CLAUDE.md) before changing any threshold/count.

Discovered while investigating a user report ("something isn't working
with worldai_claw" -> "check gh actions"), session 2026-07-10.
close_reason:
FIXED. worldai_claw PR #257 (https://github.com/jleechanorg/worldai_claw/pull/257)
added --maxWorkers=2 to the backend-test jest invocation, capping Jest's
worker pool instead of letting it default to os.cpus().length (which
reported the Colima VM's 10 cores rather than the container's actual
~1.67 CPU / ~2.6GB cgroup allocation).

Verified in real CI, not just locally: PR #257's backend-test check ran
twice and passed both times -- once on ez-mac-runner-b-4 (Mac fleet,
where the original OOM was observed) and once on ez-runner-c-14
(jeff-ubuntu's Linux fleet), confirming the fix holds cross-platform.
Local validation before pushing: 6/6 suites, 190/190 tests, peak RSS
~951MB (well under the 2.6GB ceiling), vs the prior unbounded config that
was getting OOM-killed.

Not merged (worldai_claw merge policy not evaluated/authorized here --
PR left open for the user).

This closes the fix track. The broader capacity/config question this
bead raised (whether the Mac/Linux per-runner memory clamp should also
be raised) is now moot for this specific symptom since the CI-side fix
resolved it without touching fleet capacity -- no further action needed
unless a similar OOM recurs on a different workload.

title: ci-reduction sidekick: shared scratch clone caused branch-switch collision across concurrent lane sub-agents
description: During the 2026-07-09 ci-reduction sidekick mission (4 parallel lane sub-agents all working in ONE shared git clone at /private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-jleechan-projects-other-ez-gh-actions/808fdb69-83de-4e4d-8f22-96f575174bf6/scratchpad/worldai-ci-reduction), LANE-C's agent reported its branch (ci/shard-drop-push-trigger) got silently switched to ci/presubmit-per-job-filters mid-task after a git add, apparently because another concurrent lane agent ran git checkout in the SAME working directory. LANE-C self-recovered by creating an isolated git worktree (git worktree add) for its own branch and verified no cross-lane file contamination in the final PR (#8302, single file changed as expected). FIX for future multi-lane sidekick missions: give each lane its own git worktree or clone up front instead of a shared one — do not rely on agents self-recovering from this. See STATE.md at /tmp/worldai/sidekick/ci-reduction/STATE.md for full mission context.
notes: Second same-day occurrence of this class, other host: a codex sub-agent on jeff-ubuntu reset the SHARED jeff checkout mid-cargo-install during the b4afd85 deploy (2026-07-09 ~12:48 PDT), causing one failed restart and ~2min daemon downtime (recovered). jeff-side lesson identical: deploy from a detached worktree only; never share a mutable checkout between concurrent agents. Two incidents in one day (4-lane ci-reduction clone collision + this) — this bead is now the canonical harness fix for both.

title: daemon: add per-slot state-transition log line (EXECUTING/IDLE/DOWN with timestamps) to make churn measurable, not just greppable
description:
P4 item (c) from r83a / retro doc docs/retro-48h-runner-problems-20260709.md class 3 (slot-drift/JIT races, the LARGEST failure class: 5,307 respawns over ~11.5h before PR #33 fixed the underlying bug -- no metric caught it live at the time, only reconstructible after the fact by grepping thousands of log lines). Deferred out of r83a's own time-box (Rust daemon code, not safely rushable without deploy-owner validation).

CURRENT STATE: src/main.rs:778 only logs 'respawned ephemeral runner {name}' on the STARTED transition, via EnsureCountOutcome (src/docker_backend.rs:1260-1263, fields: started: Vec<String>, missing: u32). There is no tracking of a slot's PREVIOUS state, so a churn burst (e.g. a slot flapping EXECUTING->IDLE->DOWN->EXECUTING every serve tick) is invisible except as N separate 'respawned' lines an operator has to manually count and time-bucket after the fact.

GOAL: emit one log line per slot per state change, e.g. 'slot ez-runner-c-4: EXECUTING -> DOWN (was executing for 340s)' or 'slot ez-runner-c-4: DOWN -> EXECUTING (was down for 12s)', so a churn burst shows up as a visible RATE (N transitions/slot/min) in the log stream itself, not just reconstructible via grep -c.

TWO IMPLEMENTATION OPTIONS (pick based on effort/risk tradeoff, both untried, needs investigation):
(a) Extend EnsureCountOutcome with a 'reclaimed'/'removed' Vec<String> alongside 'started', and diff each tick's started/reclaimed sets against the PREVIOUS tick's slot roster (would need a small persistent HashMap<String, SlotState> in the serve loop, e.g. near queue_monitor::QueueMonitorState at main.rs:758) to emit the transition line. Cheapest -- reuses ensure_count's existing per-tick cadence, no new sampling.
(b) A new lightweight periodic sampler (similar to queue_monitor's InvariantSamplerState) that runs a docker-top-style check every tick and diffs against its own previous snapshot -- closer to what doctor-runner already does out-of-process in bash, but in-process and continuous instead of only-on-demand. More general (catches EXECUTING<->IDLE transitions that ensure_count itself doesn't see, since ensure_count only cares about container-alive, not worker-busy) but more code.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: cargo test green; a unit test that simulates two consecutive ensure_count ticks with a slot flapping and asserts exactly one transition line is emitted (not one per tick regardless of state). Deploy is main's (Mac) responsibility per the single-writer rule -- this bead's implementer should cargo test + commit + push ONLY, never restart/systemctl/launchctl.

title: fix(docker): format cpus argument to 2 decimal places to prevent float precision error
description: Docker run fails with exit code 125 when cpus is a high-precision float (e.g. 0.8571428571428571). Formatting it to 2 decimal places resolved this.
close_reason: Resolved by formatting cpus to 2 decimal places in src/docker_backend.rs.

title: Fleet version-skew: Mac(ccbd10e-dirty) + Linux(6a3138d) both running non-main binaries — Gate 0 violated
description: 2026-07-07 22:4x PT read-only audit. main HEAD=56d1e9a but Mac ezgha=0.1.0-ccbd10e-dirty, Linux ezgha=0.1.0-6a3138d — three different versions, neither fleet on main, Mac even 'dirty' (uncommitted). Root cause: concurrent Gate-0 loops across multiple sessions (each cargo install + service restart) racing to deploy their own commit + the po2-respawn-pacing branch getting built onto Linux without merge. Consequences: unreviewed code live on fleets (the pacing deadlock jleechan bug came in this way), and repeated restarts knock the fleet to 0. FIX: single owner deploys ONE main-merged binary to both fleets; serialize Gate-0 restarts (a restart-lock so concurrent sessions don't thrash the daemon). Relates to the respawn-pacing incident + ez-gh-actions-po2.

title: reliability: wire starvation/token-freshness/state-transition metrics from 48h retro into doctor + queue_monitor
description: P4: implement the earliest-detection metrics the 48h retrospective (see retrospective bead) identifies. Baseline candidates: (a) IDLE-STARVED alert — any runner listening while repo queue>0 for >=5min (queue_monitor invariant, complements INV-1/INV-2); (b) token-freshness probe in doctor — gh_token mtime vs 45min timer expectation + live 200-check against /orgs/.../runners; (c) per-slot state-transition log line (EXECUTING->IDLE->DOWN transitions with timestamps) so churn is measurable not anecdotal; (d) fix watchdog exit 78 (bead jleechan-0ox — fold in or cross-ref). Gate on P3 output; dedupe against ez-gh-actions-x9n before implementing. Provenance: user directive 2026-07-09.
close_reason:
Scoped per-item disposition (P4 of the 2026-07-09 measurement/reliability plan), gated on c97c's retro doc (docs/retro-48h-runner-problems-20260709.md):

(a) IDLE-STARVED alert -- ALREADY SHIPPED in hca0/commit dbcf907 (this same mission, LANE-1): doctor-runner sections 9/10 flag any runner idle while QUEUE_OLDEST_FRESH_AGE_MIN >= 5min. No further work.

(b) token-freshness probe with live 200-check -- SHIPPED in commit 7d7c3c2 (this commit). Section 4b's existing mtime check (fresh<45min/warn/stale>60min) could not detect the jleechan-wzk failure mode (a token that looks fresh by mtime but is cryptographically dead after a private-key rotation). Added an explicit live API call using GH_TOKEN overridden from the gh_token file's own content (every other gh api call in doctor-runner rides the operator's ambient gh auth, not ezgha's app token, so none of them actually exercised this token's validity) -- verified live, returns 200 against the current fresh token.

(c) per-slot state-transition log line -- DEFERRED, filed as jleechan-pz33 with exact file:line anchors (src/main.rs:778 respawn log, src/docker_backend.rs:1260 EnsureCountOutcome struct), two implementation options, and acceptance criteria. This is Rust daemon code touching the serve loop; not safely rushable without deploy-owner cargo-test+restart validation within this session's time-box. Targets the retro doc's largest failure class (slot-drift/JIT races, 5,307 respawns over ~11.5h) where this metric would have made the churn visible as a rate instead of only reconstructible after the fact.

(d) watchdog exit 78 -- cross-referenced to existing jleechan-0ox (OPEN, P3), not re-scoped or re-diagnosed here; that bead already has its own investigation plan.

DEDUPE vs ez-gh-actions-x9n: x9n asks for a broader daemon-side, webhook-driven registration-failure-rate + queue-time-growth alerting layer (prefers workflow_job events over polling). r83a's items are narrower, doctor-visible, pull-based probes -- (a) and (b) are complementary to x9n, not duplicative, and do not close it. x9n remains open as the broader alerting mission; noted in the retro doc's class 2 (API rate-limit) as the natural home for a 'daemon-side push alert' follow-up.

title: worldai CI-A: green-gate.yml has ZERO path filtering — precheck runs on self-hosted for every PR event incl body edits
description: green-gate.yml triggers on pull_request types [opened, ready_for_review, edited, reopened, synchronize] with NO paths/paths-ignore, and green_gate_precheck runs on SELF_HOSTED_RUNNER_LABELS (2.2m per run when green, 20m timeout). 'edited' means every PR-body edit (frequent — bots edit bodies) re-burns a self-hosted slot. FIX: (a) add paths-ignore for roadmap/**, .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/**, pure-markdown (mirror presubmit.yml's list — careful: Gate 6 reads PR BODY not files, so body-edit reruns ARE intentional; scope the reduction to file-path filtering only, do NOT drop 'edited'); (b) evaluate moving the precheck to ubuntu-latest — it is bounded gh-api/jq lookups per its own comment ('bounded gh-api/jq lookups... no longer occupies a scarce self-hosted runner' was the intent when Gates 4/8 moved out; the precheck itself still runs self-hosted — moving it too frees ~1 slot-hour/day). Evidence: audit 2026-07-09, /tmp/wf-audit/green-gate.yml lines 77-100.
notes: PR opened: https://github.com/jleechanorg/worldarchitect.ai/pull/8303 (draft, mergeable, 1 file: green-gate.yml +59/-18). SCOPE EXPANDED mid-flight (corroborated by both my own investigation and the ultracode audit): moved ALL 3 of green_gate_precheck + bugbot_gate_wait + smoke_gate_wait from self-hosted to ubuntu-latest (audit's single biggest LANE-A item, ~810 min/day), plus paths-ignore mirroring presubmit.yml, plus fixed a stale top-of-file comment that falsely claimed Gate4/8 jobs were already GitHub-hosted. green_gate aggregator job (4th) deliberately left self-hosted (not verified dependency-free). Gate-script PASS, lint baseline unchanged. Skeptic verdict: PASS (no environment: key, no Docker, only secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN which works identically on any runner). Still OPEN pending human review/merge.
close_reason: MERGED to worldarchitect.ai main 2026-07-09/10 under user authorization 'merge approved for non prod changes' (all 4 PRs 100% workflow-file-only): rnc=#8303, gbs=#8304, huy=#8302, oud=#8305. Est ~5,400 self-hosted min/day freed (~17% fleet capacity). Delete-candidate test-email-notification.yml remains listed in #8305 body for separate human sign-off (not deleted).

title: fix(docker_backend): skip stale reclaim when local container alive (Path 1 race)
close_reason: Fixed by PR #33 (commit 1a9baf4, merged 2026-07-09T06:20:15Z). release_stale_slots now checks whether a local container with the expected name is still alive before reclaiming an unmatched slot (src/docker_backend.rs ~line 549-552) — skips reclaim when container exists, treats as in-flight. Verified by sidekick audit (verify-workitems-roadmap, 2026-07-09): diff read directly, confirmed match to this bead's FIX spec.

title: doc: note min_available_memory_mb may need lowering on memory-constrained dev machines
description:
Adversarial review of PR #52 (jleechan-aeh0, memory-floor circuit breaker) flagged: the new `min_available_memory_mb` default (8192 MB) is a single global default with no environment/host-tier differentiation. On a memory-constrained dev laptop (8GB or borderline 16GB total RAM), 8192MB available could plausibly not clear the floor under normal light use, causing `ensure_count` to perpetually (but safely, fail-open with an alert, not a crash) refuse to spawn on a healthy box.

Mitigation already exists: the field uses the same config-override pattern as `min_free_disk_gb` (already overridden per-host in production config), so a constrained dev machine can override with one TOML line, no code change needed.

Action: add a doc comment / README note next to `min_available_memory_mb` in src/config.rs (or the config docs) telling operators on memory-constrained dev machines to lower this value.

Source: PR https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/pull/52 adversarial review, 2026-07-10.

title: BUG: reaper misses very-old queued runs (2x 528m survived every tick) — force-cancel gap
description: Provenance: 2026-07-07 21:0x PT checkpoint. Runs 28896281392 + 28896281366 (branch sidekick/lane-h-gitleaks, test.yml + presubmit.yml, NOT deploys) sat queued 528min (8.8h) and escaped EVERY reaper tail-cancel tick, until manually force-cancelled. cleanup-stuck-runs.sh tail pass cancels age>20m then batch-force-cancels survivors — so these should have been caught. Hypotheses to investigate: (1) they predate the force-cancel-fallback deploy (commit 071ce2e) and the plain-cancel 202-but-stays-queued behavior left them wedged, and subsequent ticks' batched re-listing force-cancel only targets ids IT cancelled that tick, NOT pre-existing wedged runs from prior ticks; (2) the per-page=100 listing may not include them if queue>100 and they sort late. LIKELY ROOT CAUSE = #1: the batched force-cancel only re-checks runs cancelled in the SAME tick; a run wedged in a PRIOR tick is re-plain-cancelled (202, no-op) forever, never force-cancelled. FIX: in the tail pass, force-cancel ANY candidate still queued after its plain cancel regardless of which tick first cancelled it — i.e., track/force-cancel by age>threshold+still-queued, not by same-tick membership. Repro: any run that survives one throttled/failed tick. Ties to jleechan-me9 (reaper hardening).
close_reason: Fixed in scripts/cleanup-stuck-runs.sh (ez-gh-actions-qf4d, mirrors missing ez-gh-actions-ssjg): replaced the same-tick batched re-listing force-cancel with verify_and_force_cancel(rid), called synchronously right after EVERY individual cancel_run() (superseded + tail passes). Each run's fate (still-queued -> force-cancel) is now resolved per-run at cancel time, not dependent on same-tick batch-list membership -- exactly the fix this bead's root-cause analysis (#1) specified. Tests: tests/cleanup_stuck_runs_force_cancel_test.sh proves force-cancel fires when status stays queued after plain cancel. cargo test 220 passed, shellcheck clean.

title: Verify Gate-4 bugbot_gate_wait fail-closed live on first completed post-merge Green Gate run

If runs fail on the new job SHAPE (not code), revert candidate — flag main session immediately. Close bead with run URL evidence when verified.

title: ao spawn fails with 'spawn git ENOENT' for agent-orchestrator project despite git being on PATH
description:
`ao spawn --project agent-orchestrator --agent codex` failed immediately
with `spawn git ENOENT` during factory-rg-sidekick's Phase A dispatch
attempt for agent-orchestrator-mirror#12 (2026-07-10), even though `git`
demonstrably exists and resolves fine on jeff-ubuntu via `which git` and
plain Node `execSync` in the same working directory. This points to a
PATH-propagation bug specific to how `ao spawn`'s child-process spawning
resolves binaries -- not a missing-tool problem like the sibling
git-lfs gap (jleechan-2qfz).

Not root-caused yet -- the discovering sidekick time-boxed it and fell
back to a direct fix instead of chasing this further. This bead exists so
the finding isn't lost (it was previously only mentioned narratively in a
teammate status report, never tracked as its own item).

Suggested next steps: reproduce `ao spawn` for the agent-orchestrator
project specifically (may be project-scoped, not universal -- the
dark-factory project's ao spawn attempt failed differently, on git-lfs,
not ENOENT), inspect ao's spawn implementation for how it constructs the
child process's PATH/env, compare against a working ao spawn invocation
for a different project to isolate what differs.

Discovered during a root-cause investigation into why /af (auto-factory)
was never actually used to drive any of this session's ~10 PRs -- this is
one of several compounding reasons (alongside /af's own single-repo scope
hardcoded to worldarchitect.ai, and jleechan-3ns6's AO lifecycle backfill
being disabled for dark-factory).
notes:
Root cause confirmed 2026-07-11: this is NOT PATH propagation. The configured project repository path was absent on jeff-ubuntu. Node child_process.execFile fails while changing to a nonexistent cwd and reports the misleading "spawn git ENOENT", even though git resolves correctly.

Fix: shared workspace-worktree plugin validates the expanded configured repo path before Git operations in create(), findManagedWorkspace(), and restore(), and emits an actionable agent-orchestrator.yaml path-field error. Debug-only packages/cli/src/commands/spawn.ts instrumentation was removed.

Strict TDD evidence (Node v22.23.1):
RED, production guard temporarily removed:
pnpm --filter @jleechanorg/ao-plugin-workspace-worktree test -- -t "throws an actionable error when the project repo path does not exist"
FAIL: AssertionError: promise resolved instead of rejecting (1 failed, 62 skipped).

GREEN:
same focused command: 1 passed, 62 skipped.
pnpm --filter @jleechanorg/ao-plugin-workspace-worktree test: 63 passed.
pnpm --filter @jleechanorg/ao-plugin-workspace-worktree typecheck: PASS.
git diff --check: PASS.
gitleaks 8.21.2 staged scan: no leaks.

PR: https://github.com/jleechanorg/agent-orchestrator-ts/pull/758
Do not close until the PR lands.
