title: Evaluate runner over-subscription on Linux (16 -> 20+ slots) given IO-bound job mix
description: Provenance: 2026-07-07. GitHub model = strictly 1 job/runner; a runner cannot multitask. But the HOST can run more runners than cores when jobs are IO-wait-heavy (deploys, polls, network tests). Post-resize Linux has 24 CPUs / 16 slots = 1.5cpu each; if post-stabilization measurement (jleechan-tan) shows significant IO-wait share, raise runner.count toward 20-24 with cpu clamp ~1.0-1.2 instead. Depends on: jleechan-tan re-measurement + cold-start bead (more runners = more churn = respawn overhead matters more). Single-writer: Linux config.toml owned by jeff-ubuntu mission — coordinate via INBOX.

title: dark-factory daemon must run from INSTALLED ARTIFACTS (binary/versioned install), not a git checkout — branch churn under running launchd daemon caused silent behavior swap (2026-07-11); includes post-PR#248-merge redeploy+verify
description:
Context:
- Incident: ai.dark-factory.af-tick executes whatever branch is checked out in ~/projects/dark-factory. On 2026-07-11 the tree sat on fix-af-multirepo-dispatch @ c0117942 (crashing every tick on the TARGET_REPO bug, jleechan-gvdw) until another session switched it to thin-skill-migration-f-factory at 17:16 PDT — which silently 'fixed' the crash by running entirely different code. Neither state was a deliberate deploy.
- Why it matters: same failure class ez-gh-actions already solved with the single-writer deploy rule (ez-gh-actions-k5yk) and Gate 0 SHA-pinning. A dev working tree doubling as the launchd daemon's execution root means every branch checkout is an unaudited production deploy.
- Evidence: this session's observations 16:xx-17:23 PDT — git -C ~/projects/dark-factory log/branch before+after; ~/Library/Logs/dark-factory/af-tick.err.log crash window 15:20-16:42 PDT.

Implementation (in dark-factory repo, not here):
- Give the launchd job its own deploy checkout (e.g. ~/deploy/dark-factory pinned to origin/main, updated only by an explicit deploy step that logs old SHA -> new SHA), OR embed a Gate-0-style HEAD-SHA check that refuses to tick when the checkout drifts from origin/main.
- Post-PR#248-merge step: after https://github.com/jleechanorg/dark-factory/pull/248 merges (fix in flight via sidekick, see jleechan-zp2i), switch the daemon execution tree back to main at the merge SHA and prove TWO consecutive SCHEDULED ticks complete without 'unbound variable' or intake errors (jleechan-gvdw acceptance criteria).
- Non-goals: do not restart the daemon out-of-band (single-writer); do not fix here in ez-gh-actions.

Acceptance:
- launchd plist/wrapper points at a deploy-owned path with template committed to the repo (launchd-plist-template rule); deploy step logs SHA transition; scheduled-tick proof posted.
Staleness: tree/branch state verified 2026-07-11 17:23 PDT; re-check before implementing.
notes: FACTORY ROUTING: target_repo: jleechanorg/dark-factory — new-work bead in dark-factory repo. Deliverable per DESIGN CORRECTION note: installed-artifact deploy (binary/Gate-0 model), launchd ProgramArguments point at installed path never ~/projects/dark-factory, deploy step logs SHA transition + refuses dirty/non-main source.

title: beads flush can clobber concurrent sessions' beads — stale-db full-file rewrite dropped 4 beads on push race (2026-07-12)
description: Incident: ez-gh-actions commit 5b93080 (sibling session, 2026-07-12 ~01:3x UTC) flushed .beads/issues.jsonl from a DB that predated commits 5c97fae/3382907, silently DELETING jleechan-dhuo/zp2i/vxs8/9c7l and reverting jleechan-gvdw + ez-gh-actions-uh2 to stale versions. Caught only because the next push hit a rebase conflict and this session diffed bead ids per side (in-mine-only vs in-theirs-only) before resolving; a clean auto-rebase would have shipped the data loss invisibly. Resolution used: per-bead newest-wins union by updated_at (commit 9d655b5). Fix directions (pick in beads tooling, not per-agent discipline): (a) br flush merges against the on-disk jsonl (newest-wins by updated_at) instead of full-file overwrite from db; (b) pre-flush guard: refuse when jsonl contains bead ids/updated_at newer than db (means db is stale — import first); (c) failing that, a CI check on bead-count/id regression in .beads commits. Evidence: this session transcript 2026-07-12; diff methodology preserved in the resolution commit. Related: worldai has bead-jsonl-sort-check.yml (jleechan-95d) but nothing catches deletions.
notes: FACTORY ROUTING: target_repo: jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions — new-work bead. Deliverable: CI check (or br-side guard) that fails a PR/commit when .beads/issues.jsonl DELETES bead ids or regresses updated_at vs the parent commit (deletion clobber guard). Second occurrence tonight: commits 5b93080 and bb340c2 both dropped concurrent beads. 2026-07-19 /green audit: PR https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/pull/63 at 95499ba250508669a7144f96b9ffbf0c1eccd96a is /advice APPROVED but /er PARTIAL. Before merge, obtain real GitHub workflow-event Layer 2 proof for the production-by-effect trusted workflow plus validator (277 delta lines), then refresh exact-head /er PASS and all six green gates.

title: doctor-runner: dedupe DOWN-persistence sleeps between sections 9 and 10 (doubled wall time)
description: Post PR #40 + 6b699ca: section 9 (2-sample DOWN persistence, 30s sleep) and section 10 / PR #40's CYCLING implementation (25s re-sample) can EACH sleep on first-pass DOWN candidates in the same run — up to ~55s added wall time for correct-but-redundant double verification. Fix: single shared re-sample pass — section 9 records the persistent verdict + timestamps; section 10 consumes it (it already defers to section 9 for local DOWN since 6b699ca; extend to the CYCLING probe so only ONE sleep happens per run). Suggested by jeff-ubuntu session 2026-07-09 after PR #40 merge. Low priority — results are correct, only latency is doubled.

title: fix(ezgha): re-mint cached gh_token on private-key rotation (root cause of 401)
description:
ROOT CAUSE: ~/.config/ezgha/app_private_key.pem mtime 2026-07-08 01:59 (rotated 21h ago). The Mac daemon's cached ghs_NE... token was minted at 21:18 using the OLD key. When the key was rotated, the cached token was NOT re-minted; tokens signed by the old key are permanently rejected by GitHub. jeff-ubuntu's daemon works because it was restarted after the rotation (ghs_v4... mtime 22:42 is fresh).

This is NOT a normal token TTL expiry — ghs_ tokens default 1h max, but the signature is the durable problem. Even a fresh-from-key-ring token won't work until the daemon uses the new private key to sign the JWT.

WHY: ezgha's token-mint path reads app_private_key.pem at startup and caches the minted ghs_ token until expiry. If the key file is replaced on disk while the daemon is running, the cached token is still bound to the old key. The daemon doesn't watch for key-file changes. Same root cause as the jeff-ubuntu 401 earlier — they restart the daemon after rotation, we don't.

FIX options (in order of effort):
1. Quick (operational): restart the Mac daemon. The startup path will re-read app_private_key.pem (now the new key) and mint a fresh ghs_ token. launchctl kickstart -k gui/501/org.jleechanorg.ezgha. Note: per CLAUDE.md Gate-0 rule, do NOT restart if load_1min > 12 OR containers < 12; current Mac state is 0/0 containers (fleet actively drained by this 401), so the EXCEPTION clause applies — restart IS the remediation, not the risk.
2. Better (process): make ezgha watch app_private_key.pem for changes (e.g. via notify or inotify) and re-mint the cached token when the file changes. This is a code change.
3. Best (security): also rotate the ghs_ token on a timer (e.g. every 30min) so even if the key rotates, the cached token is replaced before its signature binds to the wrong key.

VERIFY: after restart, tail /tmp/ezgha-launchd-stderr.log and confirm a successful (200) response on the next list_runners call. Mac fleet returns to 5-6 containers within 30s.

LINKED: jleechan-9rk (the immediate auth-incident bead). Provenance 2026-07-08 22:55 PDT, diagnosed during fleet-health investigation after Mac fleet collapsed to 0/6.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 23e734f (claude/fable-5: feat(github): kick token-refresh job on 401 Bad credentials). run_gh chokepoint detects 'Bad credentials' (never bare 401 — permission 403s and rate limits excluded by tests) and kicks the installed refresh job (launchctl kickstart / systemctl --user start) with a 300s cooldown; daemon re-reads token file per gh call so recovery is seconds instead of the 45-min timer worst case. 3 new unit tests; 214 pass. Deployed to Mac binary 0.1.0-23e734f same day.

comments.author: verify-sidekick
comments.text: Sidekick audit (verify-workitems-roadmap, 2026-07-09): verified PR #35 (caf7c3e, merged) does NOT cover this bead. PR #35 adds retry-once-on-5xx/timeout mint logic + 45s hard timeout wrapper (scripts/mint_gh_app_token.py, scripts/refresh_gh_app_token.sh) — those are orthogonal fixes for transient mint failures/hangs. This bead's FIX options 2 (watch app_private_key.pem for changes, re-mint on rotation) and 3 (rotate ghs_ token on a timer) are NOT implemented anywhere in PR #35 or elsewhere as of this audit. Remains open, distinct, uncovered code-level gap.

title: GitHub-side wedge: in_progress job survives cancel+force-cancel (200 OK) after runner VM death
description: Provenance: Mac Colima resize 2026-07-07 ~19:3x PT (sidekick STATE.md /tmp/ez-gh-actions/sidekick/runner-queue-healthy-mac/STATE.md). Run 28912311508 (deploy-preview) started on ez-mac-runner-b-3 in the snapshot-to-stop race window; after VM restart the old runner registration (id 137026) stayed wedged on GitHub: cancel AND force-cancel both returned 200 but job still shows in_progress; runner DELETE returns 422 'currently running a job'. Not fixable via API — GitHub-side heartbeat timeout must expire. Implications: (1) reaper design (ez-gh-actions-qbl) should time-bound this case and defer rather than retry-loop; (2) any VM interrupt protocol must expect a snapshot-then-act race and a possibly-wedged registration afterward; ezgha's own retry loop clears it once GitHub's side times out. Operational lesson also applies to the pending Linux VM resize.

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: 2026-07-10 recurrence: Linux c-10/c-14 and Mac b-4 repeatedly showed offline+busy/no-container with runner deletion HTTP 422. On Mac this contributed to ensure_count failures and backend restart escalation. New child bead ez-gh-actions-ghd2.2 tracks bounded multi-tick handling without restarting the backend.

title: Demand-side: auto-factory push batching + CI value audit (#8214) follow-through
description: Provenance: FIXPLAN §4.3-4.4 + root-cause RC-demand (bot pushes fire 6-8 workflows each; 5 workflows = 62% of 800-run sample). Items: (1) drive worldai PR #8214 (CI value audit KEEP/TUNE/CUT) to merge-ready and act on CUT verdicts; (2) auto-factory should batch/debounce pushes (amend+single-push per green unit rather than N pushes = N CI storms); (3) consider org-level merge-queue or push-coalescing for bot branches. Long-term lane — needs #8214 outcome first.

title: Cold-start reduction: pre-bake pinned deps into runner image + shared pip/git cache volumes
description: Cold-start reduction — PHASED per swarm adversarial review (design: docs/design/coldstart-plan-v4-phased-20260707.md, reviewed design: coldstart-design-reviewed-20260707.md). /advice 3 rounds (opus+codex, 11 flaws incl .git-poisoning RCE) + swarm 5-lens review (REVISE, 2 hard blockers + over-engineering finding). PLAN: P1 = C2 wheelhouse (bake ABI-matched ruff/mypy/reqs wheels into Dockerfile.runner + PIP_FIND_LINKS; ~36% of win, LOW risk, no daemon change, ship NOW). P2 = checkout tuning (fetch-depth:1 + blob:none audit worldai; diff branch chore/ci-fast-checkout FIRST). GATE = measure remaining checkout+setup after P1+P2+48h; build C1 mirror ONLY if remaining gap large + checkout-dominated. P3 (conditional) = C1 read-only mirror + snapshot-flip + v3.1 (H dedicated thread, I workspace-precreation spike) + swarm BLOCKER A (host-FS disk guard; existing free_disk_gb at docker_backend.rs:761-780 measures VM overlay not host — confirmed bug) + BLOCKER B (adversary-resistant reaper TTL, semi-trusted job can pin disk by holding fd open).

title: Fix doctor section 7 cross-host self-test false negative
description: Provenance 2026-07-11T22:29Z: ./doctor-runner returned rc=1 and suggested restart while its own local docker-top gate proved 6/6 Mac and remote proof showed 16/16 Linux executing with zero queued jobs. Section 7 rejected six successful ezgha-selftest runs because they landed on Linux names rather than the local Mac prefix. Fix proof routing/verdict so cross-host success does not create a destructive false-negative remediation.

Remaining section-7 WARNs are pre-fix history (5/6 runs on ez-runner-c-*) aging out of the 6-run window; section 7 still needs the cross-host awareness this bead tracks so successes on the OTHER host's fleet aren't warned as foreign.

title: AO gemini worker hook-injection dirties tracked .gemini/settings.json and blocks Gate 0 deploys
description: AO session 953501c04ccc-ez-gh-action-d7cbe139 injected Pre/PostToolUse hook wiring (session-specific absolute AO_DATA_DIR paths) into the TRACKED file .gemini/settings.json and never cleaned it up. Consequence: permanent dirty working tree in the repo -> verify-exit-criteria.sh Gate 0 fails ('uncommitted changes outside allowed paths') on every deploy; tonight's deploy-owner had to stash around it 6+ times then revert it (verified no live gemini worker before revert; AO re-injects at next spawn so revert is safe). FIX OPTIONS (pick in AO repo or here): (a) AO injects hooks via an UNTRACKED overlay (.gemini/settings.local.json if gemini-cli supports it) or restores the file on session end; (b) add .gemini/ to Gate 0's allowed-dirty paths alongside .beads/ (one-line change in docs/verify-exit-criteria.sh line ~156 grep -vE pattern); (c) untrack the file entirely + commit an example. Provenance: session 2026-07-12, git diff captured in transcript; file last legitimately committed in b91a709.

title: P1 INCIDENT: po2 respawn-pacing deadlocks fleet refill at LOW load (Linux 3/16, load 6 vs ceiling 20)
description: ACTIVE 2026-07-07 22:4x-22:48 PT. Linux fleet collapsed 16→9→4→3/16 while load stayed LOW (8.19→6.51→5.95, ceiling 20). Daemon repeatedly logs 'warning: host 1-minute load average X leaves no safe respawn headroom below ceiling 20.00; delaying runner respawn' at X=5.95 — 70% headroom, yet refusing to respawn. LIKELY ROOT CAUSE: the pacing projects FULL-fleet load (current_load + count*per_runner_estimate) and refuses to respawn ANY runner if the projection exceeds ceiling, instead of respawning incrementally — a deadlock: never adds runners because it fears the full fleet would exceed ceiling, so fleet never fills. Directly causes: (a) 'runners all go down' after any restart, (b) 'only ~2 workflows handled' (starved capacity). CODE: not in main (56d1e9a); lives on branch origin/sidekick/po2-respawn-pacing commit 153d1b8 'make respawn refill gate-primary'. Linux binary running 6a3138d (a build of that branch), Mac running ccbd10e-dirty — NEITHER is main. FIX: (1) incremental respawn (add one, re-measure) not all-or-nothing projection; (2) merge a single reviewed binary to main and deploy both fleets (Gate 0 SHA-match currently violated on both). Owner: jeff-ubuntu single-writer for Linux deploy; coordinate. Read-only investigation, no changes made.

title: deploy grace-window fix (d0e814e/3c2080e) to jeff-ubuntu
description: jeff-ubuntu is still on 44f0f42, which predates the registration grace-window fix (commit d0e814e, deployed to Mac as binary 3c2080e on 2026-07-09 ~18:41 PDT). This is a live production-churn fix, not hardening -- the sidekick p0-churn-sidekick reproduced the g3i churn claim live on jeff (306 respawns + 177 reclaims / 3h restart-free) before shipping it. Relayed via /tmp/ezgha-coord-ack-from-mac.txt to the parallel jeff-ubuntu session (single-writer rule: Mac session is ssh-read-only into jeff, cannot deploy remotely). Needs Gate-0-checked cargo install + restart on jeff's own hands at their next quiet window (load<12, containers>=12).

Binary now 2bb9551-dirty, includes d0e814e grace-window fix + PR #45/#46. Post-restart: 16/16 containers up, service active. Version-skew between Mac and jeff resolved.

title: Optimize daily-campaign-report: replace auth.list_users + per-user subcollection scan with collection_group + count aggregation
description: Provenance: Lane D codex investigation 2026-07-07 (bead rev-a10h9), 97.48min outlier breakdown: 70m53s infra (setup-python 32m42s + pip 38m11s → addressed by cold-start Phase 1 wheelhouse) + 24m49s GENUINE script time. Root cause of script time: unbounded N-users × per-campaign-story-stream query — auth.list_users() then per-user subcollection scan, streaming every user/campaign just to compute counts. No retry loops. Fix: (1) collection_group('campaigns') query + Firestore count() aggregation instead of streaming; (2) bound duplicate-scan to top-N post-filter candidates; (3) add per-phase timing so the 120min timeout (PR #8247) distinguishes infra vs script time. worldai repo change (mvp_site report script), separate PR, needs measurement. Natural re-validation case for cold-start Phase 1.

title: RESUMPTION runbook: sidekick pr56-pr248-review-fix-merge — fix PR #248 (gvdw) + verify PR #56, main session merges
description: Crash-recovery bead for in-session sidekick teammate. STATE.md: /tmp/ez-gh-actions/sidekick/pr56-pr248-review-fix-merge/STATE.md. Mission: fix dark-factory PR #248 failing test check + jleechan-gvdw TARGET_REPO bug (fresh clone /tmp/df-pr248-fix, never touch ~/projects/dark-factory), verify ez-gh-actions PR #56 readiness. Sidekick never merges; main session merges after green proof (user directive 2026-07-11 'review this work and fix as needed then merge it' — non-worldai repos, standard caution). On crash: respawn named teammate sidekick-pr56-pr248 pointed at STATE.md. Close when both PRs merged.
notes: PROGRESS 2026-07-11 ~18:00 PDT: PR #248 MERGED (f129db88) after independent verification. PR #56 held pre-merge: sidekick found 2 live bot findings + 1 corroborating (bead jleechan-9c7l), finding #1 is self-outage-caliber (120s restart timeout unbounded vs 120s outer startup deadline / TimeoutStartSec=130). Main session decided fix-then-merge per user's 'fix as needed then merge it'; sidekick tasked with remediation: (1) bound each restart attempt by remaining outer-budget with fail-fast floor, (2) grace/retry loop on post-restart docker_reachable probe, (3) TimeoutStartSec raise only with blast-radius statement. Merge after CI green at new head.
close_reason: Mission complete: dark-factory PR #248 merged (f129db88), ez-gh-actions PR #56 merged (3ebab28) — both fixed by sidekick, independently verified and merged by main session. Follow-ons tracked separately: gvdw (scheduled-tick proof), vxs8 (installed-artifact deploy), dhuo (intake REST fallback), w528 (flush clobber).

title: Investigate and fix Linux host reconciliation under churn (missing-registration + partial ensure_count)
description:
Root-cause from 2026-07-08: Linux under-capacity on jeff-ubuntu (13/16) with repeated ensure_count partial success, missing-registration, offline-respawning, and stale slot churn despite no global runner outages. This blocks clean capacity recovery after registry/API propagation delay windows.

Action: harden docker_backend path where release_stale_slots reclaims slot by runner_id when GH live_ids misses ID. Preserve local-container-alive slots through GH propagation window.

Evidence: live cross-checks + queue_monitor alerts and daemon journal on jeff-ubuntu from investigation session before this update.
close_reason: Duplicate/same-root-cause as jleechan-tku and jleechan-g5h (already closed). Root cause described here (release_stale_slots reclaims by runner_id when GH live_ids lags JIT propagation, causing missing-registration + churn on jeff-ubuntu) is the exact Path-1 race fixed by PR #33 (commit 1a9baf4, merged 2026-07-09) — see jleechan-g5h's own closure note citing the same PR. Verified by sidekick audit (verify-workitems-roadmap, 2026-07-09): current Linux fleet re-measured at 16/16 containers LISTENING (no missing-registration churn observed).

title: P0: watchdog logs 'manual intervention needed' but never runs its own printed colima remediation + deployed watchdog is a stale divergent copy
description: Two defects: (1) deployed watchdog ensure_colima_running() (lines 104-127) on colima start failure prints "try 'colima stop --force && colima start'" and returns 2 — never executes it; fleet sat down 3h16m on 2026-07-14 (watchdog log lines 666-767, ~/Library/Logs/ezgha-watchdog-launchd.log) and same class on 2026-07-07 (line 31, state=Broken). (2) The launchd plist org.jleechanorg.ezgha-watchdog invokes an OLD 221-line copy at /Users/jleechan/worldarchitect.ai/.claude/skills/ezgha-watchdog/scripts/ezgha-fleet-watchdog.sh, NOT this repo's 451-line scripts/ezgha-fleet-watchdog.sh (which has no colima logic at all). Fix: consolidate to repo script, add stale-state auto-remediation (stop --force + retry with backoff/max-attempts/hysteresis marker per docs/harness-early-victory-5whys.md). Blast radius: watchdog change — normal peak 1 remediation/incident, cap 2 attempts/30min.

title: P1: nothing rebuilds/verifies ezgha-runner:latest after Colima VM recreation — daemon disk probe fails 'image missing', fleet stays down
description:
P1: nothing rebuilds/verifies ezgha-runner:latest after image loss -- daemon disk probe fails "image missing", fleet stays down.

UPDATE 2026-07-14 root-cause investigation: the 2026-07-14 Mac outage image-loss lane DISPROVED the original "after Colima VM recreation" trigger for this occurrence -- basedisk/diffdisk birth-times (stat -f) confirmed unchanged since Jun 13; the 2026-07-13 event was a memory-only resize (16GiB to 24GiB), not a disk/VM recreation. The actual mechanism found: a weekly launchd job at ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.jleechan.cleanup-docker.plist runs docker image prune -af Sundays 03:30 UTC and has demonstrably deleted images before (699.7MB on 2026-06-28, 567.7MB on 2026-07-05 per its own execution log) -- this is the real vector by which ezgha-runner:latest can vanish, independent of any VM recreation. Fix scope is now TWO surfaces: (a) this bead's original ask -- pre-spawn image-existence check (docker image inspect) in src/docker_backend.rs start_one() with fail-loud/auto-rebuild fallback (still valid regardless of which event deletes the image), and (b) a NEW sibling fix to exclude ezgha-runner:latest from the prune cron itself, tracked separately as bead jleechan-h30q, since preventing deletion is cheaper than detecting it after the fact.

title: P1: install.sh chicken-and-egg — docker prereq check uses broken default socket and aborts BEFORE applying its own detected DOCKER_HOST override
description: 2026-07-14: /var/run/docker.sock was a stale symlink to missing ~/.docker/run/docker.sock. install.sh line ~229 detected ~/.colima/default/docker.sock and printed 'setting DOCKER_HOST', but the prereq gate (docker daemon unreachable) had already failed the run. Workaround: DOCKER_HOST=unix://$HOME/.colima/default/docker.sock ./install.sh. Fix: run socket detection BEFORE the docker prereq check and use the override for the check itself. Also: macOS-only clippy failures (dead_code/needless_return, fixed on main 2026-07-14) blocked the deploy gate — add a macOS clippy CI lane.

title: P0 HARNESS: subagents run destructive colima/limactl commands despite read-only instructions — verifier killed prod VM mid-recovery 2026-07-14
description: A workflow verifier agent (transcript agent-a338d1372f097ee65.jsonl, wf_f5f8e63a-fd1) ran 'timeout 20 colima stop --force && colima start' and 'timeout 10 colima start' at ~18:08 PT to 'verify' a claim whose text quoted the command — killing the VM my session had just restored (ha.stderr.log 18:08:34 'Shutting down VZ'). Short-timeout colima starts killed mid-flight also CREATE the 'vz driver running but host agent is not' stale state — this agent behavior class is plausibly a recurring cause of the outages themselves. Fix: (1) PreToolUse hook blocking colima/limactl stop/start/delete from subagent sessions, (2) never quote executable remediation commands verbatim in verifier prompts, (3) prompts must say VERIFY BY READING, not re-running.

title: P1: Mac host Data volume at 94% — 40GB runner floor will re-trip within days; add cleanup automation
description: 2026-07-14 outage extension: host had 33GB free < 40GB floor (MACOS_HOST_DISK_FLOOR_GB). Reclaimed ~22GB (cargo targets ralph-orchestrator/ccusage/ez-gh-actions-deploy/wt-phase1, Library/Caches Aside+claude-cli+granola+CodexBar, xcrun simctl delete unavailable) -> 55GB. Still 94% full: ~/projects 144G, App Support 23G, ~/.worktrees 21G (30+ stale wa-* worktrees), ~/.codex 28G (logs_2.sqlite 3.9G growing), Caches/Google 2.1G, ms-playwright 1G, go-build 686M. Fix: launchd cleanup agent (launchd-auto-cleanup skill) + stale-worktree reaper; alert before floor breach.
notes: ROOT CAUSE FOUND (2026-07-15 swarm, 4 lanes + prior-session history): (1) The recurring 'disk vanishing' during CI churn is COLIMA TRIM LAG — guest VM deletes ephemeral runner data but never returns blocks to the host sparse disk file; a prior session's 'colima ssh -- sudo fstrim -av' returned 19.7GiB in one pass (repeat passes: 7G, 5.7G, 4G). (2) Big drops self-recover partly via macOS purgeable reclaim (observed 3.3Gi->25Gi within minutes; also 2026-07-12 incident 285Mi->6.1Gi). (3) Messages 27G is LONG-TERM accumulation (0 files since Jul 13, 170 since Jul 1), NOT overnight sync — user-data, needs user review; WARNING per research: changing 'Keep Messages: 30 days' while Messages-in-iCloud is ON permanently deletes from iCloud on ALL devices. DURABLE FIX: bounded periodic fstrim (launchd or daemon tick: when host free < 25GB and guest free is high, run fstrim with timeout + hysteresis) + the reclaim table at /tmp/ez-gh-actions/sidekick/mac-disk-consumption-investigation/lane4-diskmap.md (top safe items: /private/tmp stale worktree copies 6.2G, ~/.hermes/.venv 691M + .git gc 2.4G, codex logs sqlite VACUUM 3.9G when codex stopped; ask-user: ~/.codex/sessions pre-June archive 17G).
close_reason: Durable fix shipped and verified live 2026-07-15: PR #86 (merged 11:06Z) added scripts/colima-trim-guard.sh (60s poll, <40GiB trigger, <30GiB emergency bypass, 55s bounded fstrim, fail-closed) with launchd template launchd/org.jleechanorg.ezgha-colima-trim.plist.template installed via install.sh:434 — confirmed registered in launchctl (last exit 0). PR #85 (merged 11:00Z) made the Mac disk floor config-driven. /advice 3-reviewer consensus (opus + fetched-source research + cursor cross-model) independently converged on exactly this design: host-side pressure-triggered fstrim; research lane confirmed NO colima/lima built-in exists (lima-vm/lima#356 open since 2021) and Docker Desktop auto-TRIM claims are unverified. Empirical: fstrim returned 19.7GB against lima maintainers' doubts — vz discard path works on this setup. Reclaim table for future manual passes preserved in bead notes.

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: 2026-07-15 live recurrence: after Mac fleet refilled and ran short ephemeral jobs, /System/Volumes/Data fell from ~35 GiB to 14 GiB even though exited --rm containers had deleted their guest data. Manual non-destructive 'colima ssh -- sudo fstrim -av' returned 19.7 GiB from /mnt/lima-colima and host free rose to 31 GiB immediately. Earlier trims in same recovery returned 7 GiB, 5.7 GiB, and 4 GiB. This proves Colima discard/TRIM lag is the repeated growth mechanism; cleanup/self-heal should invoke bounded fstrim when guest free blocks are high but outer APFS approaches alert threshold, preserving images/volumes. Admission floor alone cannot self-heal this.

title: P1: sidekick agents hand-appended pseudo-comment JSONL records to .beads/issues.jsonl — br 0.2.16 import breaks repo-wide
description: issues.jsonl contained 12 hand-crafted 'comment' records in 3 shapes ({id:<issue>,comment:...}, {issue_id:...,id:139,author,text}, {id:99995,issue_id:...}) appended by sidekick-reliability-boundary-0725 lanes. br 0.2.16 fails ALL commands: 'missing field title' / 'Duplicate issue id' / 'invalid type: integer'. Repaired 2026-07-14: records extracted to .beads/recovered-comments-20260714.jsonl, JSONL regenerated via br sync --flush-only --force (302 valid lines = db count). Fix: forbid raw JSONL appends in sidekick prompts (br CLI only); consider br upgrade if comments are a real newer-version feature.

title: Exclude ezgha-runner:latest from weekly docker image prune -af cron
description:
Root-caused 2026-07-14 Mac outage investigation (image-loss lane): ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.jleechan.cleanup-docker.plist runs a weekly launchd job (Sundays 03:30 UTC) executing `docker image prune -af` via cleanup-docker.sh on jleechan's Mac. This is a system-wide cron NOT owned by ez-gh-actions, but it is the demonstrated vector for ezgha-runner:latest vanishing (execution log shows real deletions: 699.7MB 2026-06-28, 567.7MB 2026-07-05). Since ezgha-runner:latest is a locally-built tagged image with no running/stopped container reference between fleet cycles, `-af` (all unused images, not just dangling) reclaims it.

Fix: modify cleanup-docker.sh to skip images matching `ezgha-runner` (e.g. `docker image prune -af --filter 'label!=ezgha-keep'` after labelling the build, or an explicit `docker images --format '{{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}}' | grep -v ezgha-runner | xargs -n1 docker rmi` style allow-list, or simplest: change prune to `docker image prune -f` scoped with a repository exclude, or tag the image and reference it from a placeholder container so `-a` won't touch it.

Owner surface: cleanup-docker.sh (lives outside this repo, likely ~/.local/bin or ~/scripts — locate via <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
<plist version="1.0">
<dict>
  <key>Label</key>
  <string>com.jleechan.cleanup-docker</string>

title: Mac disk pressure: reclaim ~56GB stale git worktrees (95% full, 54GB free)
description:
2026-07-14 outage investigation (disk-consumer lane) found the Mac's Data volume at 95% capacity (895G used / 54G avail, df -H /System/Volumes/Data). Verification found 151 git-worktree-prefixed directories under ~/projects (worktree_*, wt-*, _wt<n>*, _wf<n>*) totaling ~56GB; 95 of them untouched >=7 days (~27GB), 51 untouched >=14 days (oldest 22 days, ~15GB). Confirmed as real `git worktree` checkouts via `git worktree list`, each carrying its own venv/node_modules (~1GB avg). Several are `locked` (worldarchitect-44, -45, -57 observed) and must be skipped/confirmed before removal.

IMPORTANT CAVEAT from verification: this disk pressure was investigated as a possible link in today's ezgha outage causal chain and that causal link was REFUTED — the outage's actual blockers (Colima stale-state + watchdog no-op, image loss via prune cron) are unrelated to host disk fullness, and the Colima VM disk itself was confirmed NOT recreated/resized-on-disk. This bead is a real, separate, latent-risk cleanup item (95% full is objectively risky) but is NOT proven to have caused the 2026-07-14 outage — do not conflate the two in future incident write-ups.

Fix: ASK USER first, then per-worktree `git worktree list` + branch/PR merge-status check; for merged/closed and unlocked entries, `git worktree remove <path>` (never raw `rm -rf`, which leaves dangling refs in the parent .git/worktrees). Owner: ops runbook, user-approved. Blast radius: per-worktree removal only affects that worktree's checkout; locked/active worktrees must be excluded.

title: Investigate high Mac reboot cadence (~10 reboots in ~3 weeks) -- self-outage risk for ezgha Mac fleet
description:
2026-07-14 Mac outage root-cause investigation (reboot-trigger lane) confirmed a genuine reboot at 14:13 PT today via wtmp plus pmset boot-marker plus uptime cross-check (all three independently corroborate). Proposed fix from that lane flagged a broader pattern: this MacBook has rebooted approximately 10 times in the last approximately 3 weeks. Every one of those reboots re-triggers the full ezgha-outage causal chain (Colima left Stopped, then watchdog gap, then fleet down until manual/auto recovery), so reboot frequency IS a first-class reliability driver for the 6 Mac ezgha runners, not just an isolated incident.

Open question this bead should resolve: is the box being manually restarted during normal interactive dev work (npm/jest/chrome-headless-shell/ios-simulator/cmux sessions were active immediately before at least one of these reboots), or is there a common automatic trigger (OS update, thermal/watchdog, power event) across all approximately 10 timestamps? The 2026-07-14 reboot itself has an OPEN CONTRADICTION: softwareupdate --history shows no OS update install today, but boot-time forensics (Installer Progress UI / IOKit Boot phase plus postinstall daemon activity at 14:14:20-14:14:29) suggest an update DID apply during this boot -- this reboot's trigger is not cleanly attributed and should be resolved as part of this investigation (or a fresh log collect archive analyzed offline, since the live unified-log store showed a "Wall Clock adjustment detected" warning that degrades query reliability across the reboot boundary).

If root cause is manual/interactive-workflow driven, the durable fix is process isolation: run the 6 Mac ezgha runners on a machine/session that is never used as the interactive daily-driver, or add a pre-restart guard/confirmation when ezgha-managed containers are active. Owner: ops runbook / host architecture decision (needs user sign-off, not a pure code fix). Blast radius: none for investigation phase; the eventual fix (dedicated host or restart guard) has real cost/inconvenience tradeoffs requiring explicit approval.

title: Relocate aggregate runner dashboard into ez-gh-actions
description: Canonical runner dashboard relocation from misplaced worldarchitect.ai PR https://github.com/jleechanorg/worldarchitect.ai/pull/8409 and issue https://github.com/jleechanorg/worldarchitect.ai/issues/8405. Branch codex/runner-dashboard-relocation-20260714. Requires aggregate-only public schema, exact 6 Mac + 16 Linux local-capacity truth, fail-closed staleness, transactional launchd install, CI contracts, evidence, replacement PR, and close/move links. No live service mutation or merge.

2026-07-15 merge review reopened: /advice and source audit found the dashboard disk probe covers Docker-daemon space but omits the outer host filesystem gate enforced by src/docker_backend.rs (40 GiB minimum on macOS). This can report healthy during a real spawn refusal. Fixing on PR 81 with a regression test before merge.

title: XML-escape DOCKER_HOST in generic macOS auxiliary plists
description: Cold cross-model review of ez-gh-actions PR https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/pull/81 found install.sh:397 embeds DOCKER_HOST_OVERRIDE directly in XML for generic token-refresh/queue-reaper/watchdog plists. Repro value unix:///fixture/colima&primary.sock yields invalid XML. Provenance: git blame origin/main attributes the line to pre-existing commit 60e59d8b; PR #81 dashboard path delegates to launchd/install-launchagents.sh and uses plistlib with an ampersand fixture, so this is not a PR #81 blocker. Fix shared generic installer with plistlib/XML escaping and add direct install.sh coverage.

title: Mac 40 GiB host reserve blocks fleet refill; separate alert threshold from spawn admission
description: ROOT CAUSE (2026-07-15 live Mac outage): commit 46dd073 introduced MACOS_HOST_DISK_FLOOR_GB=40 and max(configured,40) in src/docker_backend.rs. The dual host+guest filesystem probe is valid, but /history found 40 originated as an 'e.g. free<40G' disk-magician cleanup trigger and was copied into ezgha without runner/image/scratch/APFS capacity derivation. Live proof: after rebuilding shared 3.35GB ezgha-runner:latest and fstrim, host was 43 GiB free; daemon spawned 6/6 and reported busy=6 with 84 queued. Normal job scratch growth dropped host below 40, then ephemeral completions/reclaims reduced local executing capacity 6->4->3 while 34-38 GiB remained free; admission refused every refill. Narrow fix: keep both daemon-volume and /System/Volumes/Data probes, apply configured min_free_disk_gb to admission (live config 5; default 10) until a capacity-derived Mac reserve exists, and keep 40 only as cleanup/alert threshold. Tests must prove six-slot refill is not fleet-wide disabled at 39 GiB and retain true low-space fail-closed behavior. Provenance: https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/commit/46dd07304efa7d74097d4ab57bac20123f7c8657 ; existing related cleanup bead jleechan-er7x; exact runtime /tmp/ezgha-launchd-stderr.log.
close_reason: Merged PR #85, deployed exact main f388a8b, and proved real 5->6 refill at 10 GiB host-free.

At 11:02:37Z host free was 11 GiB and slot 5 exited (fleet 5/6); replacement container appeared by 11:02:47Z; at 11:02:57Z with 10 GiB free, slot 5 reached Runner.Worker PID 653528 and fleet returned 6/6. title: Mac Colima trim guard: reclaim discarded CI blocks before APFS admission outage
description: ROOT CAUSE 2026-07-15: delayed thin-disk discard, separate from admission threshold. /System/Volumes/Data repeatedly fell to 14-20 GiB under six short-job --rm runners while Colima guest still had ~43 GiB free. Manual 'colima ssh -- sudo fstrim -av' non-destructively reclaimed 7, 5.7, 4, 19.7, and 27.3 GiB potential blocks and restored host free immediately. Guest fstrim.timer is weekly (next ~4 days), far slower than CI growth. Implement repo-owned Mac-only trim guard: poll 5m, early trigger host<40GiB, derive exact Colima profile fail-closed from launchd DOCKER_HOST, estimate reclaimable sparse allocated-minus-guest-used and require >=1GiB, singleton + 15m cooldown, bounded <=60s fstrim only /mnt/lima-colima and /, log before/after. Never docker prune/rm, never start/stop/restart Colima, fail closed on identity/mount/discard ambiguity. Add launchd template/install/uninstall and stub tests plus disposable-profile real proof; cross-link disk-magician pressure sweep but do not reuse its broad destructive cleanup. Provenance: /tmp/ezgha-launchd-stderr.log, live 2026-07-15 recovery, jleechan-9rpn, jleechan-er7x.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Reproduce and isolate the pre-stage hang before colima_status using exact deployed script and launchd environment. 2. A dead-PID lock is recovered safely and a live lock still excludes concurrent runs. 3. Every invocation exits within 60 seconds, and structured events identify all pre-stage operations so no outer_timeout can occur without a named failing step. 4. Under six real short-job runners, observe at least two launchd polls plus a triggered trim; no poll wedges and the next poll still runs. 5. Preserve fail-closed Colima identity, mount, and discard checks; never prune Docker data or start, stop, or restart Colima. 6. Deploy from exact origin/main, verify installed script hash matches the commit, and publish before/after host allocation, guest used, host free, and event-log evidence.
notes: The proactive threshold policy remains tracked separately in jleechan-6547. This regression bead owns the guard execution/liveness failure and deployed proof.

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: 2026-07-15 live correction to initial design: fixed 15-minute cooldown is unsafe. Manual fstrim at 10:26Z reclaimed 15.6 GiB and raised host free 21->30 GiB, but active CI drove it back to 21 GiB by 10:29:51Z; follow-up trims reclaimed 7.2 and 9.7 GiB. At observed burn rate the host crosses the interim 15 GiB floor in ~5.5 minutes and configured 5 GiB floor in ~9 minutes. PR #86 review therefore requires pressure/success-aware cooldown or a lower emergency threshold bypass, plus regression and real-callstack proof. PR: https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/pull/86

Cross-repo incident beads: disk_magician jleechan-wy3s and jleechan-etjw.

title: Gate 3 compares NanoCpus to configured cap instead of effective VM-derived share
description: 2026-07-15 exact-head deploy 8624d3c: live config limits.cpus=4.0, vm_total_mb=24576, runner.count=6; daemon correctly created new containers at HostConfig.NanoCpus=1670000000 after aggregate CPU-envelope derivation, but docs/verify-exit-criteria.sh lines 495-531 computes EXPECTED_NANO_CPUS directly from configured limits.cpus and Gate 3 fails expecting 4000000000. All six slots had live Runner.Worker processes; this is a verifier false negative. Fix verifier/doctor to derive the same effective CPU limit as production or query an authoritative preview, with regression for configured cap > per-runner daemon share. Provenance: ./docs/verify-exit-criteria.sh run 2026-07-15T10:34Z on commit https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/commit/8624d3c7f02bbaace94dbcd418af3293693c8635.

title: Repo Secret Scan inherits generic self-hosted label and starves behind org queue
description: 2026-07-15 PR #85 Secret Scan repeatedly remained queued while test-on-ezgha completed and six Mac runners churned. Root cause: org Actions variable SELF_HOSTED_RUNNER_LABELS was explicitly ["self-hosted"], overriding .github/workflows/gitleaks.yml fallback ["self-hosted","ezgha"]. A repo-scoped override was created as ["self-hosted","ezgha"] at 10:51Z; rerun attempt 3 exposed both labels and completed on ez-mac-runner-b-2 at 10:55:40Z. Scope is only jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions; org variable unchanged. Track the external configuration and add a doctor/documentation assertion so deletion cannot silently restore generic-label starvation.

title: P2: docker build (BuildKit) silently produces incomplete apt package lists — Dockerfile.runner build failed 3x, DOCKER_BUILDKIT=0 fixed it
description: 2026-07-16: docker build -f Dockerfile.runner failed repeatedly with 'python3-venv has no installation candidate' even with --no-cache, while a plain 'docker run ... apt-get update && apt-get install python3-venv' in the SAME base image succeeded immediately. DOCKER_BUILDKIT=0 (legacy builder) built successfully on first try. Root cause not fully isolated — suspect BuildKit's build-context network/DNS path differs from a running container's runtime network path on this colima/vz setup, or a stale BuildKit builder-side apt cache surviving --no-cache. Workaround: prefix any Dockerfile.runner rebuild with DOCKER_BUILDKIT=0 if buildx-path apt installs fail unexpectedly. Also noted: the main ezgha launchd job was found fully unloaded (not crashed — 'launchctl list' showed no entry) after ~5h of healthy operation on 2026-07-16 ~01:2x PT with no operator action recorded in this session; re-bootstrapped successfully. Cause of the unload is unconfirmed — flag if it recurs.

title: Validate Mac job routing and refresh top 10 WorldArchitect PR branches
description: User-directed 2026-07-16 operation: dispatch Mac-specific real jobs, prove ez-mac-runner-b-* execution locally, rank live open jleechanorg/worldarchitect.ai PRs by importance, and update the top ten branches onto current main with exact-head verification.
close_reason: Mac-specific jobs succeeded one-to-one across ez-mac-runner-b-1..6; top ten selected WorldArchitect PR branches updated via CAS onto main b239acab and verified behind_by=0 with exact-head workflow runs.

title: Rotate launchd-inherited GitHub token exposed by diagnostics
description: Provenance 2026-07-16 Mac runner recovery: raw 'launchctl print gui/501/org.jleechanorg.ezgha' output included inherited GITHUB_TOKEN/AO_BOT_GH_TOKEN values before the wrapper unsets them. The service wrapper prevents use by ezgha, but operator diagnostics can disclose the live credential in transcripts. Rotate the credential, remove secret-bearing inherited launchd environment, and update doctor/diagnostic guidance to extract only state/pid/exit fields instead of printing the full environment. Do not record the token value in the bead.

title: Raise Colima trim trigger above the 40 GiB emergency floor
description: 2026-07-17 post-fix audit: scripts/colima-trim-guard.sh hard-codes HOST_TRIGGER_KIB=40 GiB. The incident snapshot had 46 GiB free and the final live check had 76.2 GiB free (92% Data used), so even a healthy guard intentionally skips at both states. The stale-lock/timeout regression is fixed by https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/commit/043536db61e355fab5ecd9b917e9c79f9d5db236, but it does not satisfy the user expectation that 92-95% full should be prevented. Derive a proactive trigger/hysteresis from host capacity and observed CI burn/reclaim rate; retain reclaimable>=1GiB, fail-closed identity, bounded stage, and backoff safeguards. Provenance: scripts/colima-trim-guard.sh:42, disk snapshot 2026-07-17T18:58:58Z (830 GiB used/46 free), final df 837541288 KiB used/79868680 KiB free.
notes: Correction 2026-07-17: this bead was independently (and duplicatively) re-created as jleechan-uio3 in a different br scope (disk_magician cwd) by an agent that couldn't see this repo-scoped bead from outside ~/projects_other/ez-gh-actions -- br beads are scoped by source_repo_path/cwd, not globally visible. jleechan-uio3 was closed as 'resolved differently': ez-gh-actions commit 688a798 overrides the guest's fstrim.timer to run every 5min (was weekly) AND fixes fstrim.service's ExecStart to use --all instead of --listed-in /etc/fstab (which silently skipped /mnt/lima-colima entirely). Verified live: Data volume 837.0->791.7 GiB, Colima 49.53->4.19 GiB. This directly addresses the disk-growth symptom this bead was filed for, though it does NOT literally raise the 40 GiB host-free trigger as originally scoped -- it makes routine trimming independent of that trigger entirely (runs on a fixed 5-min guest-side timer regardless of host free space), which is arguably the same outcome via a different mechanism. Leaving open for the original scoped ask (an explicit hysteresis/proactive-threshold change to colima-trim-guard.sh itself) in case that's still wanted on top of the guest-native fix.

title: Move high-churn runner workspaces and caches out of ephemeral Docker writable layers
description: ROOT CAUSE evidence 2026-07-17: all six ez-mac-runner-b-* containers have zero mounts, so checkouts, build scratch, and caches live in ephemeral overlay writable layers. Runner b-5 alone reached SizeRw=1741754368 bytes and /home about 2188824 KiB. Across one measured 60-second churn interval, aggregate runner writable layers grew 1125425152 bytes, Colima host allocation grew 515264 KiB, and macOS host used grew 518664 KiB with matching container die/destroy/create/start events. Colima host allocation was about 55.93 GiB while actual guest-used filesystems totaled only about 6.81 GiB, proving repeated runner writes plus delayed discard inflate the sparse disk. Design managed workspace and high-churn cache storage with bounded cleanup and isolation; do not merely move unbounded growth onto the host. This is the producer-side child of disk_magician jleechan-etjw and is distinct from jleechan-9ofp guard liveness, jleechan-6547 threshold policy, and jleechan-yov cold-start optimization.
design: Prefer existing Docker bind/volume mechanisms and current runner lifecycle hooks. Start with the smallest safe mount set: per-job workspace plus explicitly allowlisted package/build caches. Avoid a shared writable Git tree and avoid any cache that can retain credentials or executable state across trust boundaries. Include a rollback path to current ephemeral storage.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Capture a disposable representative job baseline: container mounts, SizeRw, /home and _work size, Colima host allocation, guest used, and host free before and after. 2. Runner containers use explicit managed mounts for _work and the highest-churn tool caches, or a documented equivalent that keeps transient job writes out of the container writable layer. 3. Cleanup and retention are bounded by age and size, preserve per-job isolation, prevent cross-job source or credential contamination, and never touch Qdrant or protected session stores. 4. Run six concurrent real jobs through container turnover; prove writable-layer growth is materially reduced and Colima host allocation stabilizes or remains bounded by verified discard. 5. Stop/recreate one disposable runner and prove slot refill, checkout correctness, cache permissions, and job cleanup without manual intervention. 6. Deploy from exact origin/main and observe at least 30 minutes of active churn with timestamped before/after evidence for host allocation, guest used, aggregate SizeRw, host free, and trim events.
notes:
2026-07-18 update: Phase 2 shipped and live-verified. workspace_host_path
config field mounts a per-runner-name subdir RW at /home/runner/_work,
wiped before every container start (fail-open if unconfigured). Deployed
to production: docker inspect on a real recreated container
(ez-mac-runner-b-6) confirmed the bind, docker exec as uid 1001 confirmed
real read-write, and a full remove+respawn cycle confirmed the wipe-before-
start guarantee against the live daemon (not just unit tests). Hit and
fixed a real deployment incident along the way: a raw `cp` of the release
binary over the deployed ~/.cargo/bin/ezgha broke macOS code-signing
provenance and caused an immediate SIGKILL crash-loop; fixed with
`codesign --sign - --force`. Commits: see docs/design/disk-churn-reduction-
swarm-20260717.md Phase 2 section.

Still open: acceptance criterion 1 (before/after writable-layer bytes on a
real representative pip-heavy job) not yet captured -- needs an actual
worldarchitect.ai-style job to land on this fleet while someone is
watching docker stats / SizeRw.
2026-07-19 REGRESSION FOUND + FIXED: workspace_host_path mount (Phase 2) broke setup-python/setup-node/setup-gcloud on Mac fleet (41%, 13/32 job failure rate over ~1-2 days) -- root cause confirmed via /swarm + direct live repro: tar extraction of an archive containing a symlink corrupts the symlink into an unreadable 0-byte mode-000 file when destination is this virtiofs-backed mount on Colima/vz (plain ln -s works fine; extraction into container's own overlay fs works fine; only tar-extracting a real archive onto virtiofs corrupts symlink members). Confirmed with actions/setup-python's own tarball, which the runner extracts into _work/_actions when downloading the action.

FIX (commit 0d5a802, pushed to main): tmpfs-shadow the three fixed runner-internal cache dirs (_actions, _temp, _tool) inside the workspace mount -- keeps runner's own action/tool-cache extraction off virtiofs while checkouts/build scratch (the actual disk-churn win, living under _work/<owner>/<repo>) unaffected. TDD: tests/workspace_mount_symlink_extraction_test.sh (Layer 2 real-Docker, RED->GREEN) + docker_backend::tests::workspace_mount_shadows_actions_temp_tool_with_tmpfs (Layer 1 unit). 318/318 unit tests pass, clippy clean.

NOT YET DEPLOYED to the running daemon: Mac host load average was 62/124/231 at fix time -- deferred per Gate 0 safety check (load_1min>12 threshold), cargo install --path . + systemctl restart still needed once load recovers.

title: Monitor completed self-hosted job success rate by runner prefix
description: Provenance: 2026-07-18 virtiofs outage saga STATE.md reports 13/32 Mac jobs failed while doctor-runner activity gates still read busy/healthy. Existing paths: doctor-runner section 7 only prints six ezgha-selftest conclusions without a verdict; src/canary.rs records synthetic canary conclusions; src/queue_monitor.rs enumerates only queued/in_progress jobs and is already API-budget-sensitive; dashboard probes local capacity only. Add an objective completed-job conclusion window outside the daemon serve loop, with explicit UNKNOWN on fetch/truncation, bounded API cost, runner-prefix attribution, RED-before-GREEN tests, and minimal later integration into doctor/dashboard.

title: P0: jeff-ubuntu silently underprovisioned at 10/16 runners with stale deployed SHA
description: Live provenance 2026-07-18 from /Users/jleechan/projects_other/ez-gh-actions during virtiofs-saga root-cause mission. Repo policy requires 16 Linux + 6 Mac = 22 slots and forbids explaining away any shortfall. Read-only evidence: ssh jeff-ubuntu doctor-runner reported runner.count=10, slot_assignments=10, and 10/10 IDLE-OK; /home/jleechan/.config/ezgha/config.toml line 11 is count = 10 with mtime 2026-07-13 03:22 PDT; journal at service start says runner_count=10, fleet_budget_mb=31971, per_runner_budget_mb=3197; Linux checkout and installed binary are dc13770 while live GitHub origin/main is f240b7d. Related beads ez-gh-actions-ah94 and ez-gh-actions-qyzo explicitly recorded runner count 16 unchanged / count reduction gated on user signoff; searches found no tracker item authorizing 10. Queue was empty at observation, so this is latent capacity loss rather than current starvation. Root-cause who/why count changed, reconcile memory-safety constraints, restore/approve capacity explicitly, deploy current provenance, and prove 16 Linux plus 6 Mac under a real busy burst. Do not change the user config without human authorization; this bead records the live defect and exact evidence.
close_reason: Closed as invalid/stale-contract finding: operator-selected 10-runner Jeff-Ubuntu contract is documented in /Users/jleechan/roadmap/nextsteps-2026-07-12-ezgha-10-runner-host-availability.md and tracked for repo contract reconciliation by jleechan-f621. The dc13770-to-current-main deployment skew remains separate provenance evidence, not proof that count=10 is unauthorized.

title: Replace stale 16-runner Linux fleet contract with 10-runner contract

title: sidekick: virtiofs-saga-root-cause
description:
STATE.md at /tmp/ez-gh-actions/sidekick/virtiofs-saga-root-cause/STATE.md — mission: root-cause Mac ez-gh-actions runner outage saga + fix the checkout-path symlink-extraction gap via TDD/4layer. Two sidekicks in flight: codex tmux (lanes A doctor-runner-parser-fix, B read-only-dir repro, C job-outcome monitor) + this in-session claude teammate (fix implementation).

UPDATE 2026-07-19/20 (fix-implementation teammate): checkout-path tar-wrapper fix shipped in two revisions. v1 (commit 243e1a5) added EZGHA_VIRTIOFS_WORKSPACE=1 env wiring plus a naive stage-then-copy tar wrapper, TDD Layer1+Layer2 green. Lane B's adversarial review found real bugs (path-boundary false-positive, keep-old-files overwrite-protection defeated) and a doc overclaim (npm/pip/docker don't use PATH tar, confirmed unaffected by this bug). Fixing those bugs revealed v2's "only stage empty destinations" approach never engages in production since actions/checkout always populates the destination first. Redesigned as v3 (commit 4970b5e): mirror pre-existing destination content into the tmpfs stage first, extract, copy back, propagate tar's real exit code -- fixes the realistic populated-checkout case while preserving keep-old-files semantics. Also fixed bead jleechan-krow (noexec tmpfs shadow blocker) in the same commit since it was a small RED-tested one-liner blocking the shared test suite. Documented (not fixed, genuinely out of scope) two further narrow limitations around mode-000 archive members -- see docker/tar-workspace-wrapper.sh header plus CLAUDE.md for full detail. 319/319 cargo tests pass, clippy clean, Layer 2 real-Docker suite 7/7. DEPLOY BLOCKED on host load average (89-131 1-min avg observed, repo threshold is 12 or less) -- fix is committed, pushed, and tested but NOT yet live on the running fleet. Next session/lane should poll uptime and deploy (cargo install plus restart plus live verify via docker inspect/exec) once load drops.

ADDENDUM (from the separate read-only-dirs verification teammate, not the codex tmux lane): their probe covered mode-0555 dirs, mode-0444 files, and symlinks -- perms alone never corrupted, only symlink members did. They did not test mode-000 regular files directly, so cannot independently confirm that finding, but note it is plausibly the SAME root mechanism as the symlink bug (tar's write-then-restrictive-permission-fixup step breaking specifically on virtiofs) rather than a truly separate mechanism -- flagged here so a future reader does not assume the two are unrelated just because this bead's fix only targets symlinks.

DEPLOYED AND LIVE-VERIFIED 2026-07-18 ~19:17 PT: per a relayed standing user directive (don't gate routine deploys on this Mac's chronic load average), ran cargo install + launchctl kickstart. Recycled one confirmed-idle runner to get a fresh post-restart container. Confirmed via docker inspect (EZGHA_VIRTIOFS_WORKSPACE=1 present, tmpfs shadows show exec) and a real functional test inside the live container: extracted a symlink archive into a pre-populated checkout-path destination (simulating post-checkout state) -- symlink extracted correctly, pre-existing content preserved, confirmed both in-container and on the host-side virtiofs mount. doctor-runner shows fleet fully healthy (Mac 6/6, Linux 10/10, no DOWN/STARVED); one unrelated gate (Mac real-execution check) read 0/6 due to all 6 sampled selftest runs happening to route to Linux this window, not any actual failure -- confirmed all 6 succeeded, just on the other host. The checkout-path virtiofs symlink fix is now confirmed live and working in production, not just in tests.

THIRD adversarial finding + fix (2026-07-18 ~19:20-19:26 PT): a different in-session teammate (not the codex tmux lane) independently reviewed v3's mirror logic and found GNU tar's multi-C per-member directory targeting broke silently -- the wrapper collapsed all -C flags into one trailing -C, which GNU tar ignores when nothing follows it, causing rc=0 (looks successful) while both real destinations ended up empty. Worse than the disclosed fallback ceiling: silent success with wrong output. Fixed by detecting 2+ -C occurrences and bailing to native tar unmodified before staging. Verified live: wrapped and unwrapped now produce identical output. Committed+pushed as 16ae5fc, deployed, and live-verified on a fresh production container (rc=0, both destinations correctly populated). 319/319 unit tests, clippy clean, Layer 2 suite 8/8. Fleet confirmed 6/6 Mac + 10/10 Linux healthy. This fix has now survived three independent adversarial review rounds, each finding and fixing a real, distinct bug -- a future reader should keep testing live rather than trusting the header comment at face value.

WIND-DOWN 2026-07-18 ~19:28 PT: team-lead independently spot-checked (binary embeds 16ae5fc, multi-C guard present with clear rationale) and confirmed this teammate's assigned scope (the FIX DIRECTIVE) is complete. This teammate (sidekick-virtiofs-saga-root-cause-2) is now winding down. Bead left OPEN (not closed) since it is a shared resumption bead for the broader multi-lane mission (job-outcome monitor, root-cause synthesis doc, still-open beads yib3/kobt/apye) owned by the codex tmux lane -- whoever drives overall mission completion should close this bead. Final artifact state: commits 243e1a5 -> 4970b5e -> 16ae5fc on ez-gh-actions main, docker/tar-workspace-wrapper.sh + docker_backend.rs + tests/workspace_mount_symlink_extraction_test.sh + CLAUDE.md all updated and deployed; fleet confirmed healthy (6/6 Mac, 10/10 Linux) after final deploy.
close_reason: Mission complete: docs/design/mac-runner-virtiofs-saga-root-cause-2026-07.md written and committed (1cf69de), covering the full saga (5-cause outage cascade, disk-churn fix, symlink-corruption regression + 3-round fix, prevention gaps, harness-drift meta-lesson) with commit/bead evidence citations. Adversarially reviewed by a separate Sonnet subagent: PASS WITH NITS (all SHAs/bead-IDs/technical claims independently verified correct; nits were cosmetic date/line-count drift, explicitly non-blocking). Bead triage complete: jleechan-krow closed (fix confirmed live); jleechan-yib3/kobt/apye/frzq left open (still accurately tracked); jleechan-pl2t/f621 already correctly closed by a prior lane, untouched.

title: P0: tmpfs workspace shadows are noexec and block setup runtimes/actions
description: Regression in commit 0d5a802's optionless tmpfs shadows for /home/runner/_work/_actions, _temp, and _tool. Independent live read-only inspection of production ez-mac-runner-b-1 plus daemon-shaped throwaway containers shows all three mounts are rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec. Minimal executable copied mode0755 into each returns rc126 Permission denied; an actual ARM64 /usr/bin/python3 copied into the setup-python tool-cache shape under _tool also returns rc126. This means the tar/symlink mitigation restores extraction but breaks execution compatibility. Narrow proven correction: emit --tmpfs /home/runner/_work/{_actions,_temp,_tool}:exec for all three; throwaway mountinfo retains nosuid,nodev, removes noexec, executables run rc0, and setup-python-v5 tar extraction preserves symlinks. Acceptance: TDD Layer 1 asserts exact :exec daemon args for all three; Layer 2 real Docker proves raw virtiofs corruption, shadow extraction success, and executable rc0 in all three; cargo test/clippy clean; adversarial review; safe daemon deploy plus fresh real container mountinfo/exec and completed real setup job. Related parent jleechan-93cf. Do not weaken the per-start workspace wipe.
close_reason: Fix confirmed live: :exec added to all three tmpfs shadow mounts in commit 4970b5e, verified present at src/docker_backend.rs:2163, deployed and live-verified twice (docker inspect + real exec test in production containers). Closed during final bead triage for the virtiofs-saga root-cause synthesis doc (docs/design/mac-runner-virtiofs-saga-root-cause-2026-07.md).

title: Wire mac-virtiofs-canary.yml with real nested-docker access (docker.sock forwarding)
description: The new .github/workflows/mac-virtiofs-canary.yml (PR https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/pull/91) dispatches tests/workspace_mount_symlink_extraction_test.sh to the real Mac fleet (confirmed via runner_name ez-mac-runner-b-2, run 29673346085), but the ephemeral runner containers have no docker socket/DOCKER_HOST forwarded in, so the test's nested 'docker run' calls can't reach a daemon and it SKIPs rather than exercising real assertions. Need to forward the host docker socket (or equivalent) into the ez-mac-runner-b-* containers so this canary actually exercises the virtiofs symlink-corruption assertions instead of just proving dispatch plumbing.

title: Reconcile stale runner labels in config/config.toml.mac.example vs live deployed config
description: config/config.toml.mac.example documents labels = ["self-hosted", "self-hosted-mikey", "ezgha", "macOS", "ARM64"], but the live deployed config at ~/Library/Application Support/org.jleechanorg.ezgha/config.toml actually registers runners with 'self-hosted-macos' instead of 'macOS'/'ARM64'. Discovered while building PR https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/pull/91 -- using the example file's labels verbatim in a new workflow's runs-on would have caused it to queue forever against a label combo no live runner actually has. Update the example file to match the real deployed config (or vice versa, whichever is the intended canonical set) so future workflow authors don't hit the same trap.

title: workspace_host_path is currently disabled fleet-wide on Mac (rev-izqjg EBUSY rollback) -- re-enable or accept as permanently off
description: workspace_host_path (the bind-mount feature that the virtiofs symlink-corruption bug and its fix, commits 0d5a802/4970b5e/16ae5fc, all pertain to) is currently disabled fleet-wide in the live Mac config due to a prior EBUSY rollback (rev-izqjg). Discovered while building PR https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/pull/91 (mac-virtiofs-canary.yml): with the feature off, tests/workspace_mount_symlink_extraction_test.sh has nothing to actually exercise even once nested-docker access is fixed (jleechan-h6la). Need a decision: re-enable workspace_host_path (root-causing the EBUSY rollback first) or formally accept it as permanently off, in which case the virtiofs canary and its underlying fix are dead code protecting against a disabled feature.

title: Make PR 68 shell-test CI self-hosted and honestly hermetic
description:

- Update fleet fixtures to 10 Linux plus 6 Mac.
- Ensure classifier tests stub Docker at the command boundary and prove all four states from the real extracted classifier.

Acceptance criteria:
- rg confirms no ubuntu-latest in private-repo workflows unless explicitly approved.
- CI runner executes every hermetic tests/*.sh and reports every failure without last-status masking.
- Host-dependent tests are explicitly classified and separately gated.
- Fresh production /er PASS, /green, and /advice APPROVE exist before merge.

title: Redesign PR 70 so recovery mutation is owned by one persistent locked controller
description:
Context:
- PR: https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/pull/70
- Head: b6224b073d499303ab1ac2ffec874e3004ae4f58
- Reviews: https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/pull/70#discussion_r3565581865, https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/pull/70#discussion_r3565581866, and https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/pull/70#discussion_r3565586900
- Existing bead: ez-gh-actions-ghd2.7.
- Finding: restart mutation occurs before the controller lock and manual lockout policy, controller state is recreated per call, and socket health uses regular-file open semantics. The PR also defers required convergence criteria.
- Staleness note: verified at the PR head on 2026-07-19; re-read current restart ownership before redesign.

Implementation:
- One persistent RecoveryController instance must own classification, lock acquisition, policy checks, restart mutation, health polling, and transition recording.
- Move the actual restart command inside the locked attempt closure; no caller may mutate the backend before policy approval.
- Probe Unix sockets by connection using the existing platform/backend helper, not File::open.
- Do not arm launchd/systemd units until all original convergence acceptance criteria and dependency beads are satisfied.

Acceptance criteria:
- RED-before-GREEN proves manual lockout, cooldown, and concurrent lock contention prevent the restart command itself from running.
- State persists across multiple serve-loop calls in one process.
- Layer 2 disposable backend tests prove two successful Docker polls plus configured container and job-pickup convergence before success.
- PR is rebased or replaced, conflicts resolved, full tests pass, and fresh production /er PASS, /green, and /advice APPROVE exist before merge.

This bead exists only to record: (1) the fmt fix landed, (2) the sync gap recurred a second time and needs a durable fix (likely: sidekick/lane worktrees should never run 'br create' locally -- route bead creation through the main/shared repo checkout, or br needs a real cross-worktree sync mechanism).

title: Restrict public fork PR access to self-hosted CI
description: Provenance: review of PR https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/pull/66 at head 2104bc154179c65ed2fcd508c70a29a36c516833 on 2026-07-19. Existing .github/workflows/ci-selfhosted.yml uses unrestricted pull_request even though its comment states fork PRs must not reach self-hosted runners; main commit 6d5b1dd already contained the mismatch. Acceptance: add a server-side fork-safe condition or trusted-event design, prove same-repo PRs run and fork PRs cannot acquire self-hosted jobs, keep private-repo cost policy, and document exact-head evidence.
notes: 2026-07-19 follow-up provenance: PR 66 repair also found origin/main .github/workflows/gitleaks.yml schedules pull_request on self-hosted runners in this public repo. Expand audit/acceptance from ci-selfhosted.yml to every public pull_request workflow using self-hosted labels. Current PR 66 remains bounded to its introduced provenance gate; existing gitleaks exposure must be fixed under this P0 bead with trusted-code/fork-denial proof.

title: Ship read-only dual-Lima diagnostic and rollback-marker foundation
description:
Provenance: https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/pull/67 at 4631f243a3b44cccfc7fb68e9c6a9a6117156f26; child scope of ez-gh-actions-apye.

Acceptance criteria:
- Detect canonical and legacy Lima instances from filesystem markers without starting/stopping either VM.
- Resolve a named Unix Docker context and label fallback provenance explicitly.
- Prefer an alive canonical socket; report job_drain_required when migration from a live legacy socket is possible.
- Keep the command read-only by default.
- Permit rollback-marker writes only with explicit verified provenance; preserve corrupt prior state and write atomically.
- Prove the exact binary on macOS and jeff-ubuntu without changing Docker contexts or services.

Out of scope: performing Docker context migration, quiescing the live daemon, restarting services, or sustained 10 Linux + 6 Mac execution proof; those remain on parent ez-gh-actions-apye.

title: doctor-runner execution gate contradicts IDLE-OK four-state contract
description: Provenance: authoritative ./doctor-runner on 2026-07-19 after merged PR https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/pull/64 commit https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/commit/6cc98b16dabeff6371e3f678701278fa791c6811. Live queue was drained (0 queued), all 6 Mac + 10 Linux slots were accounted with 0 DOWN/0 CYCLING, 15 were correctly classified IDLE-OK and one Linux slot EXECUTING, yet doctor-runner exited 1 because compute_execution_slot_critical requires 16/16 Runner.Worker processes. This contradicts AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md and bead jleechan-hca0: IDLE-OK is healthy; exit nonzero only for DOWN or IDLE-STARVED. Root cause is PR64 lines 1324-1342 replacing the live-slot gate with an unconditional execution-only gate. Acceptance: RED-before-GREEN proves 1 executing + 15 IDLE-OK + zero queue passes, missing/down or IDLE-STARVED fails through the existing critical path, 10 Linux + 6 Mac totals remain authoritative, doctor-runner live run reports no false execution-slot failure, exact-head /er and /advice pass before merge.

title: Mac runner NanoCpus mismatch: daemon spawns 1.67e9, Gate 3 expects 4e9
description: 2026-07-19 evidence: fresh Mac containers spawned by NEW binary (ce93fb9/4b5b80d) still get HostConfig.NanoCpus=1670000000 while docs/verify-exit-criteria.sh Gate 3 expects 4000000000. 1.67 = ~10cores/6slots suggests daemon divides VM CPUs across count while verifier expects a fixed 4-CPU envelope (possibly from the 2026-07-13 Colima 24GiB resize note in config.toml line 10 — CPU envelope may not have been resized alongside memory). Determine which side is right (config vs verifier), fix the loser. Not outage-related; observed both before and after full fleet recovery from the 2026-07-19 GitHub Actions cert incident.

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED 2026-07-20 04:22 from live daemon log: 'note: clamping cpus 4 -> 1.6666666666666667 (daemon 10 / 6 runners)'. Config requests cpus=4 per runner; daemon clamps to VM cores (10) / count (6) = 1.67 and emits NanoCpus=1670000000. docs/verify-exit-criteria.sh Gate 3 checks the UNCLAMPED configured value (4e9). Fix options: (a) verifier should compute the same clamp the daemon applies, or (b) resize Colima VM to 24 vCPUs so 4/runner is fundable (matches the 2026-07-13 24GiB memory resize intent). Decide and fix one side.

title: ci-selfhosted.yml provenance validator checks out stale base.sha — 127 on PRs older than .githooks
description: 2026-07-20 evidence: PR 91 head 8541050 failed test-on-ezgha with 'bash: .githooks/commit-msg: No such file or directory' (exit 127). The 'Checkout trusted base validator' step uses ref ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }} which stays frozen at the PR's creation-time base (a27f2e2, predates .githooks). Any PR opened before the hook landed hard-fails regardless of title. Fix: checkout the base BRANCH tip instead (ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.ref }} or 'main') — still trusted (base repo), never stale. Workaround used: PUT pulls/91/update-branch to refresh base.sha. File: .github/workflows/ci-selfhosted.yml 'Checkout trusted base validator' step.
